The new shrine building was completed on {{ill|June 1, 1965|ja|昭和40年6月1日}}.『北日本新聞』1965年5月21日付朝刊12面（富山新川版）『来月1日から遷座祭 魚津神社が完成』より。『富山県神社誌』（1983年11月10日、富山県神社庁発行）346頁。 In {{ill|1965|ja|昭和40年}}, the Atago Shrine, formerly located on the grounds of Uozu Municipal Ōmachi Elementary School, was relocated to the north of the main shrine. In {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43年}}, a large torii gate (8.5 meters tall) was erected through a donation.『富山新聞』1968年9月18日付朝刊10面『魚津神社に大鳥居』より。 In {{ill|1970|ja|昭和45年}}, a sumo ring was added to the grounds.『北日本新聞』1970年8月1日付朝刊（富山新川版）12面『魚津相撲場が完成』より。 == Shrine and Grounds == * Construction began: September 30, 1963 (groundbreaking ceremony held on September 24)『北日本新聞』1963年9月20日付朝刊7面『二千三百万円で新築 7年ぶりに解決の魚津神社』より。 * Completed: May 30, 1965 * Architect: Matsumoto Taisaku * Builder: Asano Nobuharu * Shrine grounds: 1,238 tsubo == Integrated Shrines == [[File:Atago-sha (Uozu)009.jpg|thumb|Atago Shrine]] * Shinmei Shrine (Shinmei-chō) – Established on March 17, 702 (Daibō 2). * Hi-no-Miya Shrine (Shimomuragi-chō) – Rebuilt during the Genki era. * Inari Shrine (Kanayachō) – Built during the Bunsei era. * Ichihime Shrine (Shinkanayachō) – Relics moved to Uozu Shrine in 1979. * Kizuki Shrine (Kōnoura-chō) – Established during the Bunsei era by the Uozu magistrate. == Annual Events == * January 26 – {{ill|Atago Fire Festival|ja|愛宕社の火祭り}} * June 4–6 – Uozu Shrine Festival - Locally known as "Shinmeihan's Festival," this event attracts numerous stalls along the central streets of Uozu. - Historical children’s kabuki floats were once paraded, using traditional techniques and lacquerware craftsmanship. Some relics are preserved in the {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Matsuri float|ja|山車}} * {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Uozu Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT::Uozu Shrine}}[[Category:Shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Uozu Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT::Uozu Shrine}}[[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Uozu Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT::Uozu Shrine}}[[Category:History of Uozu City]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Uozu Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT::Uozu Shrine}}[[Category:Buildings and structures in Uozu City]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Uozu Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT::Uozu Shrine}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Uozu Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Uozu Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT::Uozu Shrine}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Uozu Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT::Uozu Shrine}}[[Category:dd]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Uozu Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[ja:魚津神社]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name= Uozu Jinja |image= [[File:Uozu-Jinja006.jpg|250px]] |location= {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}} {{ill|Uozu|ja|魚津市}} Chūō-dōri 1-3-28『富山県神社誌』（1983年11月10日、富山県神社庁発行）346ページ。 |Coordinates= {{coord|36|49|05.6|N|137|23|51.5|E|scale:25000}} |Main deity= |Shrine rank= |Established= |Main hall= |Other names= |festival= June 4 |Rituals= Uozu Jinja Festival (June 4–6)
Atago Fire Festival (January 26) }} [[File:富山県魚津市 魚津神社 社紋・神紋.png|220px|thumb|The crest and emblem of Uozu Shrine in Uozu City, Toyama Prefecture.]] '''Uozu Shrine''' (魚津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Uozu|ja|魚津市}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}. == Deities == * Main deity: {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}}, originally enshrined in the Shinmei Shrine. * Enshrined deities: - {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|ja|建御名方命}}, originally enshrined in the Shinmei Shrine. - {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|ウカノミタマ|lt=Ukano Mitama no Mikoto}}, originally enshrined in the Inari Shrine. - {{ill|Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|ja|彦火火出見命}}, originally enshrined in the Hi-no-Miya Shrine. - {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命}}, originally enshrined in the Kizuki Shrine. - {{ill|Kamuo Ichihime|ja|神大市比売|lt=Ōichihime no Mikoto}}, originally enshrined in the Ichihime Shrine. - {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速須佐之男命|Susanoo-no-Mikoto=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Takehayasusanoo-no-Mikoto}}, originally a guardian deity of Uozu Castle. - {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売命|lt=Kushinadahime no Mikoto}}, originally a guardian deity of Uozu Castle. - {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, enshrined by Maeda Noritsuna of the Kaga Domain. == History == Originally named Shinmei Shrine, the site became Uozu Shrine in {{ill|1956|ja|昭和31年}} following the integration of five shrines located in areas affected by the {{ill|Uozu Great Fire|ja|魚津大火}}. The new shrine building was completed on {{ill|June 1, 1965|ja|昭和40年6月1日}}.『北日本新聞』1965年5月21日付朝刊12面（富山新川版）『来月1日から遷座祭 魚津神社が完成』より。『富山県神社誌』（1983年11月10日、富山県神社庁発行）346頁。 In {{ill|1965|ja|昭和40年}}, the Atago Shrine, formerly located on the grounds of Uozu Municipal Ōmachi Elementary School, was relocated to the north of the main shrine. In {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43年}}, a large torii gate (8.5 meters tall) was erected through a donation.『富山新聞』1968年9月18日付朝刊10面『魚津神社に大鳥居』より。 In {{ill|1970|ja|昭和45年}}, a sumo ring was added to the grounds.『北日本新聞』1970年8月1日付朝刊（富山新川版）12面『魚津相撲場が完成』より。 == Shrine and Grounds == * Construction began: September 30, 1963 (groundbreaking ceremony held on September 24)『北日本新聞』1963年9月20日付朝刊7面『二千三百万円で新築 7年ぶりに解決の魚津神社』より。 * Completed: May 30, 1965 * Architect: Matsumoto Taisaku * Builder: Asano Nobuharu * Shrine grounds: 1,238 tsubo == Integrated Shrines == [[File:Atago-sha (Uozu)009.jpg|thumb|Atago Shrine]] * Shinmei Shrine (Shinmei-chō) – Established on March 17, 702 (Daibō 2). * Hi-no-Miya Shrine (Shimomuragi-chō) – Rebuilt during the Genki era. * Inari Shrine (Kanayachō) – Built during the Bunsei era. * Ichihime Shrine (Shinkanayachō) – Relics moved to Uozu Shrine in 1979. * Kizuki Shrine (Kōnoura-chō) – Established during the Bunsei era by the Uozu magistrate. == Annual Events == * January 26 – {{ill|Atago Fire Festival|ja|愛宕社の火祭り}} * June 4–6 – Uozu Shrine Festival - Locally known as "Shinmeihan's Festival," this event attracts numerous stalls along the central streets of Uozu. - Historical children’s kabuki floats were once paraded, using traditional techniques and lacquerware craftsmanship. Some relics are preserved in the {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Matsuri float|ja|山車}} * {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Uozu Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT::Uozu Shrine}}[[Category:Shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Uozu Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT::Uozu Shrine}}[[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Uozu Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT::Uozu Shrine}}[[Category:History of Uozu City]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Uozu Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT::Uozu Shrine}}[[Category:Buildings and structures in Uozu City]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Uozu Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT::Uozu Shrine}} [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Uozu Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Uozu Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT::Uozu Shrine}} [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Uozu Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT::Uozu Shrine}}[[Category:dd]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Uozu Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[ja:魚津神社]]

{{Shrine |Name= Uozu Jinja |image= [[File:Uozu-Jinja006.jpg|250px]] |location= {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}} {{ill|Uozu|ja|魚津市}} Chūō-dōri 1-3-28『富山県神社誌』（1983年11月10日、富山県神社庁発行）346ページ。 |Coordinates= {{coord|36|49|05.6|N|137|23|51.5|E|scale:25000}} |Main deity= |Shrine rank= |Established= |Main hall= |Other names= |festival= June 4 |Rituals= Uozu Jinja Festival (June 4–6)

In {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43年}}, a large torii gate (8.5 meters tall) was erected through a donation.『富山新聞』1968年9月18日付朝刊10面『魚津神社に大鳥居』より。 In {{ill|1970|ja|昭和45年}}, a sumo ring was added to the grounds.『北日本新聞』1970年8月1日付朝刊（富山新川版）12面『魚津相撲場が完成』より。 == Shrine and Grounds == * Construction began: September 30, 1963 (groundbreaking ceremony held on September 24)『北日本新聞』1963年9月20日付朝刊7面『二千三百万円で新築 7年ぶりに解決の魚津神社』より。 * Completed: May 30, 1965 * Architect: Matsumoto Taisaku * Builder: Asano Nobuharu * Shrine grounds: 1,238 tsubo == Integrated Shrines == [[File:Atago-sha (Uozu)009.jpg|thumb|Atago Shrine]] * Shinmei Shrine (Shinmei-chō) – Established on March 17, 702 (Daibō 2). * Hi-no-Miya Shrine (Shimomuragi-chō) – Rebuilt during the Genki era. * Inari Shrine (Kanayachō) – Built during the Bunsei era. * Ichihime Shrine (Shinkanayachō) – Relics moved to Uozu Shrine in 1979. * Kizuki Shrine (Kōnoura-chō) – Established during the Bunsei era by the Uozu magistrate. == Annual Events == * January 26 – {{ill|Atago Fire Festival|ja|愛宕社の火祭り}} * June 4–6 – Uozu Shrine Festival - Locally known as "Shinmeihan's Festival," this event attracts numerous stalls along the central streets of Uozu. - Historical children’s kabuki floats were once paraded, using traditional techniques and lacquerware craftsmanship. Some relics are preserved in the {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Matsuri float|ja|山車}} * {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Uozu Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{citation needed|Uozu Shrine}}[[Category:Shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Uozu Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{citation needed|Uozu Shrine}}[[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Uozu Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{citation needed|Uozu Shrine}}[[Category:History of Uozu City]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Uozu Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{citation needed|Uozu Shrine}}[[Category:Buildings and structures in Uozu City]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Uozu Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{citation needed|Uozu Shrine}} [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Uozu Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Uozu Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{citation needed|Uozu Shrine}} [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Uozu Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{citation needed|Uozu Shrine}}[[Category:dd]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Uozu Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[ja:魚津神社]]

{{Shrine |name= Uozu Jinja |image= [[File:Uozu-Jinja006.jpg|250px]] |location= {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}} {{ill|Uozu|ja|魚津市}} Chūō-dōri 1-3-28『富山県神社誌』（1983年11月10日、富山県神社庁発行）346ページ。 |coordinates= {{coord|36|49|05.6|N|137|23|51.5|E|scale:25000}} |Main deity= |Shrine rank= |Established= |Main hall= |Other names= |festival= June 4 |Rituals= Uozu Jinja Festival (June 4–6)
Atago Fire Festival (January 26) }} [[File:富山県魚津市 魚津神社 社紋・神紋.png|220px|thumb|The crest and emblem of Uozu Shrine in Uozu City, Toyama Prefecture.]] '''Uozu Shrine''' (魚津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Uozu|ja|魚津市}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}. == Deities == * Main deity: {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}}, originally enshrined in the Shinmei Shrine. * Enshrined deities: - {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|ja|建御名方命}}, originally enshrined in the Shinmei Shrine. - {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|ウカノミタマ|lt=Ukano Mitama no Mikoto}}, originally enshrined in the Inari Shrine. - {{ill|Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|ja|彦火火出見命}}, originally enshrined in the Hi-no-Miya Shrine. - {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命}}, originally enshrined in the Kizuki Shrine. - {{ill|Kamuo Ichihime|ja|神大市比売|lt=Ōichihime no Mikoto}}, originally enshrined in the Ichihime Shrine. - {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速須佐之男命|Susanoo-no-Mikoto=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Takehayasusanoo-no-Mikoto}}, originally a guardian deity of Uozu Castle. - {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売命|lt=Kushinadahime no Mikoto}}, originally a guardian deity of Uozu Castle. - {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, enshrined by Maeda Noritsuna of the Kaga Domain. == History == Originally named Shinmei Shrine, the site became Uozu Shrine in {{ill|1956|ja|昭和31年}} following the integration of five shrines located in areas affected by the {{ill|Uozu Great Fire|ja|魚津大火}}. The new shrine building was completed on {{ill|June 1, 1965|ja|昭和40年6月1日}}.『北日本新聞』1965年5月21日付朝刊12面（富山新川版）『来月1日から遷座祭 魚津神社が完成』より。『富山県神社誌』（1983年11月10日、富山県神社庁発行）346頁。 In {{ill|1965|ja|昭和40年}}, the Atago Shrine, formerly located on the grounds of Uozu Municipal Ōmachi Elementary School, was relocated to the north of the main shrine. In {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43年}}, a large torii gate (8.5 meters tall) was erected through a donation.『富山新聞』1968年9月18日付朝刊10面『魚津神社に大鳥居』より。 In {{ill|1970|ja|昭和45年}}, a sumo ring was added to the grounds.『北日本新聞』1970年8月1日付朝刊（富山新川版）12面『魚津相撲場が完成』より。 == Shrine and Grounds == * Construction began: September 30, 1963 (groundbreaking ceremony held on September 24)『北日本新聞』1963年9月20日付朝刊7面『二千三百万円で新築 7年ぶりに解決の魚津神社』より。 * Completed: May 30, 1965 * Architect: Matsumoto Taisaku * Builder: Asano Nobuharu * Shrine grounds: 1,238 tsubo == Integrated Shrines == [[File:Atago-sha (Uozu)009.jpg|thumb|Atago Shrine]] * Shinmei Shrine (Shinmei-chō) – Established on March 17, 702 (Daibō 2). * Hi-no-Miya Shrine (Shimomuragi-chō) – Rebuilt during the Genki era. * Inari Shrine (Kanayachō) – Built during the Bunsei era. * Ichihime Shrine (Shinkanayachō) – Relics moved to Uozu Shrine in 1979. * Kizuki Shrine (Kōnoura-chō) – Established during the Bunsei era by the Uozu magistrate. == Annual Events == * January 26 – {{ill|Atago Fire Festival|ja|愛宕社の火祭り}} * June 4–6 – Uozu Shrine Festival - Locally known as "Shinmeihan's Festival," this event attracts numerous stalls along the central streets of Uozu. - Historical children’s kabuki floats were once paraded, using traditional techniques and lacquerware craftsmanship. Some relics are preserved in the {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Matsuri float|ja|山車}} * {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Uozu Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{citation needed|Uozu Shrine}}[[Category:Shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Uozu Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{citation needed|Uozu Shrine}}[[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Uozu Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{citation needed|Uozu Shrine}}[[Category:History of Uozu City]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Uozu Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{citation needed|Uozu Shrine}}[[Category:Buildings and structures in Uozu City]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Uozu Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{citation needed|Uozu Shrine}} [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Uozu Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Uozu Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{citation needed|Uozu Shrine}} [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Uozu Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{citation needed|Uozu Shrine}}[[Category:dd]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Uozu Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[ja:魚津神社]]

{{Shrine |name= Uozu Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Uozu-Jinja006.jpg|250px]] |location= {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}} {{ill|Uozu|ja|魚津市}} Chūō-dōri 1-3-28『富山県神社誌』（1983年11月10日、富山県神社庁発行）346ページ。 |coordinates= {{coord|36|49|05.6|N|137|23|51.5|E|scale:25000}} |Main deity= |Shrine rank= |Established= |Main hall= |Other names= |festival= June 4 |Rituals= Uozu Jinja Festival (June 4–6)
Atago Fire Festival (January 26) }} [[File:富山県魚津市 魚津神社 社紋・神紋.png|220px|thumb|The crest and emblem of Uozu Shrine in Uozu City, Toyama Prefecture.]] '''Uozu Shrine''' (魚津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Uozu|ja|魚津市}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}. == Deities == * Main deity: {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}}, originally enshrined in the Shinmei Shrine. * Enshrined deities: - {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|ja|建御名方命}}, originally enshrined in the Shinmei Shrine. - {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|ウカノミタマ|lt=Ukano Mitama no Mikoto}}, originally enshrined in the Inari Shrine. - {{ill|Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|ja|彦火火出見命}}, originally enshrined in the Hi-no-Miya Shrine. - {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命}}, originally enshrined in the Kizuki Shrine. - {{ill|Kamuo Ichihime|ja|神大市比売|lt=Ōichihime no Mikoto}}, originally enshrined in the Ichihime Shrine. - {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速須佐之男命|Susanoo-no-Mikoto=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Takehayasusanoo-no-Mikoto}}, originally a guardian deity of Uozu Castle. - {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売命|lt=Kushinadahime no Mikoto}}, originally a guardian deity of Uozu Castle. - {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, enshrined by Maeda Noritsuna of the Kaga Domain. == History == Originally named Shinmei Shrine, the site became Uozu Shrine in {{ill|1956|ja|昭和31年}} following the integration of five shrines located in areas affected by the {{ill|Uozu Great Fire|ja|魚津大火}}. The new shrine building was completed on {{ill|June 1, 1965|ja|昭和40年6月1日}}.『北日本新聞』1965年5月21日付朝刊12面（富山新川版）『来月1日から遷座祭 魚津神社が完成』より。『富山県神社誌』（1983年11月10日、富山県神社庁発行）346頁。 In {{ill|1965|ja|昭和40年}}, the Atago Shrine, formerly located on the grounds of Uozu Municipal Ōmachi Elementary School, was relocated to the north of the main shrine. In {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43年}}, a large torii gate (8.5 meters tall) was erected through a donation.『富山新聞』1968年9月18日付朝刊10面『魚津神社に大鳥居』より。 In {{ill|1970|ja|昭和45年}}, a sumo ring was added to the grounds.『北日本新聞』1970年8月1日付朝刊（富山新川版）12面『魚津相撲場が完成』より。 == Shrine and Grounds == * Construction began: September 30, 1963 (groundbreaking ceremony held on September 24)『北日本新聞』1963年9月20日付朝刊7面『二千三百万円で新築 7年ぶりに解決の魚津神社』より。 * Completed: May 30, 1965 * Architect: Matsumoto Taisaku * Builder: Asano Nobuharu * Shrine grounds: 1,238 tsubo == Integrated Shrines == [[File:Atago-sha (Uozu)009.jpg|thumb|Atago Shrine]] * Shinmei Shrine (Shinmei-chō) – Established on March 17, 702 (Daibō 2). * Hi-no-Miya Shrine (Shimomuragi-chō) – Rebuilt during the Genki era. * Inari Shrine (Kanayachō) – Built during the Bunsei era. * Ichihime Shrine (Shinkanayachō) – Relics moved to Uozu Shrine in 1979. * Kizuki Shrine (Kōnoura-chō) – Established during the Bunsei era by the Uozu magistrate. == Annual Events == * January 26 – {{ill|Atago Fire Festival|ja|愛宕社の火祭り}} * June 4–6 – Uozu Shrine Festival - Locally known as "Shinmeihan's Festival," this event attracts numerous stalls along the central streets of Uozu. - Historical children’s kabuki floats were once paraded, using traditional techniques and lacquerware craftsmanship. Some relics are preserved in the {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Matsuri float|ja|山車}} * {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Uozu Shrine}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages In {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43年}}, a large torii gate (8.5 meters tall) was erected through a donation.『富山新聞』1968年9月18日付朝刊10面『魚津神社に大鳥居』より。 In {{ill|1970|ja|昭和45年}}, a sumo ring was added to the grounds.『北日本新聞』1970年8月1日付朝刊（富山新川版）12面『魚津相撲場が完成』より。 == Shrine and Grounds == * Construction began: September 30, 1963 (groundbreaking ceremony held on September 24)『北日本新聞』1963年9月20日付朝刊7面『二千三百万円で新築 7年ぶりに解決の魚津神社』より。 * Completed: May 30, 1965 * Architect: Matsumoto Taisaku * Builder: Asano Nobuharu * Shrine grounds: 1,238 tsubo == Integrated Shrines == [[File:Atago-sha (Uozu)009.jpg|thumb|Atago Shrine]] * Shinmei Shrine (Shinmei-chō) – Established on March 17, 702 (Daibō 2). * Hi-no-Miya Shrine (Shimomuragi-chō) – Rebuilt during the Genki era. * Inari Shrine (Kanayachō) – Built during the Bunsei era. * Ichihime Shrine (Shinkanayachō) – Relics moved to Uozu Shrine in 1979. * Kizuki Shrine (Kōnoura-chō) – Established during the Bunsei era by the Uozu magistrate. == Annual Events == * January 26 – {{ill|Atago Fire Festival|ja|愛宕社の火祭り}} * June 4–6 – Uozu Shrine Festival - Locally known as "Shinmeihan's Festival," this event attracts numerous stalls along the central streets of Uozu. - Historical children’s kabuki floats were once paraded, using traditional techniques and lacquerware craftsmanship. Some relics are preserved in the {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Matsuri float|ja|山車}} * {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Uozu Shrine}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] In {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43年}}, a large torii gate (8.5 meters tall) was erected through a donation.『富山新聞』1968年9月18日付朝刊10面『魚津神社に大鳥居』より。 In {{ill|1970|ja|昭和45年}}, a sumo ring was added to the grounds.『北日本新聞』1970年8月1日付朝刊（富山新川版）12面『魚津相撲場が完成』より。 == Shrine and Grounds == * Construction began: September 30, 1963 (groundbreaking ceremony held on September 24)『北日本新聞』1963年9月20日付朝刊7面『二千三百万円で新築 7年ぶりに解決の魚津神社』より。 * Completed: May 30, 1965 * Architect: Matsumoto Taisaku * Builder: Asano Nobuharu * Shrine grounds: 1,238 tsubo == Integrated Shrines == [[File:Atago-sha (Uozu)009.jpg|thumb|Atago Shrine]] * Shinmei Shrine (Shinmei-chō) – Established on March 17, 702 (Daibō 2). * Hi-no-Miya Shrine (Shimomuragi-chō) – Rebuilt during the Genki era. * Inari Shrine (Kanayachō) – Built during the Bunsei era. * Ichihime Shrine (Shinkanayachō) – Relics moved to Uozu Shrine in 1979. * Kizuki Shrine (Kōnoura-chō) – Established during the Bunsei era by the Uozu magistrate. == Annual Events == * January 26 – {{ill|Atago Fire Festival|ja|愛宕社の火祭り}} * June 4–6 – Uozu Shrine Festival - Locally known as "Shinmeihan's Festival," this event attracts numerous stalls along the central streets of Uozu. - Historical children’s kabuki floats were once paraded, using traditional techniques and lacquerware craftsmanship. Some relics are preserved in the {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Matsuri float|ja|山車}} * {{ill|Uozu History and Atago Fire Festival (January 26) }} [[File:富山県魚津市 魚津神社 社紋・神紋.png|220px|thumb|The crest and emblem of Uozu Shrine in Uozu City, Toyama Prefecture.]] '''Uozu Shrine''' (魚津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Uozu|ja|魚津市}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}. == Deities == * Main deity: {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}}, originally enshrined in the Shinmei Shrine. * Enshrined deities: - {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|ja|建御名方命}}, originally enshrined in the Shinmei Shrine. - {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|ウカノミタマ|lt=Ukano Mitama no Mikoto}}, originally enshrined in the Inari Shrine. - {{ill|Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|ja|彦火火出見命}}, originally enshrined in the Hi-no-Miya Shrine. - {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命}}, originally enshrined in the Kizuki Shrine. - {{ill|Kamuo Ichihime|ja|神大市比売|lt=Ōichihime no Mikoto}}, originally enshrined in the Ichihime Shrine. - {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速須佐之男命|Susanoo-no-Mikoto=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Takehayasusanoo-no-Mikoto}}, originally a guardian deity of Uozu Castle. - {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売命|lt=Kushinadahime no Mikoto}}, originally a guardian deity of Uozu Castle. - {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, enshrined by Maeda Noritsuna of the Kaga Domain. == History == Originally named Shinmei Shrine, the site became Uozu Shrine in {{ill|1956|ja|昭和31年}} following the integration of five shrines located in areas affected by the {{ill|Uozu Great Fire|ja|魚津大火}}. The new shrine building was completed on {{ill|June 1, 1965|ja|昭和40年6月1日}}.『北日本新聞』1965年5月21日付朝刊12面（富山新川版）『来月1日から遷座祭 魚津神社が完成』より。『富山県神社誌』（1983年11月10日、富山県神社庁発行）346頁。 In {{ill|1965|ja|昭和40年}}, the Atago Shrine, formerly located on the grounds of Uozu Municipal Ōmachi Elementary School, was relocated to the north of the main shrine. In {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43年}}, a large torii gate (8.5 meters tall) was erected through a donation.『富山新聞』1968年9月18日付朝刊10面『魚津神社に大鳥居』より。 In {{ill|1970|ja|昭和45年}}, a sumo ring was added to the grounds.『北日本新聞』1970年8月1日付朝刊（富山新川版）12面『魚津相撲場が完成』より。 == Shrine and Grounds == * Construction began: September 30, 1963 (groundbreaking ceremony held on September 24)『北日本新聞』1963年9月20日付朝刊7面『二千三百万円で新築 7年ぶりに解決の魚津神社』より。 * Completed: May 30, 1965 * Architect: Matsumoto Taisaku * Builder: Asano Nobuharu * Shrine grounds: 1,238 tsubo == Integrated Shrines == [[File:Atago-sha (Uozu)009.jpg|thumb|Atago Shrine]] * Shinmei Shrine (Shinmei-chō) – Established on March 17, 702 (Daibō 2). * Hi-no-Miya Shrine (Shimomuragi-chō) – Rebuilt during the Genki era. * Inari Shrine (Kanayachō) – Built during the Bunsei era. * Ichihime Shrine (Shinkanayachō) – Relics moved to Uozu Shrine in 1979. * Kizuki Shrine (Kōnoura-chō) – Established during the Bunsei era by the Uozu magistrate. == Annual Events == * January 26 – {{ill|Atago Fire Festival|ja|愛宕社の火祭り}} * June 4–6 – Uozu Shrine Festival - Locally known as "Shinmeihan's Festival," this event attracts numerous stalls along the central streets of Uozu. - Historical children’s kabuki floats were once paraded, using traditional techniques and lacquerware craftsmanship. Some relics are preserved in the {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Matsuri float|ja|山車}} * {{ill|Uozu History and Atago Fire Festival (January 26) }} [[File:富山県魚津市 魚津神社 社紋・神紋.png|220px|thumb|The crest and emblem of Uozu Shrine in Uozu City, Toyama Prefecture.]] '''Uozu Shrine''' (魚津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Uozu|ja|魚津市}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}. == Deities == * Main deity: {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}}, originally enshrined in the Shinmei Shrine. * Enshrined deities: - {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|ja|建御名方命}}, originally enshrined in the Shinmei Shrine. - {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|ウカノミタマ|lt=Ukano Mitama no Mikoto}}, originally enshrined in the Inari Shrine. - {{ill|Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|ja|彦火火出見命}}, originally enshrined in the Hi-no-Miya Shrine. - {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命}}, originally enshrined in the Kizuki Shrine. - {{ill|Kamuo Ichihime|ja|神大市比売|lt=Ōichihime no Mikoto}}, originally enshrined in the Ichihime Shrine. - {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速須佐之男命|Susanoo-no-Mikoto=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Takehayasusanoo-no-Mikoto}}, originally a guardian deity of Uozu Castle. - {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売命|lt=Kushinadahime no Mikoto}}, originally a guardian deity of Uozu Castle. - {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, enshrined by Maeda Noritsuna of the Kaga Domain. == History == Originally named Shinmei Shrine, the site became Uozu Shrine in {{ill|1956|ja|昭和31年}} following the integration of five shrines located in areas affected by the {{ill|Uozu Great Fire|ja|魚津大火}}. The new shrine building was completed on {{ill|June 1, 1965|ja|昭和40年6月1日}}.『北日本新聞』1965年5月21日付朝刊12面（富山新川版）『来月1日から遷座祭 魚津神社が完成』より。『富山県神社誌』（1983年11月10日、富山県神社庁発行）346頁。 In {{ill|1965|ja|昭和40年}}, the Atago Shrine, formerly located on the grounds of Uozu Municipal Ōmachi Elementary School, was relocated to the north of the main shrine. In {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43年}}, a large torii gate (8.5 meters tall) was erected through a donation.『富山新聞』1968年9月18日付朝刊10面『魚津神社に大鳥居』より。 In {{ill|1970|ja|昭和45年}}, a sumo ring was added to the grounds.『北日本新聞』1970年8月1日付朝刊（富山新川版）12面『魚津相撲場が完成』より。 == Shrine and Grounds == * Construction began: September 30, 1963 (groundbreaking ceremony held on September 24)『北日本新聞』1963年9月20日付朝刊7面『二千三百万円で新築 7年ぶりに解決の魚津神社』より。 * Completed: May 30, 1965 * Architect: Matsumoto Taisaku * Builder: Asano Nobuharu * Shrine grounds: 1,238 tsubo == Integrated Shrines == [[File:Atago-sha (Uozu)009.jpg|thumb|Atago Shrine]] * Shinmei Shrine (Shinmei-chō) – Established on March 17, 702 (Daibō 2). * Hi-no-Miya Shrine (Shimomuragi-chō) – Rebuilt during the Genki era. * Inari Shrine (Kanayachō) – Built during the Bunsei era. * Ichihime Shrine (Shinkanayachō) – Relics moved to Uozu Shrine in 1979. * Kizuki Shrine (Kōnoura-chō) – Established during the Bunsei era by the Uozu magistrate. == Annual Events == * January 26 – {{ill|Atago Fire Festival|ja|愛宕社の火祭り}} * June 4–6 – Uozu Shrine Festival - Locally known as "Shinmeihan's Festival," this event attracts numerous stalls along the central streets of Uozu. - Historical children’s kabuki floats were once paraded, using traditional techniques and lacquerware craftsmanship. Some relics are preserved in the {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Matsuri float|ja|山車}} * {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Uozu Shrine}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] Atago Fire Festival (January 26) }} [[File:富山県魚津市 魚津神社 社紋・神紋.png|220px|thumb|The crest and emblem of Uozu Shrine in Uozu City, Toyama Prefecture.]] '''Uozu Shrine''' (魚津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Uozu|ja|魚津市}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}. == Deities == * Main deity: {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}}, originally enshrined in the Shinmei Shrine. * Enshrined deities: - {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|ja|建御名方命}}, originally enshrined in the Shinmei Shrine. - {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|ウカノミタマ|lt=Ukano Mitama no Mikoto}}, originally enshrined in the Inari Shrine. - {{ill|Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|ja|彦火火出見命}}, originally enshrined in the Hi-no-Miya Shrine. - {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命}}, originally enshrined in the Kizuki Shrine. - {{ill|Kamuo Ichihime|ja|神大市比売|lt=Ōichihime no Mikoto}}, originally enshrined in the Ichihime Shrine. - {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速須佐之男命|Susanoo-no-Mikoto=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Takehayasusanoo-no-Mikoto}}, originally a guardian deity of Uozu Castle. - {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売命|lt=Kushinadahime no Mikoto}}, originally a guardian deity of Uozu Castle. - {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, enshrined by Maeda Noritsuna of the Kaga Domain. == History == Originally named Shinmei Shrine, the site became Uozu Shrine in {{ill|1956|ja|昭和31年}} following the integration of five shrines located in areas affected by the {{ill|Uozu Great Fire|ja|魚津大火}}. The new shrine building was completed on {{ill|June 1, 1965|ja|昭和40年6月1日}}.『北日本新聞』1965年5月21日付朝刊12面（富山新川版）『来月1日から遷座祭 魚津神社が完成』より。『富山県神社誌』（1983年11月10日、富山県神社庁発行）346頁。 In {{ill|1965|ja|昭和40年}}, the Atago Shrine, formerly located on the grounds of Uozu Municipal Ōmachi Elementary School, was relocated to the north of the main shrine. In {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43年}}, a large torii gate (8.5 meters tall) was erected through a donation.『富山新聞』1968年9月18日付朝刊10面『魚津神社に大鳥居』より。 In {{ill|1970|ja|昭和45年}}, a sumo ring was added to the grounds.『北日本新聞』1970年8月1日付朝刊（富山新川版）12面『魚津相撲場が完成』より。 == Shrine and Grounds == * Construction began: September 30, 1963 (groundbreaking ceremony held on September 24)『北日本新聞』1963年9月20日付朝刊7面『二千三百万円で新築 7年ぶりに解決の魚津神社』より。 * Completed: May 30, 1965 * Architect: Matsumoto Taisaku * Builder: Asano Nobuharu * Shrine grounds: 1,238 tsubo == Integrated Shrines == [[File:Atago-sha (Uozu)009.jpg|thumb|Atago Shrine]] * Shinmei Shrine (Shinmei-chō) – Established on March 17, 702 (Daibō 2). * Hi-no-Miya Shrine (Shimomuragi-chō) – Rebuilt during the Genki era. * Inari Shrine (Kanayachō) – Built during the Bunsei era. * Ichihime Shrine (Shinkanayachō) – Relics moved to Uozu Shrine in 1979. * Kizuki Shrine (Kōnoura-chō) – Established during the Bunsei era by the Uozu magistrate. == Annual Events == * January 26 – {{ill|Atago Fire Festival|ja|愛宕社の火祭り}} * June 4–6 – Uozu Shrine Festival - Locally known as "Shinmeihan's Festival," this event attracts numerous stalls along the central streets of Uozu. - Historical children’s kabuki floats were once paraded, using traditional techniques and lacquerware craftsmanship. Some relics are preserved in the {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Matsuri float|ja|山車}} * {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Uozu Shrine}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Uozu Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Uozu-Jinja006.jpg|250px]] |location= {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}} {{ill|Uozu|ja|魚津市}} Chūō-dōri 1-3-28『富山県神社誌』（1983年11月10日、富山県神社庁発行）346ページ。 |coordinates= {{coord|36|49|05.6|N|137|23|51.5|E|scale:25000}} |Main deity= |Shrine rank= |Established= |Main hall= |Other names= |festival= June 4 |Rituals= Uozu Jinja Festival (June 4–6)
Atago Fire Festival (January 26) }} [[File:富山県魚津市 魚津神社 社紋・神紋.png|220px|thumb|The crest and emblem of Uozu Shrine in Uozu City, Toyama Prefecture.]] '''Uozu Shrine''' (魚津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Uozu|ja|魚津市}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}. == Deities == * Main deity: {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}}, originally enshrined in the Shinmei Shrine. * Enshrined deities: - {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|ja|建御名方命}}, originally enshrined in the Shinmei Shrine. - {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|ウカノミタマ|lt=Ukano Mitama no Mikoto}}, originally enshrined in the Inari Shrine. - {{ill|Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|ja|彦火火出見命}}, originally enshrined in the Hi-no-Miya Shrine. - {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命}}, originally enshrined in the Kizuki Shrine. - {{ill|Kamuo Ichihime|ja|神大市比売|lt=Ōichihime no Mikoto}}, originally enshrined in the Ichihime Shrine. - {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速須佐之男命|Susanoo-no-Mikoto=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Takehayasusanoo-no-Mikoto}}, originally a guardian deity of Uozu Castle. - {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売命|lt=Kushinadahime no Mikoto}}, originally a guardian deity of Uozu Castle. - {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, enshrined by Maeda Noritsuna of the Kaga Domain. == History == Originally named Shinmei Shrine, the site became Uozu Shrine in {{ill|1956|ja|昭和31年}} following the integration of five shrines located in areas affected by the {{ill|Uozu Great Fire|ja|魚津大火}}. The new shrine building was completed on {{ill|June 1, 1965|ja|昭和40年6月1日}}.『北日本新聞』1965年5月21日付朝刊12面（富山新川版）『来月1日から遷座祭 魚津神社が完成』より。『富山県神社誌』（1983年11月10日、富山県神社庁発行）346頁。 In {{ill|1965|ja|昭和40年}}, the Atago Shrine, formerly located on the grounds of Uozu Municipal Ōmachi Elementary School, was relocated to the north of the main shrine. In {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43年}}, a large torii gate (8.5 meters tall) was erected through a donation.『富山新聞』1968年9月18日付朝刊10面『魚津神社に大鳥居』より。 In {{ill|1970|ja|昭和45年}}, a sumo ring was added to the grounds.『北日本新聞』1970年8月1日付朝刊（富山新川版）12面『魚津相撲場が完成』より。 == Shrine and Grounds == * Construction began: September 30, 1963 (groundbreaking ceremony held on September 24)『北日本新聞』1963年9月20日付朝刊7面『二千三百万円で新築 7年ぶりに解決の魚津神社』より。 * Completed: May 30, 1965 * Architect: Matsumoto Taisaku * Builder: Asano Nobuharu * Shrine grounds: 1,238 tsubo == Integrated Shrines == [[File:Atago-sha (Uozu)009.jpg|thumb|Atago Shrine]] * Shinmei Shrine (Shinmei-chō) – Established on March 17, 702 (Daibō 2). * Hi-no-Miya Shrine (Shimomuragi-chō) – Rebuilt during the Genki era. * Inari Shrine (Kanayachō) – Built during the Bunsei era. * Ichihime Shrine (Shinkanayachō) – Relics moved to Uozu Shrine in 1979. * Kizuki Shrine (Kōnoura-chō) – Established during the Bunsei era by the Uozu magistrate. == Annual Events == * January 26 – {{ill|Atago Fire Festival|ja|愛宕社の火祭り}} * June 4–6 – Uozu Shrine Festival - Locally known as "Shinmeihan's Festival," this event attracts numerous stalls along the central streets of Uozu. - Historical children’s kabuki floats were once paraded, using traditional techniques and lacquerware craftsmanship. Some relics are preserved in the {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Matsuri float|ja|山車}} * {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Uozu Shrine}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Uozu Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Uozu-Jinja006.jpg|250px]] |location= {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}} {{ill|Uozu|ja|魚津市}} Chūō-dōri 1-3-28『富山県神社誌』（1983年11月10日、富山県神社庁発行）346ページ。 |coordinates= {{coord|36|49|05.6|N|137|23|51.5|E|scale:25000}} |Main deity= |Shrine rank= |Established= |Main hall= |Other names= |festival= June 4 |Rituals= Uozu Jinja Festival (June 4–6)
Atago Fire Festival (January 26) }} [[File:富山県魚津市 魚津神社 社紋・神紋.png|220px|thumb|The crest and emblem of Uozu Shrine in Uozu City, Toyama Prefecture.]] '''Uozu Shrine''' (魚津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Uozu|ja|魚津市}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}. == Deities == * Main deity: {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}}, originally enshrined in the Shinmei Shrine. * Enshrined deities: - {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|ja|建御名方命}}, originally enshrined in the Shinmei Shrine. - {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|ウカノミタマ|lt=Ukano Mitama no Mikoto}}, originally enshrined in the Inari Shrine. - {{ill|Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|ja|彦火火出見命}}, originally enshrined in the Hi-no-Miya Shrine. - {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命}}, originally enshrined in the Kizuki Shrine. - {{ill|Kamuo Ichihime|ja|神大市比売|lt=Ōichihime no Mikoto}}, originally enshrined in the Ichihime Shrine. - {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速須佐之男命|Susanoo-no-Mikoto=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Takehayasusanoo-no-Mikoto}}, originally a guardian deity of Uozu Castle. - {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売命|lt=Kushinadahime no Mikoto}}, originally a guardian deity of Uozu Castle. - {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, enshrined by Maeda Noritsuna of the Kaga Domain. == History == Originally named Shinmei Shrine, the site became Uozu Shrine in {{ill|1956|ja|昭和31年}} following the integration of five shrines located in areas affected by the {{ill|Uozu Great Fire|ja|魚津大火}}. The new shrine building was completed on {{ill|June 1, 1965|ja|昭和40年6月1日}}.『北日本新聞』1965年5月21日付朝刊12面（富山新川版）『来月1日から遷座祭 魚津神社が完成』より。『富山県神社誌』（1983年11月10日、富山県神社庁発行）346頁。 In {{ill|1965|ja|昭和40年}}, the Atago Shrine, formerly located on the grounds of Uozu Municipal Ōmachi Elementary School, was relocated to the north of the main shrine. In {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43年}}, a large torii gate (8.5 meters tall) was erected through a donation.『富山新聞』1968年9月18日付朝刊10面『魚津神社に大鳥居』より。 In {{ill|1970|ja|昭和45年}}, a sumo ring was added to the grounds.『北日本新聞』1970年8月1日付朝刊（富山新川版）12面『魚津相撲場が完成』より。 == Shrine and Grounds == * Construction began: September 30, 1963 (groundbreaking ceremony held on September 24)『北日本新聞』1963年9月20日付朝刊7面『二千三百万円で新築 7年ぶりに解決の魚津神社』より。 * Completed: May 30, 1965 * Architect: Matsumoto Taisaku * Builder: Asano Nobuharu * Shrine grounds: 1,238 tsubo == Integrated Shrines == [[File:Atago-sha (Uozu)009.jpg|thumb|Atago Shrine]] * Shinmei Shrine (Shinmei-chō) – Established on March 17, 702 (Daibō 2). * Hi-no-Miya Shrine (Shimomuragi-chō) – Rebuilt during the Genki era. * Inari Shrine (Kanayachō) – Built during the Bunsei era. * Ichihime Shrine (Shinkanayachō) – Relics moved to Uozu Shrine in 1979. * Kizuki Shrine (Kōnoura-chō) – Established during the Bunsei era by the Uozu magistrate. == Annual Events == * January 26 – {{ill|Atago Fire Festival|ja|愛宕社の火祭り}} * June 4–6 – Uozu Shrine Festival - Locally known as "Shinmeihan's Festival," this event attracts numerous stalls along the central streets of Uozu. - Historical children’s kabuki floats were once paraded, using traditional techniques and lacquerware craftsmanship. Some relics are preserved in the {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Matsuri float|ja|山車}} * {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Uozu Shrine}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Uozu Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Uozu-Jinja006.jpg|250px]] |location= {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}} {{ill|Uozu|ja|魚津市}} Chūō-dōri 1-3-28『富山県神社誌』（1983年11月10日、富山県神社庁発行）346ページ。 |coordinates= {{coord|36|49|05.6|N|137|23|51.5|E|scale:25000}} |Main deity= |Shrine rank= |Established= |Main hall= |Other names= |festival= June 4 |Rituals= Uozu Jinja Festival (June 4–6)
Atago Fire Festival (January 26) }} '''Uozu Shrine''' (魚津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Uozu|ja|魚津市}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}. == Deities == * Main deity: {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}}, originally enshrined in the Shinmei Shrine. * Enshrined deities: - {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|ja|建御名方命}}, originally enshrined in the Shinmei Shrine. - {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|ウカノミタマ|lt=Ukano Mitama no Mikoto}}, originally enshrined in the Inari Shrine. - {{ill|Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|ja|彦火火出見命}}, originally enshrined in the Hi-no-Miya Shrine. - {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命}}, originally enshrined in the Kizuki Shrine. - {{ill|Kamuo Ichihime|ja|神大市比売|lt=Ōichihime no Mikoto}}, originally enshrined in the Ichihime Shrine. - {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速須佐之男命|Susanoo-no-Mikoto=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Takehayasusanoo-no-Mikoto}}, originally a guardian deity of Uozu Castle. - {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売命|lt=Kushinadahime no Mikoto}}, originally a guardian deity of Uozu Castle. - {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, enshrined by Maeda Noritsuna of the Kaga Domain. == History == Originally named Shinmei Shrine, the site became Uozu Shrine in {{ill|1956|ja|昭和31年}} following the integration of five shrines located in areas affected by the {{ill|Uozu Great Fire|ja|魚津大火}}. The new shrine building was completed on {{ill|June 1, 1965|ja|昭和40年6月1日}}.『北日本新聞』1965年5月21日付朝刊12面（富山新川版）『来月1日から遷座祭 魚津神社が完成』より。『富山県神社誌』（1983年11月10日、富山県神社庁発行）346頁。 In {{ill|1965|ja|昭和40年}}, the Atago Shrine, formerly located on the grounds of Uozu Municipal Ōmachi Elementary School, was relocated to the north of the main shrine. In {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43年}}, a large torii gate (8.5 meters tall) was erected through a donation.『富山新聞』1968年9月18日付朝刊10面『魚津神社に大鳥居』より。 In {{ill|1970|ja|昭和45年}}, a sumo ring was added to the grounds.『北日本新聞』1970年8月1日付朝刊（富山新川版）12面『魚津相撲場が完成』より。 == Shrine and Grounds == * Construction began: September 30, 1963 (groundbreaking ceremony held on September 24)『北日本新聞』1963年9月20日付朝刊7面『二千三百万円で新築 7年ぶりに解決の魚津神社』より。 * Completed: May 30, 1965 * Architect: Matsumoto Taisaku * Builder: Asano Nobuharu * Shrine grounds: 1,238 tsubo == Integrated Shrines == [[File:Atago-sha (Uozu)009.jpg|thumb|Atago Shrine]] * Shinmei Shrine (Shinmei-chō) – Established on March 17, 702 (Daibō 2). * Hi-no-Miya Shrine (Shimomuragi-chō) – Rebuilt during the Genki era. * Inari Shrine (Kanayachō) – Built during the Bunsei era. * Ichihime Shrine (Shinkanayachō) – Relics moved to Uozu Shrine in 1979. * Kizuki Shrine (Kōnoura-chō) – Established during the Bunsei era by the Uozu magistrate. == Annual Events == * January 26 – {{ill|Atago Fire Festival|ja|愛宕社の火祭り}} * June 4–6 – Uozu Shrine Festival - Locally known as "Shinmeihan's Festival," this event attracts numerous stalls along the central streets of Uozu. - Historical children’s kabuki floats were once paraded, using traditional techniques and lacquerware craftsmanship. Some relics are preserved in the {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Matsuri float|ja|山車}} * {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Uozu Shrine}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:qq---qq]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Uozu Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Uozu-Jinja006.jpg|250px]] |location= {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}} {{ill|Uozu|ja|魚津市}} Chūō-dōri 1-3-28『富山県神社誌』（1983年11月10日、富山県神社庁発行）346ページ。 |coordinates= {{coord|36|49|05.6|N|137|23|51.5|E|scale:25000}} |Main deity= |Shrine rank= |Established= |Main hall= |Other names= |festival= June 4 |Rituals= Uozu Jinja Festival (June 4–6)
Atago Fire Festival (January 26) }} '''Uozu Shrine''' (魚津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Uozu|ja|魚津市}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}. == Deities == * Main deity: {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}}, originally enshrined in the Shinmei Shrine. * Enshrined deities: - {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|ja|建御名方命}}, originally enshrined in the Shinmei Shrine. - {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|ウカノミタマ|lt=Ukano Mitama no Mikoto}}, originally enshrined in the Inari Shrine. - {{ill|Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|ja|彦火火出見命}}, originally enshrined in the Hi-no-Miya Shrine. - {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命}}, originally enshrined in the Kizuki Shrine. - {{ill|Kamuo Ichihime|ja|神大市比売|lt=Ōichihime no Mikoto}}, originally enshrined in the Ichihime Shrine. - {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速須佐之男命|Susanoo-no-Mikoto=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Takehayasusanoo-no-Mikoto}}, originally a guardian deity of Uozu Castle. - {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売命|lt=Kushinadahime no Mikoto}}, originally a guardian deity of Uozu Castle. - {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, enshrined by Maeda Noritsuna of the Kaga Domain. == History == Originally named Shinmei Shrine, the site became Uozu Shrine in {{ill|1956|ja|昭和31年}} following the integration of five shrines located in areas affected by the {{ill|Uozu Great Fire|ja|魚津大火}}. The new shrine building was completed on {{ill|June 1, 1965|ja|昭和40年6月1日}}.『北日本新聞』1965年5月21日付朝刊12面（富山新川版）『来月1日から遷座祭 魚津神社が完成』より。『富山県神社誌』（1983年11月10日、富山県神社庁発行）346頁。 In {{ill|1965|ja|昭和40年}}, the Atago Shrine, formerly located on the grounds of Uozu Municipal Ōmachi Elementary School, was relocated to the north of the main shrine. In {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43年}}, a large torii gate (8.5 meters tall) was erected through a donation.『富山新聞』1968年9月18日付朝刊10面『魚津神社に大鳥居』より。 In {{ill|1970|ja|昭和45年}}, a sumo ring was added to the grounds.『北日本新聞』1970年8月1日付朝刊（富山新川版）12面『魚津相撲場が完成』より。 == Shrine and Grounds == * Construction began: September 30, 1963 (groundbreaking ceremony held on September 24)『北日本新聞』1963年9月20日付朝刊7面『二千三百万円で新築 7年ぶりに解決の魚津神社』より。 * Completed: May 30, 1965 * Architect: Matsumoto Taisaku * Builder: Asano Nobuharu * Shrine grounds: 1,238 tsubo == Integrated Shrines == [[File:Atago-sha (Uozu)009.jpg|thumb|Atago Shrine]] * Shinmei Shrine (Shinmei-chō) – Established on March 17, 702 (Daibō 2). * Hi-no-Miya Shrine (Shimomuragi-chō) – Rebuilt during the Genki era. * Inari Shrine (Kanayachō) – Built during the Bunsei era. * Ichihime Shrine (Shinkanayachō) – Relics moved to Uozu Shrine in 1979. * Kizuki Shrine (Kōnoura-chō) – Established during the Bunsei era by the Uozu magistrate. == Annual Events == * January 26 – {{ill|Atago Fire Festival|ja|愛宕社の火祭り}} * June 4–6 – Uozu Shrine Festival - Locally known as "Shinmeihan's Festival," this event attracts numerous stalls along the central streets of Uozu. - Historical children’s kabuki floats were once paraded, using traditional techniques and lacquerware craftsmanship. Some relics are preserved in the {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Matsuri float|ja|山車}} * {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Uozu Shrine}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category:tier 4]] The new shrine building was completed on {{ill|June 1, 1965|ja|昭和40年6月1日}}.『北日本新聞』1965年5月21日付朝刊12面（富山新川版）『来月1日から遷座祭 魚津神社が完成』より。『富山県神社誌』（1983年11月10日、富山県神社庁発行）346頁。 In {{ill|1965|ja|昭和40年}}, the Atago Shrine, formerly located on the grounds of Uozu Municipal Ōmachi Elementary School, was relocated to the north of the main shrine. In {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43年}}, a large torii gate (8.5 meters tall) was erected through a donation.『富山新聞』1968年9月18日付朝刊10面『魚津神社に大鳥居』より。 In {{ill|1970|ja|昭和45年}}, a sumo ring was added to the grounds.『北日本新聞』1970年8月1日付朝刊（富山新川版）12面『魚津相撲場が完成』より。 == Shrine and Grounds == * Construction began: September 30, 1963 (groundbreaking ceremony held on September 24)『北日本新聞』1963年9月20日付朝刊7面『二千三百万円で新築 7年ぶりに解決の魚津神社』より。 * Completed: May 30, 1965 * Architect: Matsumoto Taisaku * Builder: Asano Nobuharu * Shrine grounds: 1,238 tsubo == Integrated Shrines == [[File:Atago-sha (Uozu)009.jpg|thumb|Atago Shrine]] * Shinmei Shrine (Shinmei-chō) – Established on March 17, 702 (Daibō 2). * Hi-no-Miya Shrine (Shimomuragi-chō) – Rebuilt during the Genki era. * Inari Shrine (Kanayachō) – Built during the Bunsei era. * Ichihime Shrine (Shinkanayachō) – Relics moved to Uozu Shrine in 1979. * Kizuki Shrine (Kōnoura-chō) – Established during the Bunsei era by the Uozu magistrate. == Annual Events == * January 26 – {{ill|Atago Fire Festival|ja|愛宕社の火祭り}} * June 4–6 – Uozu Shrine Festival - Locally known as "Shinmeihan's Festival," this event attracts numerous stalls along the central streets of Uozu. - Historical children’s kabuki floats were once paraded, using traditional techniques and lacquerware craftsmanship. Some relics are preserved in the {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Matsuri float|ja|山車}} * {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Uozu Shrine}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category:tier 4]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Uozu Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Uozu-Jinja006.jpg|250px]] |location= {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}} {{ill|Uozu|ja|魚津市}} Chūō-dōri 1-3-28『富山県神社誌』（1983年11月10日、富山県神社庁発行）346ページ。 |coordinates= {{coord|36|49|05.6|N|137|23|51.5|E|scale:25000}} |Main deity= |Shrine rank= |Established= |Main hall= |Other names= |festival= June 4 |Rituals= Uozu Jinja Festival (June 4–6)
Atago Fire Festival (January 26) }} '''Uozu Shrine''' (魚津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Uozu|ja|魚津市}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}. == Deities == * Main deity: {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}}, originally enshrined in the Shinmei Shrine. * Enshrined deities: - {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|ja|建御名方命}}, originally enshrined in the Shinmei Shrine. - {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|ウカノミタマ|lt=Ukano Mitama no Mikoto}}, originally enshrined in the Inari Shrine. - {{ill|Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|ja|彦火火出見命}}, originally enshrined in the Hi-no-Miya Shrine. - {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命}}, originally enshrined in the Kizuki Shrine. - {{ill|Kamuo Ichihime|ja|神大市比売|lt=Ōichihime no Mikoto}}, originally enshrined in the Ichihime Shrine. - {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速須佐之男命|Susanoo-no-Mikoto=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Takehayasusanoo-no-Mikoto}}, originally a guardian deity of Uozu Castle. - {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売命|lt=Kushinadahime no Mikoto}}, originally a guardian deity of Uozu Castle. - {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, enshrined by Maeda Noritsuna of the Kaga Domain. == History == Originally named Shinmei Shrine, the site became Uozu Shrine in {{ill|1956|ja|昭和31年}} following the integration of five shrines located in areas affected by the {{ill|Uozu Great Fire|ja|魚津大火}}. The new shrine building was completed on {{ill|June 1, 1965|ja|昭和40年6月1日}}.『北日本新聞』1965年5月21日付朝刊12面（富山新川版）『来月1日から遷座祭 魚津神社が完成』より。『富山県神社誌』（1983年11月10日、富山県神社庁発行）346頁。 In {{ill|1965|ja|昭和40年}}, the Atago Shrine, formerly located on the grounds of Uozu Municipal Ōmachi Elementary School, was relocated to the north of the main shrine. In {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43年}}, a large torii gate (8.5 meters tall) was erected through a donation.『富山新聞』1968年9月18日付朝刊10面『魚津神社に大鳥居』より。 In {{ill|1970|ja|昭和45年}}, a sumo ring was added to the grounds.『北日本新聞』1970年8月1日付朝刊（富山新川版）12面『魚津相撲場が完成』より。 == Shrine and Grounds == * Construction began: September 30, 1963 (groundbreaking ceremony held on September 24)『北日本新聞』1963年9月20日付朝刊7面『二千三百万円で新築 7年ぶりに解決の魚津神社』より。 * Completed: May 30, 1965 * Architect: Matsumoto Taisaku * Builder: Asano Nobuharu * Shrine grounds: 1,238 tsubo == Integrated Shrines == [[File:Atago-sha (Uozu)009.jpg|thumb|Atago Shrine]] * Shinmei Shrine (Shinmei-chō) – Established on March 17, 702 (Daibō 2). * Hi-no-Miya Shrine (Shimomuragi-chō) – Rebuilt during the Genki era. * Inari Shrine (Kanayachō) – Built during the Bunsei era. * Ichihime Shrine (Shinkanayachō) – Relics moved to Uozu Shrine in 1979. * Kizuki Shrine (Kōnoura-chō) – Established during the Bunsei era by the Uozu magistrate. == Annual Events == * January 26 – {{ill|Atago Fire Festival|ja|愛宕社の火祭り}} * June 4–6 – Uozu Shrine Festival - Locally known as "Shinmeihan's Festival," this event attracts numerous stalls along the central streets of Uozu. - Historical children’s kabuki floats were once paraded, using traditional techniques and lacquerware craftsmanship. Some relics are preserved in the {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Matsuri float|ja|山車}} * {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Uozu Shrine}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category:tier 4]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Uozu Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Uozu-Jinja006.jpg|250px]] |location= {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}} {{ill|Uozu|ja|魚津市}} Chūō-dōri 1-3-28『富山県神社誌』（1983年11月10日、富山県神社庁発行）346ページ。 |coordinates= {{coord|36|49|05.6|N|137|23|51.5|E|scale:25000}} |Main deity= |Shrine rank= |Established= |Main hall= |Other names= |festival= June 4 |Rituals= Uozu Jinja Festival (June 4–6)
Atago Fire Festival (January 26) }} '''Uozu Shrine''' (魚津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Uozu|ja|魚津市}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}. == Deities == * Main deity: {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}}, originally enshrined in the Shinmei Shrine. * Enshrined deities: - {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|ja|建御名方命}}, originally enshrined in the Shinmei Shrine. - {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|ウカノミタマ|lt=Ukano Mitama no Mikoto}}, originally enshrined in the Inari Shrine. - {{ill|Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|ja|彦火火出見命}}, originally enshrined in the Hi-no-Miya Shrine. - {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命}}, originally enshrined in the Kizuki Shrine. - {{ill|Kamuo Ichihime|ja|神大市比売|lt=Ōichihime no Mikoto}}, originally enshrined in the Ichihime Shrine. - {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速須佐之男命|Susanoo-no-Mikoto=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Takehayasusanoo-no-Mikoto}}, originally a guardian deity of Uozu Castle. - {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売命|lt=Kushinadahime no Mikoto}}, originally a guardian deity of Uozu Castle. - {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, enshrined by Maeda Noritsuna of the Kaga Domain. == History == Originally named Shinmei Shrine, the site became Uozu Shrine in {{ill|1956|ja|昭和31年}} following the integration of five shrines located in areas affected by the {{ill|Uozu Great Fire|ja|魚津大火}}. The new shrine building was completed on {{ill|June 1, 1965|ja|昭和40年6月1日}}.『北日本新聞』1965年5月21日付朝刊12面（富山新川版）『来月1日から遷座祭 魚津神社が完成』より。『富山県神社誌』（1983年11月10日、富山県神社庁発行）346頁。 In {{ill|1965|ja|昭和40年}}, the Atago Shrine, formerly located on the grounds of Uozu Municipal Ōmachi Elementary School, was relocated to the north of the main shrine. In {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43年}}, a large torii gate (8.5 meters tall) was erected through a donation.『富山新聞』1968年9月18日付朝刊10面『魚津神社に大鳥居』より。 In {{ill|1970|ja|昭和45年}}, a sumo ring was added to the grounds.『北日本新聞』1970年8月1日付朝刊（富山新川版）12面『魚津相撲場が完成』より。 == Shrine and Grounds == * Construction began: September 30, 1963 (groundbreaking ceremony held on September 24)『北日本新聞』1963年9月20日付朝刊7面『二千三百万円で新築 7年ぶりに解決の魚津神社』より。 * Completed: May 30, 1965 * Architect: Matsumoto Taisaku * Builder: Asano Nobuharu * Shrine grounds: 1,238 tsubo == Integrated Shrines == [[File:Atago-sha (Uozu)009.jpg|thumb|Atago Shrine]] * Shinmei Shrine (Shinmei-chō) – Established on March 17, 702 (Daibō 2). * Hi-no-Miya Shrine (Shimomuragi-chō) – Rebuilt during the Genki era. * Inari Shrine (Kanayachō) – Built during the Bunsei era. * Ichihime Shrine (Shinkanayachō) – Relics moved to Uozu Shrine in 1979. * Kizuki Shrine (Kōnoura-chō) – Established during the Bunsei era by the Uozu magistrate. == Annual Events == * January 26 – {{ill|Atago Fire Festival|ja|愛宕社の火祭り}} * June 4–6 – Uozu Shrine Festival - Locally known as "Shinmeihan's Festival," this event attracts numerous stalls along the central streets of Uozu. - Historical children’s kabuki floats were once paraded, using traditional techniques and lacquerware craftsmanship. Some relics are preserved in the {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Matsuri float|ja|山車}} * {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Uozu Shrine}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category:tier 4]] [[category:qq {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Uozu Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Uozu-Jinja006.jpg|250px]] |location= {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}} {{ill|Uozu|ja|魚津市}} Chūō-dōri 1-3-28『富山県神社誌』（1983年11月10日、富山県神社庁発行）346ページ。 |coordinates= {{coord|36|49|05.6|N|137|23|51.5|E|scale:25000}} |Main deity= |Shrine rank= |Established= |Main hall= |Other names= |festival= June 4 |Rituals= Uozu Jinja Festival (June 4–6)
Atago Fire Festival (January 26) }} '''Uozu Shrine''' (魚津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Uozu|ja|魚津市}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}. == Deities == * Main deity: {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}}, originally enshrined in the Shinmei Shrine. * Enshrined deities: - {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|ja|建御名方命}}, originally enshrined in the Shinmei Shrine. - {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|ウカノミタマ|lt=Ukano Mitama no Mikoto}}, originally enshrined in the Inari Shrine. - {{ill|Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|ja|彦火火出見命}}, originally enshrined in the Hi-no-Miya Shrine. - {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命}}, originally enshrined in the Kizuki Shrine. - {{ill|Kamuo Ichihime|ja|神大市比売|lt=Ōichihime no Mikoto}}, originally enshrined in the Ichihime Shrine. - {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速須佐之男命|Susanoo-no-Mikoto=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Takehayasusanoo-no-Mikoto}}, originally a guardian deity of Uozu Castle. - {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売命|lt=Kushinadahime no Mikoto}}, originally a guardian deity of Uozu Castle. - {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, enshrined by Maeda Noritsuna of the Kaga Domain. == History == Originally named Shinmei Shrine, the site became Uozu Shrine in {{ill|1956|ja|昭和31年}} following the integration of five shrines located in areas affected by the {{ill|Uozu Great Fire|ja|魚津大火}}. The new shrine building was completed on {{ill|June 1, 1965|ja|昭和40年6月1日}}.『北日本新聞』1965年5月21日付朝刊12面（富山新川版）『来月1日から遷座祭 魚津神社が完成』より。『富山県神社誌』（1983年11月10日、富山県神社庁発行）346頁。 In {{ill|1965|ja|昭和40年}}, the Atago Shrine, formerly located on the grounds of Uozu Municipal Ōmachi Elementary School, was relocated to the north of the main shrine. In {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43年}}, a large torii gate (8.5 meters tall) was erected through a donation.『富山新聞』1968年9月18日付朝刊10面『魚津神社に大鳥居』より。 In {{ill|1970|ja|昭和45年}}, a sumo ring was added to the grounds.『北日本新聞』1970年8月1日付朝刊（富山新川版）12面『魚津相撲場が完成』より。 == Shrine and Grounds == * Construction began: September 30, 1963 (groundbreaking ceremony held on September 24)『北日本新聞』1963年9月20日付朝刊7面『二千三百万円で新築 7年ぶりに解決の魚津神社』より。 * Completed: May 30, 1965 * Architect: Matsumoto Taisaku * Builder: Asano Nobuharu * Shrine grounds: 1,238 tsubo == Integrated Shrines == [[File:Atago-sha (Uozu)009.jpg|thumb|Atago Shrine]] * Shinmei Shrine (Shinmei-chō) – Established on March 17, 702 (Daibō 2). * Hi-no-Miya Shrine (Shimomuragi-chō) – Rebuilt during the Genki era. * Inari Shrine (Kanayachō) – Built during the Bunsei era. * Ichihime Shrine (Shinkanayachō) – Relics moved to Uozu Shrine in 1979. * Kizuki Shrine (Kōnoura-chō) – Established during the Bunsei era by the Uozu magistrate. == Annual Events == * January 26 – {{ill|Atago Fire Festival|ja|愛宕社の火祭り}} * June 4–6 – Uozu Shrine Festival - Locally known as "Shinmeihan's Festival," this event attracts numerous stalls along the central streets of Uozu. - Historical children’s kabuki floats were once paraded, using traditional techniques and lacquerware craftsmanship. Some relics are preserved in the {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Matsuri float|ja|山車}} * {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Uozu Shrine}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category:tier 4]] [[Category:New {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Uozu Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Uozu-Jinja006.jpg|250px]] |location= {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}} {{ill|Uozu|ja|魚津市}} Chūō-dōri 1-3-28『富山県神社誌』（1983年11月10日、富山県神社庁発行）346ページ。 |coordinates= {{coord|36|49|05.6|N|137|23|51.5|E|scale:25000}} |Main deity= |Shrine rank= |Established= |Main hall= |Other names= |festival= June 4 |Rituals= Uozu Jinja Festival (June 4–6)
Atago Fire Festival (January 26) }} '''Uozu Shrine''' (魚津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Uozu|ja|魚津市}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}. == Deities == * Main deity: {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}}, originally enshrined in the Shinmei Shrine. * Enshrined deities: - {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|ja|建御名方命}}, originally enshrined in the Shinmei Shrine. - {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|ウカノミタマ|lt=Ukano Mitama no Mikoto}}, originally enshrined in the Inari Shrine. - {{ill|Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|ja|彦火火出見命}}, originally enshrined in the Hi-no-Miya Shrine. - {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命}}, originally enshrined in the Kizuki Shrine. - {{ill|Kamuo Ichihime|ja|神大市比売|lt=Ōichihime no Mikoto}}, originally enshrined in the Ichihime Shrine. - {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速須佐之男命|Susanoo-no-Mikoto=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Takehayasusanoo-no-Mikoto}}, originally a guardian deity of Uozu Castle. - {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売命|lt=Kushinadahime no Mikoto}}, originally a guardian deity of Uozu Castle. - {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, enshrined by Maeda Noritsuna of the Kaga Domain. == History == Originally named Shinmei Shrine, the site became Uozu Shrine in {{ill|1956|ja|昭和31年}} following the integration of five shrines located in areas affected by the {{ill|Uozu Great Fire|ja|魚津大火}}. The new shrine building was completed on {{ill|June 1, 1965|ja|昭和40年6月1日}}.『北日本新聞』1965年5月21日付朝刊12面（富山新川版）『来月1日から遷座祭 魚津神社が完成』より。『富山県神社誌』（1983年11月10日、富山県神社庁発行）346頁。 In {{ill|1965|ja|昭和40年}}, the Atago Shrine, formerly located on the grounds of Uozu Municipal Ōmachi Elementary School, was relocated to the north of the main shrine. In {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43年}}, a large torii gate (8.5 meters tall) was erected through a donation.『富山新聞』1968年9月18日付朝刊10面『魚津神社に大鳥居』より。 In {{ill|1970|ja|昭和45年}}, a sumo ring was added to the grounds.『北日本新聞』1970年8月1日付朝刊（富山新川版）12面『魚津相撲場が完成』より。 == Shrine and Grounds == * Construction began: September 30, 1963 (groundbreaking ceremony held on September 24)『北日本新聞』1963年9月20日付朝刊7面『二千三百万円で新築 7年ぶりに解決の魚津神社』より。 * Completed: May 30, 1965 * Architect: Matsumoto Taisaku * Builder: Asano Nobuharu * Shrine grounds: 1,238 tsubo == Integrated Shrines == [[File:Atago-sha (Uozu)009.jpg|thumb|Atago Shrine]] * Shinmei Shrine (Shinmei-chō) – Established on March 17, 702 (Daibō 2). * Hi-no-Miya Shrine (Shimomuragi-chō) – Rebuilt during the Genki era. * Inari Shrine (Kanayachō) – Built during the Bunsei era. * Ichihime Shrine (Shinkanayachō) – Relics moved to Uozu Shrine in 1979. * Kizuki Shrine (Kōnoura-chō) – Established during the Bunsei era by the Uozu magistrate. == Annual Events == * January 26 – {{ill|Atago Fire Festival|ja|愛宕社の火祭り}} * June 4–6 – Uozu Shrine Festival - Locally known as "Shinmeihan's Festival," this event attracts numerous stalls along the central streets of Uozu. - Historical children’s kabuki floats were once paraded, using traditional techniques and lacquerware craftsmanship. Some relics are preserved in the {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Matsuri float|ja|山車}} * {{ill|Uozu History and In {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43年}}, a large torii gate (8.5 meters tall) was erected through a donation.『富山新聞』1968年9月18日付朝刊10面『魚津神社に大鳥居』より。 In {{ill|1970|ja|昭和45年}}, a sumo ring was added to the grounds.『北日本新聞』1970年8月1日付朝刊（富山新川版）12面『魚津相撲場が完成』より。 == Shrine and Grounds == * Construction began: September 30, 1963 (groundbreaking ceremony held on September 24)『北日本新聞』1963年9月20日付朝刊7面『二千三百万円で新築 7年ぶりに解決の魚津神社』より。 * Completed: May 30, 1965 * Architect: Matsumoto Taisaku * Builder: Asano Nobuharu * Shrine grounds: 1,238 tsubo == Integrated Shrines == [[File:Atago-sha (Uozu)009.jpg|thumb|Atago Shrine]] * Shinmei Shrine (Shinmei-chō) – Established on March 17, 702 (Daibō 2). * Hi-no-Miya Shrine (Shimomuragi-chō) – Rebuilt during the Genki era. * Inari Shrine (Kanayachō) – Built during the Bunsei era. * Ichihime Shrine (Shinkanayachō) – Relics moved to Uozu Shrine in 1979. * Kizuki Shrine (Kōnoura-chō) – Established during the Bunsei era by the Uozu magistrate. == Annual Events == * January 26 – {{ill|Atago Fire Festival|ja|愛宕社の火祭り}} * June 4–6 – Uozu Shrine Festival - Locally known as "Shinmeihan's Festival," this event attracts numerous stalls along the central streets of Uozu. - Historical children’s kabuki floats were once paraded, using traditional techniques and lacquerware craftsmanship. Some relics are preserved in the {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Matsuri float|ja|山車}} * {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Uozu Shrine}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category:tier 4]] [[Category:Uozu Atago Fire Festival (January 26) }} '''Uozu Shrine''' (魚津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Uozu|ja|魚津市}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}. == Deities == * Main deity: {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}}, originally enshrined in the Shinmei Shrine. * Enshrined deities: - {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|ja|建御名方命}}, originally enshrined in the Shinmei Shrine. - {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|ウカノミタマ|lt=Ukano Mitama no Mikoto}}, originally enshrined in the Inari Shrine. - {{ill|Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|ja|彦火火出見命}}, originally enshrined in the Hi-no-Miya Shrine. - {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命}}, originally enshrined in the Kizuki Shrine. - {{ill|Kamuo Ichihime|ja|神大市比売|lt=Ōichihime no Mikoto}}, originally enshrined in the Ichihime Shrine. - {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速須佐之男命|Susanoo-no-Mikoto=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Takehayasusanoo-no-Mikoto}}, originally a guardian deity of Uozu Castle. - {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売命|lt=Kushinadahime no Mikoto}}, originally a guardian deity of Uozu Castle. - {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}}, enshrined by Maeda Noritsuna of the Kaga Domain. == History == Originally named Shinmei Shrine, the site became Uozu Shrine in {{ill|1956|ja|昭和31年}} following the integration of five shrines located in areas affected by the {{ill|Uozu Great Fire|ja|魚津大火}}. The new shrine building was completed on {{ill|June 1, 1965|ja|昭和40年6月1日}}.『北日本新聞』1965年5月21日付朝刊12面（富山新川版）『来月1日から遷座祭 魚津神社が完成』より。『富山県神社誌』（1983年11月10日、富山県神社庁発行）346頁。 In {{ill|1965|ja|昭和40年}}, the Atago Shrine, formerly located on the grounds of Uozu Municipal Ōmachi Elementary School, was relocated to the north of the main shrine. In {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43年}}, a large torii gate (8.5 meters tall) was erected through a donation.『富山新聞』1968年9月18日付朝刊10面『魚津神社に大鳥居』より。 In {{ill|1970|ja|昭和45年}}, a sumo ring was added to the grounds.『北日本新聞』1970年8月1日付朝刊（富山新川版）12面『魚津相撲場が完成』より。 == Shrine and Grounds == * Construction began: September 30, 1963 (groundbreaking ceremony held on September 24)『北日本新聞』1963年9月20日付朝刊7面『二千三百万円で新築 7年ぶりに解決の魚津神社』より。 * Completed: May 30, 1965 * Architect: Matsumoto Taisaku * Builder: Asano Nobuharu * Shrine grounds: 1,238 tsubo == Integrated Shrines == [[File:Atago-sha (Uozu)009.jpg|thumb|Atago Shrine]] * Shinmei Shrine (Shinmei-chō) – Established on March 17, 702 (Daibō 2). * Hi-no-Miya Shrine (Shimomuragi-chō) – Rebuilt during the Genki era. * Inari Shrine (Kanayachō) – Built during the Bunsei era. * Ichihime Shrine (Shinkanayachō) – Relics moved to Uozu Shrine in 1979. * Kizuki Shrine (Kōnoura-chō) – Established during the Bunsei era by the Uozu magistrate. == Annual Events == * January 26 – {{ill|Atago Fire Festival|ja|愛宕社の火祭り}} * June 4–6 – Uozu Shrine Festival - Locally known as "Shinmeihan's Festival," this event attracts numerous stalls along the central streets of Uozu. - Historical children’s kabuki floats were once paraded, using traditional techniques and lacquerware craftsmanship. Some relics are preserved in the {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Matsuri float|ja|山車}} * {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館}} {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Uozu Shrine}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category:tier 4]] [[Category:Uozu Shrine]]{{citation needed|Uozu In {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43年}}, a large torii gate (8.5 meters tall) was erected through a donation.『富山新聞』1968年9月18日付朝刊10面『魚津神社に大鳥居』より。 In {{ill|1970|ja|昭和45年}}, a sumo ring was added to the grounds.『北日本新聞』1970年8月1日付朝刊（富山新川版）12面『魚津相撲場が完成』より。 == Shrine and Grounds == * Construction began: September 30, 1963 (groundbreaking ceremony held on September 24)『北日本新聞』1963年9月20日付朝刊7面『二千三百万円で新築 7年ぶりに解決の魚津神社』より。 * Completed: May 30, 1965 * Architect: Matsumoto Taisaku * Builder: Asano Nobuharu * Shrine grounds: 1,238 tsubo == Integrated Shrines == [[File:Atago-sha (Uozu)009.jpg|thumb|Atago Shrine]] * Shinmei Shrine (Shinmei-chō) – Established on March 17, 702 (Daibō 2). * Hi-no-Miya Shrine (Shimomuragi-chō) – Rebuilt during the Genki era. * Inari Shrine (Kanayachō) – Built during the Bunsei era. * Ichihime Shrine (Shinkanayachō) – Relics moved to Uozu Shrine in 1979. * Kizuki Shrine (Kōnoura-chō) – Established during the Bunsei era by the Uozu magistrate. == Annual Events == * January 26 – {{ill|Atago Fire Festival|ja|愛宕社の火祭り}} * June 4–6 – Uozu Shrine Festival - Locally known as "Shinmeihan's Festival," this event attracts numerous stalls along the central streets of Uozu. - Historical children’s kabuki floats were once paraded, using traditional techniques and lacquerware craftsmanship. Some relics are preserved in the {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Matsuri float|ja|山車}} * {{ill|Uozu History and In {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43年}}, a large torii gate (8.5 meters tall) was erected through a donation.『富山新聞』1968年9月18日付朝刊10面『魚津神社に大鳥居』より。 In {{ill|1970|ja|昭和45年}}, a sumo ring was added to the grounds.『北日本新聞』1970年8月1日付朝刊（富山新川版）12面『魚津相撲場が完成』より。 == Shrine and Grounds == * Construction began: September 30, 1963 (groundbreaking ceremony held on September 24)『北日本新聞』1963年9月20日付朝刊7面『二千三百万円で新築 7年ぶりに解決の魚津神社』より。 * Completed: May 30, 1965 * Architect: Matsumoto Taisaku * Builder: Asano Nobuharu * Shrine grounds: 1,238 tsubo == Integrated Shrines == [[File:Atago-sha (Uozu)009.jpg|thumb|Atago Shrine]] * Shinmei Shrine (Shinmei-chō) – Established on March 17, 702 (Daibō 2). * Hi-no-Miya Shrine (Shimomuragi-chō) – Rebuilt during the Genki era. * Inari Shrine (Kanayachō) – Built during the Bunsei era. * Ichihime Shrine (Shinkanayachō) – Relics moved to Uozu Shrine in 1979. * Kizuki Shrine (Kōnoura-chō) – Established during the Bunsei era by the Uozu magistrate. == Annual Events == * January 26 – {{ill|Atago Fire Festival|ja|愛宕社の火祭り}} * June 4–6 – Uozu Shrine Festival - Locally known as "Shinmeihan's Festival," this event attracts numerous stalls along the central streets of Uozu. - Historical children’s kabuki floats were once paraded, using traditional techniques and lacquerware craftsmanship. Some relics are preserved in the {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Matsuri float|ja|山車}} * {{ill|Uozu History and In {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43rd year }}, a large torii gate (8.5 meters tall) was erected through a donation.『富山新聞』1968年9月18日付朝刊10面『魚津神社に大鳥居』より。 In {{ill|1970|ja|昭和45th year }}, a sumo ring was added to the grounds.『北日本新聞』1970年8月1日付朝刊（富山新川版）12面『魚津相撲場が完成』より。 == Shrine and Grounds == * Construction began: September 30, 1963 (groundbreaking ceremony held on September 24)『北日本新聞』1963rd year 9月20日付朝刊7面『二千三百万円で新築 7th year ぶりに解決の魚津神社』より。 * Completed: May 30, 1965 * Architect: Matsumoto Taisaku * Builder: Asano Nobuharu * Shrine grounds: 1,238 tsubo == Integrated Shrines == [[File:Atago-sha (Uozu)009.jpg|thumb|Atago Shrine]] * Shinmei Shrine (Shinmei-chō) – Established on March 17, 702 (Daibō 2). * Hi-no-Miya Shrine (Shimomuragi-chō) – Rebuilt during the Genki era. * Inari Shrine (Kanayachō) – Built during the Bunsei era. * Ichihime Shrine (Shinkanayachō) – Relics moved to Uozu Shrine in 1979. * Kizuki Shrine (Kōnoura-chō) – Established during the Bunsei era by the Uozu magistrate. == Annual Events == * January 26 – {{ill|Atago Fire Festival|ja|愛宕社の火祭り}} * June 4–6 – Uozu Shrine Festival - Locally known as "Shinmeihan's Festival," this event attracts numerous stalls along the central streets of Uozu. - Historical children’s kabuki floats were once paraded, using traditional techniques and lacquerware craftsmanship. Some relics are preserved in the {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Matsuri float|ja|山車}} * {{ill|Uozu History and In {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43rd year }}, a large torii gate (8.5 meters tall) was erected through a donation.『富山新聞』1968th year 9月18日付朝刊10面『魚津神社に大鳥居』より。 In {{ill|1970|ja|昭和45th year }}, a sumo ring was added to the grounds.『北日本新聞』1970th year 8月1日付朝刊（富山新川版）12面『魚津相撲場が完成』より。 == Shrine and Grounds == * Construction began: September 30, 1963 (groundbreaking ceremony held on September 24)『北日本新聞』1963rd year 9月20日付朝刊7面『二千三百万円で新築 7th year ぶりに解決の魚津神社』より。 * Completed: May 30, 1965 * Architect: Matsumoto Taisaku * Builder: Asano Nobuharu * Shrine grounds: 1,238 tsubo == Integrated Shrines == [[File:Atago-sha (Uozu)009.jpg|thumb|Atago Shrine]] * Shinmei Shrine (Shinmei-chō) – Established on March 17, 702 (Daibō 2). * Hi-no-Miya Shrine (Shimomuragi-chō) – Rebuilt during the Genki era. * Inari Shrine (Kanayachō) – Built during the Bunsei era. * Ichihime Shrine (Shinkanayachō) – Relics moved to Uozu Shrine in 1979. * Kizuki Shrine (Kōnoura-chō) – Established during the Bunsei era by the Uozu magistrate. == Annual Events == * January 26 – {{ill|Atago Fire Festival|ja|愛宕社の火祭り}} * June 4–6 – Uozu Shrine Festival ** Locally known as "Shinmeihan's Festival," this event attracts numerous stalls along the central streets of Uozu. ** Historical children’s kabuki floats were once paraded, using traditional techniques and lacquerware craftsmanship. Some relics are preserved in the {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Matsuri float|ja|山車}} * {{ill|Uozu History and The new shrine building was completed on {{ill|June 1, 1965|ja|昭和40th year 6月1日}}.『北日本新聞』1965th year 5月21日付朝刊12面（富山新川版）『来月1日から遷座祭 魚津神社が完成』より。『富山県神社誌』（1983rd year 11月10日、富山県神社庁発行）346頁。 In {{ill|1965|ja|昭和40th year }}, the Atago Shrine, formerly located on the grounds of Uozu Municipal Ōmachi Elementary School, was relocated to the north of the main shrine. In {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43rd year }}, a large torii gate (8.5 meters tall) was erected through a donation.『富山新聞』1968th year 9月18日付朝刊10面『魚津神社に大鳥居』より。 In {{ill|1970|ja|昭和45th year }}, a sumo ring was added to the grounds.『北日本新聞』1970th year 8月1日付朝刊（富山新川版）12面『魚津相撲場が完成』より。 == Shrine and Grounds == * Construction began: September 30, 1963 (groundbreaking ceremony held on September 24)『北日本新聞』1963rd year 9月20日付朝刊7面『二千三百万円で新築 7th year ぶりに解決の魚津神社』より。 * Completed: May 30, 1965 * Architect: Matsumoto Taisaku * Builder: Asano Nobuharu * Shrine grounds: 1,238 tsubo == Integrated Shrines == [[File:Atago-sha (Uozu)009.jpg|thumb|Atago Shrine]] * Shinmei Shrine (Shinmei-chō) – Established on March 17, 702 (Daibō 2). * Hi-no-Miya Shrine (Shimomuragi-chō) – Rebuilt during the Genki era. * Inari Shrine (Kanayachō) – Built during the Bunsei era. * Ichihime Shrine (Shinkanayachō) – Relics moved to Uozu Shrine in 1979. * Kizuki Shrine (Kōnoura-chō) – Established during the Bunsei era by the Uozu magistrate. == Annual Events == * January 26 – {{ill|Atago Fire Festival|ja|愛宕社の火祭り}} * June 4–6 – Uozu Shrine Festival ** Locally known as "Shinmeihan's Festival," this event attracts numerous stalls along the central streets of Uozu. ** Historical children’s kabuki floats were once paraded, using traditional techniques and lacquerware craftsmanship. Some relics are preserved in the {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Matsuri float|ja|山車}} * {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館}} {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Uozu Shrine}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category:Uozu Shrine]]{{citation needed|Uozu
