revision.comment: Bot: Reformatted page - moved categories, interwikis, DEFAULTSORT, and removed duplicates.
Bot: Replaced ill templates with appended |12=draft and created corresponding redirect pages.
Text replacement - "|天照大神|" to "|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|"
Bot: single‑shot reformat + ill→|12=draft + draft categories + iws + DEFAULTSORT + move HTML comments + meta‑cat check

Atago Fire Festival (January 26) }} '''Uozu Shrine''' (魚津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Uozu|ja|魚津市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Deities == * Main deity: {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, originally enshrined in the Shinmei Shrine. * Enshrined deities: ** {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, originally enshrined in the Shinmei Shrine. ** {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|ウカノミタマ|lt=Ukano Mitama no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, originally enshrined in the Inari Shrine. ** {{ill|Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|ja|彦火火出見命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, originally enshrined in the Hi-no-Miya Shrine. ** {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, originally enshrined in the Kizuki Shrine. ** {{ill|Kamuo Ichihime|ja|神大市比売|lt=Ōichihime no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, originally enshrined in the Ichihime Shrine. ** {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速須佐之男命|Susanoo-no-Mikoto=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Takehayasusanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, originally a guardian deity of Uozu Castle. ** {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売命|lt=Kushinadahime no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, originally a guardian deity of Uozu Castle. ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, enshrined by Maeda Noritsuna of the Kaga Domain. == History == Originally named Shinmei Shrine, the site became Uozu Shrine in {{ill|1956|ja|昭和31st year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} following the integration of five shrines located in areas affected by the {{ill|Uozu Great Fire|ja|魚津大火|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The new shrine building was completed on {{ill|June 1, 1965|ja|昭和40th year 6月1日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.『北日本新聞』1965th year 5月21日付朝刊12面（富山新川版）『来月1日から遷座祭 魚津神社が完成』より。『富山県神社誌』（1983rd year 11月10日、富山県神社庁発行）346頁。 In {{ill|1965|ja|昭和40th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the Atago Shrine, formerly located on the grounds of Uozu Municipal Ōmachi Elementary School, was relocated to the north of the main shrine. In {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a large torii gate (8.5 meters tall) was erected through a donation.『富山新聞』1968th year 9月18日付朝刊10面『魚津神社に大鳥居』より。 In {{ill|1970|ja|昭和45th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a sumo ring was added to the grounds.『北日本新聞』1970th year 8月1日付朝刊（富山新川版）12面『魚津相撲場が完成』より。 == Shrine and Grounds == * Construction began: September 30, 1963 (groundbreaking ceremony held on September 24)『北日本新聞』1963rd year 9月20日付朝刊7面『二千三百万円で新築 7th year ぶりに解決の魚津神社』より。 * Completed: May 30, 1965 * Architect: Matsumoto Taisaku * Builder: Asano Nobuharu * Shrine grounds: 1,238 tsubo == Integrated Shrines == [[File:Atago-sha (Uozu)009.jpg|thumb|Atago Shrine]] * Shinmei Shrine (Shinmei-chō) – Established on March 17, 702 (Daibō 2). * Hi-no-Miya Shrine (Shimomuragi-chō) – Rebuilt during the Genki era. * Inari Shrine (Kanayachō) – Built during the Bunsei era. * Ichihime Shrine (Shinkanayachō) – Relics moved to Uozu Shrine in 1979. * Kizuki Shrine (Kōnoura-chō) – Established during the Bunsei era by the Uozu magistrate. == Annual Events == * January 26 – {{ill|Atago Fire Festival|ja|愛宕社の火祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * June 4–6 – Uozu Shrine Festival ** Locally known as "Shinmeihan's Festival," this event attracts numerous stalls along the central streets of Uozu. ** Historical children’s kabuki floats were once paraded, using traditional techniques and lacquerware craftsmanship. Some relics are preserved in the {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Matsuri float|ja|山車|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Uozu Shrine}} {{citation needed|Uozu Shrine}} {{citation needed|Uozu Shrine}} {{citation needed|Uozu Shrine}} {{citation needed|Uozu Shrine}} {{citation needed|Uozu Shrine}} {{citation needed|Uozu Shrine}} {{citation needed|Uozu Shrine}} {{citation needed|Uozu Shrine}} {{citation needed|Uozu Shrine}} {{citation needed|Uozu Shrine}} {{citation needed|Uozu Shrine}} {{citation needed|Uozu Shrine}} {{citation needed|Uozu Shrine}} {{citation needed|Uozu Shrine}} {{Atago Faith}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Uozu Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:History of Uozu City]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Uozu City]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Atago Shrines]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Uozu Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Uozu Shrine]] [[ja:魚津神社]] [[sn:ozu Shrine]]

revision.comment: 36 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:魚津神社 up to rev 103419258
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 魚津神社 |画像= [[File:Uozu-Jinja006.jpg|250px]] |所在地= [[富山県]][[魚津市]]中央通り1-3-28『富山県神社誌』（1983年11月10日、富山県神社庁発行）346ページ。 |位置= {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|49|05.6|N|137|23|51.5|E|scale:25000}} |祭神= |社格= |創建= |本殿= |別名= |例祭= 6月4日 |神事= 魚津神社祭礼（6月4-6日）
愛宕社の火祭り（1月26日） }} '''魚津神社'''（'''うおづじんじゃ'''）は、[[富山県]][[魚津市]]にある[[神社]]である。 ==祭神== *主祭神…[[天照大神|天照皇太神]]（あまてらすおおみかみ）　元は神明社で祀る。 *相殿神…[[建御名方命]]（たけみなかたのみこと）　元は神明社で祀る。 *[[ウカノミタマ|蒼稲魂命]]（うかのみたまのみこと）　元は稲荷社で祀る。 *[[彦火火出見命]]（ひこほほでみのみこと）元は火之宮社で祀る。 *[[素盞鳴命]]（すさのおのみこと）　元は杵築社で祀る。 *[[神大市比売|大市比売命]]（おおいちひめのみこと）　元は市姫社で祀る。 *[[建速須佐之男命]]（たけはやすさのおのみこと）　元は旧魚津城の鬼門除けの守護神 *[[櫛名田比売命]]（くしなだひめのみこと）　元は旧魚津城の鬼門除けの守護神 *[[菅原大神]]（[[菅原道真]]公）　加賀藩主、前田紀綱が勧請(かんじょう) （※勧請…神仏の分身・分霊を他の地に移して祭ること） ==由緒== 元々は神明社という名前の神社だったが、[[1956年]]（[[昭和]]31年）に[[魚津大火]]が発生し、その復興事業として火災地域にあった5つの神社を統合し、魚津神社となった。[[1965年]]（昭和40年）[[6月1日]]に新社殿を再建し『北日本新聞』1965年5月21日付朝刊12面（富山新川版）『来月1日から遷座祭 魚津神社が完成』より。『富山県神社誌』（1983年11月10日、富山県神社庁発行）346頁。、[[1965年]]（昭和40年）[[9月25日]]に[[魚津市立大町小学校]]敷地内にあった愛宕神社（昔は[[魚津城]]の守護神として有名だった）の御神体を遷座し、[[1967年]]（昭和42年）に神社を本殿の北隣に移した『明理 120周年を迎えて』（1993年11月13日、魚津市立大町小学校編集・発行）47頁。。 その後、[[1968年]]（昭和43年）[[9月17日]]に[[富山縣護國神社]]に次ぐ県内2番目の規模（当時）の大鳥居（高さ8.5m、幅11m、柱の下の直径60cm）が富山県米穀株式会社社長からの寄付により竣工した『富山新聞』1968年9月18日付朝刊10面『魚津神社に大鳥居』より。。さらに[[1970年]]（昭和45年）[[7月30日]]には境内に相撲場（檜柱の神明造の屋根（茶褐色のトタン葺）を備えた、柱と柱の間5.5m、天井まで4.5m）が竣工し『北日本新聞』1970年8月1日付朝刊（富山新川版）12面『魚津相撲場が完成』より。、現在に至る。 ==社殿・境内== * 着工 - [[1963年]]（昭和38年）[[9月30日]]（起工式は同年[[9月24日]]『北日本新聞』1963年9月20日付朝刊7面『二千三百万円で新築 7年ぶりに解決の魚津神社』より。） * 竣工 - [[1965年]]（昭和40年）[[5月30日]] * 設計者 - 松本太作 * 施工者 - 朝野信治 * 境内地 - 1,238坪 ==統合した神社== [[File:Atago-sha (Uozu)009.jpg|thumb|愛宕社]] * 神明社（神明町） - [[702年]]（[[大宝 (日本)|大宝]]2年）[[3月17日]]創立の郷社。 * 火の宮神社（下村木町） - [[元亀]]年間に再建された。 * 稲荷神社（金屋町） - [[文政]]年間に建設された。 * 市姫神社（新金屋町） - 鳥居の掲額と狛犬一対が、[[1979年]]（昭和54年）に当神社敷地内に移される。 * 杵築社 (金浦町） - 文政年間に魚津奉行により創立 ==年中行事== * 1月26日- [[愛宕社の火祭り]] * 6月4-6日- 魚津神社（神明社）祭礼 ** 地元では「しんめはんの祭り」と呼ばれる富山県東部で最も[[露店]]が出る祭りで、'''魚津神社'''をメインに、中央通り、魚津中央線に最も露店が集中する。 ** [[江戸時代]]から[[大正時代]]まで子供歌舞伎曳山（[[山車]]）が出ていたが、創設年度はよくわかっていないほか、江戸時代に何基あったのかも、火事による焼失などにより増減するためよくわかっていない。[[明治時代]]には'''神明町、真成寺町、金屋町、新町、大町、餌指町、下町'''などの町が[[山車|曳山]]を所有していた。また、[[日露戦争]]勝利記念の年には11基の曳山が曳かれ賑わったという。しかし[[大正時代]]に入ると、道路に電線が引かれるようになったこと、曳山保存に手間とお金が掛かることなどにより徐々に曳かれなくなっていった。曳山は、屋台造りで前部に舞台があり、[[浄瑠璃]]に合わせて子供たちが[[歌舞伎]]を演じる子供歌舞伎曳山で、魚津の伝統工芸である[[魚津漆器]]の塗りを用いるなど、技術の粋を集めたものである。現在は、[[魚津歴史民俗博物館]]にて勾欄などが展示されている。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 『魚津の歴史読本シリーズ(6) 魚津の民俗芸能』（魚津市[[教育委員会]]）[[2016年]]（[[平成]]28年）3月31日発行 ==関連項目== * [[山車]] * [[魚津歴史民俗博物館]] {{DEFAULTSORT:うおつしんしや}} [[Category:富山県の神社]] [[Category:神明神社]] [[Category:魚津市の歴史]] [[Category:魚津市の建築物]] {{shinto-stub}}

[[1963年]]（昭和38年）[[9月30日]]（起工式は同年[[9月24日]]『北日本新聞』1963年9月20日付朝刊7面『二千三百万円で新築 7年ぶりに解決の魚津神社』より。） * 竣工 - [[1965年]]（昭和40年）[[5月30日]] * 設計者 - 松本太作 * 施工者 - 朝野信治 * 境内地 - 1,238坪 ==統合した神社== [[File:Atago-sha (Uozu)009.jpg|thumb|愛宕社]] * 神明社（神明町） - [[702年]]（[[大宝 (日本)|大宝]]2年）[[3月17日]]創立の郷社。 * 火の宮神社（下村木町） - [[元亀]]年間に再建された。 * 稲荷神社（金屋町） - [[文政]]年間に建設された。 * 市姫神社（新金屋町） - 鳥居の掲額と狛犬一対が、[[1979年]]（昭和54年）に当神社敷地内に移される。 * 杵築社 (金浦町） - 文政年間に魚津奉行により創立 ==年中行事== * 1月26日- [[愛宕社の火祭り]] * 6月4-6日- 魚津神社（神明社）祭礼 ** 地元では「しんめはんの祭り」と呼ばれる富山県東部で最も[[露店]]が出る祭りで、'''魚津神社'''をメインに、中央通り、魚津中央線に最も露店が集中する。 ** [[江戸時代]]から[[大正時代]]まで子供歌舞伎曳山（[[山車]]）が出ていたが、創設年度はよくわかっていないほか、江戸時代に何基あったのかも、火事による焼失などにより増減するためよくわかっていない。[[明治時代]]には'''神明町、真成寺町、金屋町、新町、大町、餌指町、下町'''などの町が[[山車|曳山]]を所有していた。また、[[日露戦争]]勝利記念の年には11基の曳山が曳かれ賑わったという。しかし[[大正時代]]に入ると、道路に電線が引かれるようになったこと、曳山保存に手間とお金が掛かることなどにより徐々に曳かれなくなっていった。曳山は、屋台造りで前部に舞台があり、[[浄瑠璃]]に合わせて子供たちが[[歌舞伎]]を演じる子供歌舞伎曳山で、魚津の伝統工芸である[[魚津漆器]]の塗りを用いるなど、技術の粋を集めたものである。現在は、[[魚津歴史民俗博物館]]にて勾欄などが展示されている。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 『魚津の歴史読本シリーズ(6) 魚津の民俗芸能』（魚津市[[教育委員会]]）[[2016年]]（[[平成]]28年）3月31日発行 ==関連項目== * [[山車]] * [[魚津歴史民俗博物館]] {{DEFAULTSORT:うおつしんしや}} [[Category:富山県の神社]] [[Category:神明神社]] [[Category:魚津市の歴史]] [[Category:魚津市の建築物]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Uozu Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Uozu-Jinja006.jpg|250px]] |location= {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Uozu|ja|魚津市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Chūō-dōri 1-3-28『富山県神社誌』（1983rd year 11月10日、富山県神社庁発行）346ページ。 |coordinates= {{coord|36|49|05.6|N|137|23|51.5|E|scale:25000}} |Main deity= |Shrine Rank= |Established= |Main hall= |Other names= |festival= June 4 |Rituals= Uozu Jinja Festival (June 4–6)
Atago Fire Festival (January 26) }} '''Uozu Shrine''' (魚津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Uozu|ja|魚津市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Deities == * Main deity: {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, originally enshrined in the Shinmei Shrine. * Enshrined deities: ** {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, originally enshrined in the Shinmei Shrine. ** {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|ウカノミタマ|lt=Ukano Mitama no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, originally enshrined in the Inari Shrine. ** {{ill|Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|ja|彦火火出見命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, originally enshrined in the Hi-no-Miya Shrine. ** {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, originally enshrined in the Kizuki Shrine. ** {{ill|Kamuo Ichihime|ja|神大市比売|lt=Ōichihime no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, originally enshrined in the Ichihime Shrine. ** {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速須佐之男命|Susanoo-no-Mikoto=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Takehayasusanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, originally a guardian deity of Uozu Castle. ** {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売命|lt=Kushinadahime no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, originally a guardian deity of Uozu Castle. ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, enshrined by Maeda Noritsuna of the Kaga Domain. == History == Originally named Shinmei Shrine, the site became Uozu Shrine in {{ill|1956|ja|昭和31st year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} following the integration of five shrines located in areas affected by the {{ill|Uozu Great Fire|ja|魚津大火|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The new shrine building was completed on {{ill|June 1, 1965|ja|昭和40th year 6月1日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.『北日本新聞』1965th year 5月21日付朝刊12面（富山新川版）『来月1日から遷座祭 魚津神社が完成』より。『富山県神社誌』（1983rd year 11月10日、富山県神社庁発行）346頁。 In {{ill|1965|ja|昭和40th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the Atago Shrine, formerly located on the grounds of Uozu Municipal Ōmachi Elementary School, was relocated to the north of the main shrine. In {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a large torii gate (8.5 meters tall) was erected through a donation.『富山新聞』1968th year 9月18日付朝刊10面『魚津神社に大鳥居』より。 In {{ill|1970|ja|昭和45th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a sumo ring was added to the grounds.『北日本新聞』1970th year 8月1日付朝刊（富山新川版）12面『魚津相撲場が完成』より。 == Shrine and Grounds == * Construction began: September 30, 1963 (groundbreaking ceremony held on September 24)『北日本新聞』1963rd year 9月20日付朝刊7面『二千三百万円で新築 7th year ぶりに解決の魚津神社』より。 * Completed: May 30, 1965 * Architect: Matsumoto Taisaku * Builder: Asano Nobuharu * Shrine grounds: 1,238 tsubo == Integrated Shrines == [[File:Atago-sha (Uozu)009.jpg|thumb|Atago Shrine]] * Shinmei Shrine (Shinmei-chō) – Established on March 17, 702 (Daibō 2). * Hi-no-Miya Shrine (Shimomuragi-chō) – Rebuilt during the Genki era. * Inari Shrine (Kanayachō) – Built during the Bunsei era. * Ichihime Shrine (Shinkanayachō) – Relics moved to Uozu Shrine in 1979. * Kizuki Shrine (Kōnoura-chō) – Established during the Bunsei era by the Uozu magistrate. == Annual Events == * January 26 – {{ill|Atago Fire Festival|ja|愛宕社の火祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * June 4–6 – Uozu Shrine Festival ** Locally known as "Shinmeihan's Festival," this event attracts numerous stalls along the central streets of Uozu. ** Historical children’s kabuki floats were once paraded, using traditional techniques and lacquerware craftsmanship. Some relics are preserved in the {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Matsuri float|ja|山車|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Atago Faith}} {{translated page|ja|魚津神社|version=103419258|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Uozu Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:History of Uozu City]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Uozu City]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Atago Shrines]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Uozu Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Uozu Shrine]] [[ja:魚津神社]] [[sn:ozu Shrine]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Uozu Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Uozu-Jinja006.jpg|250px]] |location= {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Uozu|ja|魚津市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu|qq=}} Chūō-dōri 1-3-28『富山県神社誌』（1983rd year 11月10日、富山県神社庁発行）346ページ。 |coordinates= {{coord|36|49|05.6|N|137|23|51.5|E|scale:25000}} |Main deity= |Shrine Rank= |Established= |Main hall= |Other names= |festival= June 4 |Rituals= Uozu Jinja Festival (June 4–6)
Atago Fire Festival (January 26) }} '''Uozu Shrine''' (魚津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Uozu|ja|魚津市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Prefecture|qq=}}. == Deities == * Main deity: {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}, originally enshrined in the Shinmei Shrine. * Enshrined deities: ** {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, originally enshrined in the Shinmei Shrine. ** {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|ウカノミタマ|lt=Ukano Mitama no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}}, originally enshrined in the Inari Shrine. ** {{ill|Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|ja|彦火火出見命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|qq=}}, originally enshrined in the Hi-no-Miya Shrine. ** {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, originally enshrined in the Kizuki Shrine. ** {{ill|Kamuo Ichihime|ja|神大市比売|lt=Ōichihime no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamuo Ichihime|qq=}}, originally enshrined in the Ichihime Shrine. ** {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速須佐之男命|Susanoo-no-Mikoto=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Takehayasusanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, originally a guardian deity of Uozu Castle. ** {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売命|lt=Kushinadahime no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=}}, originally a guardian deity of Uozu Castle. ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}}, enshrined by Maeda Noritsuna of the Kaga Domain. == History == Originally named Shinmei Shrine, the site became Uozu Shrine in {{ill|1956|ja|昭和31st year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1956|qq=}} following the integration of five shrines located in areas affected by the {{ill|Uozu Great Fire|ja|魚津大火|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu Great Fire|qq=}}. The new shrine building was completed on {{ill|June 1, 1965|ja|昭和40th year 6月1日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 1, 1965|qq=}}.『北日本新聞』1965th year 5月21日付朝刊12面（富山新川版）『来月1日から遷座祭 魚津神社が完成』より。『富山県神社誌』（1983rd year 11月10日、富山県神社庁発行）346頁。 In {{ill|1965|ja|昭和40th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1965|qq=}}, the Atago Shrine, formerly located on the grounds of Uozu Municipal Ōmachi Elementary School, was relocated to the north of the main shrine. In {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1968|qq=}}, a large torii gate (8.5 meters tall) was erected through a donation.『富山新聞』1968th year 9月18日付朝刊10面『魚津神社に大鳥居』より。 In {{ill|1970|ja|昭和45th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1970|qq=}}, a sumo ring was added to the grounds.『北日本新聞』1970th year 8月1日付朝刊（富山新川版）12面『魚津相撲場が完成』より。 == Shrine and Grounds == * Construction began: September 30, 1963 (groundbreaking ceremony held on September 24)『北日本新聞』1963rd year 9月20日付朝刊7面『二千三百万円で新築 7th year ぶりに解決の魚津神社』より。 * Completed: May 30, 1965 * Architect: Matsumoto Taisaku * Builder: Asano Nobuharu * Shrine grounds: 1,238 tsubo == Integrated Shrines == [[File:Atago-sha (Uozu)009.jpg|thumb|Atago Shrine]] * Shinmei Shrine (Shinmei-chō) – Established on March 17, 702 (Daibō 2). * Hi-no-Miya Shrine (Shimomuragi-chō) – Rebuilt during the Genki era. * Inari Shrine (Kanayachō) – Built during the Bunsei era. * Ichihime Shrine (Shinkanayachō) – Relics moved to Uozu Shrine in 1979. * Kizuki Shrine (Kōnoura-chō) – Established during the Bunsei era by the Uozu magistrate. == Annual Events == * January 26 – {{ill|Atago Fire Festival|ja|愛宕社の火祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Fire Festival|qq=}} * June 4–6 – Uozu Shrine Festival ** Locally known as "Shinmeihan's Festival," this event attracts numerous stalls along the central streets of Uozu. ** Historical children’s kabuki floats were once paraded, using traditional techniques and lacquerware craftsmanship. Some relics are preserved in the {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu History and Folklore Museum|qq=}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Matsuri float|ja|山車|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuri float|qq=}} * {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu History and Folklore Museum|qq=}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Atago Faith}} {{translated page|ja|魚津神社|version=103419258|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Uozu Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:History of Uozu City]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Uozu City]] [[Category:Atago Shrines]] [[sn:Uozu Shrine]]}} [[sn:Uozu Shrine]] [[ja:魚津神社]] [[sn:ozu Shrine]]

{{ill|Uozu|ja|魚津市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Prefecture|qq=}}. == Deities == * Main deity: {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}, originally enshrined in the Shinmei Shrine. * Enshrined deities: ** {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, originally enshrined in the Shinmei Shrine. ** {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|ウカノミタマ|lt=Ukano Mitama no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}}, originally enshrined in the Inari Shrine. ** {{ill|Hoori|lt=Hoori|lt=Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|ja|彦火火出見命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|qq=}}, originally enshrined in the Hi-no-Miya Shrine. ** {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, originally enshrined in the Kizuki Shrine. ** {{ill|Kamuo Ichihime|ja|神大市比売|lt=Ōichihime no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamuo Ichihime|qq=}}, originally enshrined in the Ichihime Shrine. ** {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速須佐之男命|Susanoo-no-Mikoto=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Takehayasusanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, originally a guardian deity of Uozu Castle. ** {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売命|lt=Kushinadahime no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=}}, originally a guardian deity of Uozu Castle. ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}}, enshrined by Maeda Noritsuna of the Kaga Domain. == History == Originally named Shinmei Shrine, the site became Uozu Shrine in {{ill|1956|ja|昭和31st year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1956|qq=}} following the integration of five shrines located in areas affected by the {{ill|Uozu Great Fire|ja|魚津大火|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu Great Fire|qq=}}. The new shrine building was completed on {{ill|June 1, 1965|ja|昭和40th year 6月1日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 1, 1965|qq=}}.『北日本新聞』1965th year 5月21日付朝刊12面（富山新川版）『来月1日から遷座祭 魚津神社が完成』より。『富山県神社誌』（1983rd year 11月10日、富山県神社庁発行）346頁。 In {{ill|1965|ja|昭和40年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1965|qq=}}, the Atago Shrine, formerly located on the grounds of Uozu Municipal Ōmachi Elementary School, was relocated to the north of the main shrine. In {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1968|qq=}}, a large torii gate (8.5 meters tall) was erected through a donation.『富山新聞』1968th year 9月18日付朝刊10面『魚津神社に大鳥居』より。 In {{ill|1970|ja|昭和45年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1970|qq=}}, a sumo ring was added to the grounds.『北日本新聞』1970th year 8月1日付朝刊（富山新川版）12面『魚津相撲場が完成』より。 == Shrine and Grounds == * Construction began: September 30, 1963 (groundbreaking ceremony held on September 24)『北日本新聞』1963rd year 9月20日付朝刊7面『二千三百万円で新築 7th year ぶりに解決の魚津神社』より。 * Completed: May 30, 1965 * Architect: Matsumoto Taisaku * Builder: Asano Nobuharu * Shrine grounds: 1,238 tsubo == Integrated Shrines == [[File:Atago-sha (Uozu)009.jpg|thumb|Atago Shrine]] * Shinmei Shrine (Shinmei-chō) – Established on March 17, 702 (Daibō 2). * Hi-no-Miya Shrine (Shimomuragi-chō) – Rebuilt during the Genki era. * Inari Shrine (Kanayachō) – Built during the Bunsei era. * Ichihime Shrine (Shinkanayachō) – Relics moved to Uozu Shrine in 1979. * Kizuki Shrine (Kōnoura-chō) – Established during the Bunsei era by the Uozu magistrate. == Annual Events == * January 26 – {{ill|Atago Fire Festival|ja|愛宕社の火祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Fire Festival|qq=}} * June 4–6 – Uozu Shrine Festival ** Locally known as "Shinmeihan's Festival," this event attracts numerous stalls along the central streets of Uozu. ** Historical children’s kabuki floats were once paraded, using traditional techniques and lacquerware craftsmanship. Some relics are preserved in the {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu History and Folklore Museum|qq=}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Matsuri float|ja|山車|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuri float|qq=}} * {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu History and Folklore Museum|qq=}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Atago Faith}} {{translated page|ja|魚津神社|version=103419258|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:History of Uozu City]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Uozu City]] [[Category:Atago Shrines]] [[sn:Uozu Shrine]]}} [[sn:Uozu Shrine]] [[ja:魚津神社]] [[sn:ozu Shrine]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Uozu Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Uozu-Jinja006.jpg|250px]] |location= [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]] [[:en:Uozu, Toyama|Uozu]] Chūō-dōri 1-3-28『富山県神社誌』（1983rd year 11月10日、富山県神社庁発行）346ページ。 |coordinates= {{coord|36|49|05.6|N|137|23|51.5|E|scale:25000}} |Main deity= |Shrine Rank= |Established= |Main hall= |Other names= |festival= June 4 |Rituals= Uozu Jinja Festival (June 4–6)
Atago Fire Festival (January 26) }} '''Uozu Shrine''' (魚津神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Uozu, Toyama|Uozu]], [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]]. == Deities == * Main deity: [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], originally enshrined in the Shinmei Shrine. * Enshrined deities: ** {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, originally enshrined in the Shinmei Shrine. ** [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukano Mitama no Mikoto]], originally enshrined in the Inari Shrine. ** {{ill|Hoori|lt=Hoori|lt=Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|ja|彦火火出見命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|qq=}}, originally enshrined in the Hi-no-Miya Shrine. ** {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, originally enshrined in the Kizuki Shrine. ** [[:en:Kamuo Ichihime|Ōichihime no Mikoto]], originally enshrined in the Ichihime Shrine. ** {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速須佐之男命|Susanoo-no-Mikoto=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Takehayasusanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, originally a guardian deity of Uozu Castle. ** {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売命|lt=Kushinadahime no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=}}, originally a guardian deity of Uozu Castle. ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}}, enshrined by Maeda Noritsuna of the Kaga Domain. == History == Originally named Shinmei Shrine, the site became Uozu Shrine in {{ill|1956|ja|昭和31年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1956|qq=}} following the integration of five shrines located in areas affected by the {{ill|Uozu Great Fire|ja|魚津大火|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu Great Fire|qq=}}. The new shrine building was completed on {{ill|June 1, 1965|ja|昭和40th year 6月1日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 1, 1965|qq=}}.『北日本新聞』1965th year 5月21日付朝刊12面（富山新川版）『来月1日から遷座祭 魚津神社が完成』より。『富山県神社誌』（1983rd year 11月10日、富山県神社庁発行）346頁。 In {{ill|1965|ja|昭和40年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1965|qq=}}, the Atago Shrine, formerly located on the grounds of Uozu Municipal Ōmachi Elementary School, was relocated to the north of the main shrine. In {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1968|qq=}}, a large torii gate (8.5 meters tall) was erected through a donation.『富山新聞』1968th year 9月18日付朝刊10面『魚津神社に大鳥居』より。 In {{ill|1970|ja|昭和45年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1970|qq=}}, a sumo ring was added to the grounds.『北日本新聞』1970th year 8月1日付朝刊（富山新川版）12面『魚津相撲場が完成』より。 == Shrine and Grounds == * Construction began: September 30, 1963 (groundbreaking ceremony held on September 24)『北日本新聞』1963rd year 9月20日付朝刊7面『二千三百万円で新築 7th year ぶりに解決の魚津神社』より。 * Completed: May 30, 1965 * Architect: Matsumoto Taisaku * Builder: Asano Nobuharu * Shrine grounds: 1,238 tsubo == Integrated Shrines == [[File:Atago-sha (Uozu)009.jpg|thumb|Atago Shrine]] * Shinmei Shrine (Shinmei-chō) – Established on March 17, 702 (Daibō 2). * Hi-no-Miya Shrine (Shimomuragi-chō) – Rebuilt during the Genki era. * Inari Shrine (Kanayachō) – Built during the Bunsei era. * Ichihime Shrine (Shinkanayachō) – Relics moved to Uozu Shrine in 1979. * Kizuki Shrine (Kōnoura-chō) – Established during the Bunsei era by the Uozu magistrate. == Annual Events == * January 26 – {{ill|Atago Fire Festival|ja|愛宕社の火祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Fire Festival|qq=}} * June 4–6 – Uozu Shrine Festival ** Locally known as "Shinmeihan's Festival," this event attracts numerous stalls along the central streets of Uozu. ** Historical children’s kabuki floats were once paraded, using traditional techniques and lacquerware craftsmanship. Some relics are preserved in the {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu History and Folklore Museum|qq=}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Matsuri float|Matsuri float]] * {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu History and Folklore Museum|qq=}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Atago Faith}} {{translated page|ja|魚津神社|version=103419258|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:History of Uozu City]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Uozu City]] [[Category:Atago Shrines]] [[sn:Uozu Shrine]]}} [[sn:Uozu Shrine]] [[ja:魚津神社]] [[sn:ozu Shrine]]

In {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1968|qq=}}, a large torii gate (8.5 meters tall) was erected through a donation.『富山新聞』1968th year 9月18日付朝刊10面『魚津神社に大鳥居』より。 In {{ill|1970|ja|昭和45年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1970|qq=}}, a sumo ring was added to the grounds.『北日本新聞』1970th year 8月1日付朝刊（富山新川版）12面『魚津相撲場が完成』より。 == Shrine and Grounds == * Construction began: September 30, 1963 (groundbreaking ceremony held on September 24)『北日本新聞』1963rd year 9月20日付朝刊7面『二千三百万円で新築 7th year ぶりに解決の魚津神社』より。 * Completed: May 30, 1965 * Architect: Matsumoto Taisaku * Builder: Asano Nobuharu * Shrine grounds: 1,238 tsubo == Integrated Shrines == [[File:Atago-sha (Uozu)009.jpg|thumb|Atago Shrine]] * Shinmei Shrine (Shinmei-chō) – Established on March 17, 702 (Daibō 2). * Hi-no-Miya Shrine (Shimomuragi-chō) – Rebuilt during the Genki era. * Inari Shrine (Kanayachō) – Built during the Bunsei era. * Ichihime Shrine (Shinkanayachō) – Relics moved to Uozu Shrine in 1979. * Kizuki Shrine (Kōnoura-chō) – Established during the Bunsei era by the Uozu magistrate. == Annual Events == * January 26 – {{ill|Atago Fire Festival|ja|愛宕社の火祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Fire Festival|qq=}} * June 4–6 – Uozu Shrine Festival ** Locally known as "Shinmeihan's Festival," this event attracts numerous stalls along the central streets of Uozu. ** Historical children’s kabuki floats were once paraded, using traditional techniques and lacquerware craftsmanship. Some relics are preserved in the {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu History and Folklore Museum|qq=}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Matsuri float|Matsuri float]] * {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu History and Folklore Museum|qq=}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Atago Faith}} {{translated page|ja|魚津神社|version=103419258|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:History of Uozu City]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Uozu City]] [[Category:Atago Shrines]] [[sn:Uozu Shrine]]}} [[sn:Uozu Shrine]] [[ja:魚津神社]] [[sn:ozu Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:魚津市の建築物]] [[Category:魚津市の歴史]]

In {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1968|qq=}}, a large torii gate (8.5 meters tall) was erected through a donation.『富山新聞』1968th year 9月18日付朝刊10面『魚津神社に大鳥居』より。 In {{ill|1970|ja|昭和45年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1970|qq=}}, a sumo ring was added to the grounds.『北日本新聞』1970th year 8月1日付朝刊（富山新川版）12面『魚津相撲場が完成』より。 == Shrine and Grounds == * Construction began: September 30, 1963 (groundbreaking ceremony held on September 24)『北日本新聞』1963rd year 9月20日付朝刊7面『二千三百万円で新築 7th year ぶりに解決の魚津神社』より。 * Completed: May 30, 1965 * Architect: Matsumoto Taisaku * Builder: Asano Nobuharu * Shrine grounds: 1,238 tsubo == Integrated Shrines == [[File:Atago-sha (Uozu)009.jpg|thumb|Atago Shrine]] * Shinmei Shrine (Shinmei-chō) – Established on March 17, 702 (Daibō 2). * Hi-no-Miya Shrine (Shimomuragi-chō) – Rebuilt during the Genki era. * Inari Shrine (Kanayachō) – Built during the Bunsei era. * Ichihime Shrine (Shinkanayachō) – Relics moved to Uozu Shrine in 1979. * Kizuki Shrine (Kōnoura-chō) – Established during the Bunsei era by the Uozu magistrate. == Annual Events == * January 26 – {{ill|Atago Fire Festival|ja|愛宕社の火祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Fire Festival|qq=}} * June 4–6 – Uozu Shrine Festival ** Locally known as "Shinmeihan's Festival," this event attracts numerous stalls along the central streets of Uozu. ** Historical children’s kabuki floats were once paraded, using traditional techniques and lacquerware craftsmanship. Some relics are preserved in the {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu History and Folklore Museum|qq=}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Matsuri float|Matsuri float]] * {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu History and Folklore Museum|qq=}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Atago Faith}} {{translated page|ja|魚津神社|version=103419258|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:History of Uozu City]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Uozu City]] [[Category:Atago Shrines]] [[sn:Uozu Shrine]]}} [[sn:Uozu Shrine]] [[ja:魚津神社]] [[sn:ozu Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Uozu, Toyama]] [[Category:History of Uozu, Toyama]]

In {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1968|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1968年}}, a large torii gate (8.5 meters tall) was erected through a donation.『富山新聞』1968th year 9月18日付朝刊10面『魚津神社に大鳥居』より。 In {{ill|1970|ja|昭和45年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1970|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1970年}}, a sumo ring was added to the grounds.『北日本新聞』1970th year 8月1日付朝刊（富山新川版）12面『魚津相撲場が完成』より。 == Shrine and Grounds == * Construction began: September 30, 1963 (groundbreaking ceremony held on September 24)『北日本新聞』1963rd year 9月20日付朝刊7面『二千三百万円で新築 7th year ぶりに解決の魚津神社』より。 * Completed: May 30, 1965 * Architect: Matsumoto Taisaku * Builder: Asano Nobuharu * Shrine grounds: 1,238 tsubo == Integrated Shrines == [[File:Atago-sha (Uozu)009.jpg|thumb|Atago Shrine]] * Shinmei Shrine (Shinmei-chō) – Established on March 17, 702 (Daibō 2). * Hi-no-Miya Shrine (Shimomuragi-chō) – Rebuilt during the Genki era. * Inari Shrine (Kanayachō) – Built during the Bunsei era. * Ichihime Shrine (Shinkanayachō) – Relics moved to Uozu Shrine in 1979. * Kizuki Shrine (Kōnoura-chō) – Established during the Bunsei era by the Uozu magistrate. == Annual Events == * January 26 – {{ill|Atago Fire Festival|ja|愛宕社の火祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Fire Festival|qq=}} * June 4–6 – Uozu Shrine Festival ** Locally known as "Shinmeihan's Festival," this event attracts numerous stalls along the central streets of Uozu. ** Historical children’s kabuki floats were once paraded, using traditional techniques and lacquerware craftsmanship. Some relics are preserved in the {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu History and Folklore Museum|qq=}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Matsuri float|Matsuri float]] * {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu History and Folklore Museum|qq=}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Atago Faith}} {{translated page|ja|魚津神社|version=103419258|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:History of Uozu City]] [[sn:Uozu Shrine]]}} [[sn:Uozu Shrine]] [[ja:魚津神社]] [[sn:ozu Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Uozu, Toyama]] [[Category:History of Uozu, Toyama]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Uozu Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Uozu-Jinja006.jpg|250px]] |location= [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]] [[:en:Uozu, Toyama|Uozu]] Chūō-dōri 1-3-28『富山県神社誌』（1983rd year 11月10日、富山県神社庁発行）346ページ。 |coordinates= {{coord|36|49|05.6|N|137|23|51.5|E|scale:25000}} |Main deity= |Shrine Rank= |Established= |Main hall= |Other names= |festival= June 4 |Rituals= Uozu Jinja Festival (June 4–6)
Atago Fire Festival (January 26) }} '''Uozu Shrine''' (魚津神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Uozu, Toyama|Uozu]], [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]]. == Deities == * Main deity: [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], originally enshrined in the Shinmei Shrine. * Enshrined deities: ** {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ}}, originally enshrined in the Shinmei Shrine. ** [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukano Mitama no Mikoto]], originally enshrined in the Inari Shrine. ** {{ill|Hoori|lt=Hoori|lt=Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|ja|彦火火出見命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}}, originally enshrined in the Hi-no-Miya Shrine. ** {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, originally enshrined in the Kizuki Shrine. ** [[:en:Kamuo Ichihime|Ōichihime no Mikoto]], originally enshrined in the Ichihime Shrine. ** {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速須佐之男命|Susanoo-no-Mikoto=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Takehayasusanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, originally a guardian deity of Uozu Castle. ** {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売命|lt=Kushinadahime no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ}}, originally a guardian deity of Uozu Castle. ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, enshrined by Maeda Noritsuna of the Kaga Domain. == History == Originally named Shinmei Shrine, the site became Uozu Shrine in {{ill|1956|ja|昭和31年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1956|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1956年}} following the integration of five shrines located in areas affected by the {{ill|Uozu Great Fire|ja|魚津大火|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu Great Fire|qq=}}. The new shrine building was completed on {{ill|June 1, 1965|ja|昭和40th year 6月1日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 1, 1965|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.『北日本新聞』1965th year 5月21日付朝刊12面（富山新川版）『来月1日から遷座祭 魚津神社が完成』より。『富山県神社誌』（1983rd year 11月10日、富山県神社庁発行）346頁。 In {{ill|1965|ja|昭和40年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1965|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1965年}}, the Atago Shrine, formerly located on the grounds of Uozu Municipal Ōmachi Elementary School, was relocated to the north of the main shrine. In {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1968|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1968年}}, a large torii gate (8.5 meters tall) was erected through a donation.『富山新聞』1968th year 9月18日付朝刊10面『魚津神社に大鳥居』より。 In {{ill|1970|ja|昭和45年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1970|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1970年}}, a sumo ring was added to the grounds.『北日本新聞』1970th year 8月1日付朝刊（富山新川版）12面『魚津相撲場が完成』より。 == Shrine and Grounds == * Construction began: September 30, 1963 (groundbreaking ceremony held on September 24)『北日本新聞』1963rd year 9月20日付朝刊7面『二千三百万円で新築 7th year ぶりに解決の魚津神社』より。 * Completed: May 30, 1965 * Architect: Matsumoto Taisaku * Builder: Asano Nobuharu * Shrine grounds: 1,238 tsubo == Integrated Shrines == [[File:Atago-sha (Uozu)009.jpg|thumb|Atago Shrine]] * Shinmei Shrine (Shinmei-chō) – Established on March 17, 702 (Daibō 2). * Hi-no-Miya Shrine (Shimomuragi-chō) – Rebuilt during the Genki era. * Inari Shrine (Kanayachō) – Built during the Bunsei era. * Ichihime Shrine (Shinkanayachō) – Relics moved to Uozu Shrine in 1979. * Kizuki Shrine (Kōnoura-chō) – Established during the Bunsei era by the Uozu magistrate. == Annual Events == * January 26 – {{ill|Atago Fire Festival|ja|愛宕社の火祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Fire Festival|qq=}} * June 4–6 – Uozu Shrine Festival ** Locally known as "Shinmeihan's Festival," this event attracts numerous stalls along the central streets of Uozu. ** Historical children’s kabuki floats were once paraded, using traditional techniques and lacquerware craftsmanship. Some relics are preserved in the {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu History and Folklore Museum|qq=}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Matsuri float|Matsuri float]] * {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu History and Folklore Museum|qq=}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Atago Faith}} {{translated page|ja|魚津神社|version=103419258|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[sn:Uozu Shrine]]}} [[sn:Uozu Shrine]] [[ja:魚津神社]] [[sn:ozu Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Uozu, Toyama]] [[Category:History of Uozu, Toyama]]

Some relics are preserved in the {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu History and Folklore Museum|qq=}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Matsuri float|Matsuri float]] * {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu History and Folklore Museum|qq=}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Atago Faith}} {{translated page|ja|魚津神社|version=103419258|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[sn:Uozu Shrine]]}} [[sn:Uozu Shrine]] [[ja:魚津神社]] [[sn:ozu Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Uozu, Toyama]] [[Category:History of Uozu, Toyama]]

Atago Fire Festival (January 26) }} '''Uozu Shrine''' (魚津神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Uozu, Toyama|Uozu]], [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]]. == Deities == * Main deity: [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], originally enshrined in the Shinmei Shrine. * Enshrined deities: ** {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ}}, originally enshrined in the Shinmei Shrine. ** [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukano Mitama no Mikoto]], originally enshrined in the Inari Shrine. ** {{ill|Hoori|lt=Hoori|lt=Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|ja|彦火火出見命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}}, originally enshrined in the Hi-no-Miya Shrine. ** {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, originally enshrined in the Kizuki Shrine. ** [[:en:Kamuo Ichihime|Ōichihime no Mikoto]], originally enshrined in the Ichihime Shrine. ** {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速須佐之男命|Susanoo-no-Mikoto=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Takehayasusanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, originally a guardian deity of Uozu Castle. ** {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売命|lt=Kushinadahime no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ}}, originally a guardian deity of Uozu Castle. ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, enshrined by Maeda Noritsuna of the Kaga Domain. == History == Originally named Shinmei Shrine, the site became Uozu Shrine in [[:en:1956|1956]] following the integration of five shrines located in areas affected by the {{ill|Uozu Great Fire|ja|魚津大火|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu Great Fire|qq=}}. The new shrine building was completed on {{ill|June 1, 1965|ja|昭和40th year 6月1日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 1, 1965|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.『北日本新聞』1965th year 5月21日付朝刊12面（富山新川版）『来月1日から遷座祭 魚津神社が完成』より。『富山県神社誌』（1983rd year 11月10日、富山県神社庁発行）346頁。 In [[:en:1965|1965]], the Atago Shrine, formerly located on the grounds of Uozu Municipal Ōmachi Elementary School, was relocated to the north of the main shrine. In {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1968|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1968年}}, a large torii gate (8.5 meters tall) was erected through a donation.『富山新聞』1968th year 9月18日付朝刊10面『魚津神社に大鳥居』より。 In {{ill|1970|ja|昭和45年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1970|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1970年}}, a sumo ring was added to the grounds.『北日本新聞』1970th year 8月1日付朝刊（富山新川版）12面『魚津相撲場が完成』より。 == Shrine and Grounds == * Construction began: September 30, 1963 (groundbreaking ceremony held on September 24)『北日本新聞』1963rd year 9月20日付朝刊7面『二千三百万円で新築 7th year ぶりに解決の魚津神社』より。 * Completed: May 30, 1965 * Architect: Matsumoto Taisaku * Builder: Asano Nobuharu * Shrine grounds: 1,238 tsubo == Integrated Shrines == [[File:Atago-sha (Uozu)009.jpg|thumb|Atago Shrine]] * Shinmei Shrine (Shinmei-chō) – Established on March 17, 702 (Daibō 2). * Hi-no-Miya Shrine (Shimomuragi-chō) – Rebuilt during the Genki era. * Inari Shrine (Kanayachō) – Built during the Bunsei era. * Ichihime Shrine (Shinkanayachō) – Relics moved to Uozu Shrine in 1979. * Kizuki Shrine (Kōnoura-chō) – Established during the Bunsei era by the Uozu magistrate. == Annual Events == * January 26 – {{ill|Atago Fire Festival|ja|愛宕社の火祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Fire Festival|qq=}} * June 4–6 – Uozu Shrine Festival ** Locally known as "Shinmeihan's Festival," this event attracts numerous stalls along the central streets of Uozu. ** Historical children’s kabuki floats were once paraded, using traditional techniques and lacquerware craftsmanship. Some relics are preserved in the {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu History and Folklore Museum|qq=}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Matsuri float|Matsuri float]] * {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu History and Folklore Museum|qq=}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Atago Faith}} {{translated page|ja|魚津神社|version=103419258|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[sn:Uozu Shrine]]}} [[sn:Uozu Shrine]] [[ja:魚津神社]] [[sn:ozu Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Uozu, Toyama]] [[Category:History of Uozu, Toyama]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11674049]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Uozu Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Uozu-Jinja006.jpg|250px]] |location= [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]] [[:en:Uozu, Toyama|Uozu]] Chūō-dōri 1-3-28『富山県神社誌』（1983rd year 11月10日、富山県神社庁発行）346ページ。 |coordinates= {{coord|36|49|05.6|N|137|23|51.5|E|scale:25000}} |Main deity= |Shrine Rank= |Established= |Main hall= |Other names= |festival= June 4 |Rituals= Uozu Jinja Festival (June 4–6)
Atago Fire Festival (January 26) }} '''Uozu Shrine''' (魚津神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Uozu, Toyama|Uozu]], [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]]. == Deities == * Main deity: [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], originally enshrined in the Shinmei Shrine. * Enshrined deities: ** {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ}}, originally enshrined in the Shinmei Shrine. ** [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukano Mitama no Mikoto]], originally enshrined in the Inari Shrine. ** {{ill|Hoori|lt=Hoori|lt=Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|ja|彦火火出見命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}}, originally enshrined in the Hi-no-Miya Shrine. ** {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, originally enshrined in the Kizuki Shrine. ** [[:en:Kamuo Ichihime|Ōichihime no Mikoto]], originally enshrined in the Ichihime Shrine. ** {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速須佐之男命|Susanoo-no-Mikoto=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Takehayasusanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, originally a guardian deity of Uozu Castle. ** {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売命|lt=Kushinadahime no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ}}, originally a guardian deity of Uozu Castle. ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, enshrined by Maeda Noritsuna of the Kaga Domain. == History == Originally named Shinmei Shrine, the site became Uozu Shrine in [[:en:1956|1956]] following the integration of five shrines located in areas affected by the {{ill|Uozu Great Fire|ja|魚津大火|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu Great Fire|qq=}}. The new shrine building was completed on {{ill|June 1, 1965|ja|昭和40th year 6月1日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 1, 1965|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.『北日本新聞』1965th year 5月21日付朝刊12面（富山新川版）『来月1日から遷座祭 魚津神社が完成』より。『富山県神社誌』（1983rd year 11月10日、富山県神社庁発行）346頁。 In [[:en:1965|1965]], the Atago Shrine, formerly located on the grounds of Uozu Municipal Ōmachi Elementary School, was relocated to the north of the main shrine. In [[:en:1968|1968]], a large torii gate (8.5 meters tall) was erected through a donation.『富山新聞』1968th year 9月18日付朝刊10面『魚津神社に大鳥居』より。 In [[:en:1970|1970]], a sumo ring was added to the grounds.『北日本新聞』1970th year 8月1日付朝刊（富山新川版）12面『魚津相撲場が完成』より。 == Shrine and Grounds == * Construction began: September 30, 1963 (groundbreaking ceremony held on September 24)『北日本新聞』1963rd year 9月20日付朝刊7面『二千三百万円で新築 7th year ぶりに解決の魚津神社』より。 * Completed: May 30, 1965 * Architect: Matsumoto Taisaku * Builder: Asano Nobuharu * Shrine grounds: 1,238 tsubo == Integrated Shrines == [[File:Atago-sha (Uozu)009.jpg|thumb|Atago Shrine]] * Shinmei Shrine (Shinmei-chō) – Established on March 17, 702 (Daibō 2). * Hi-no-Miya Shrine (Shimomuragi-chō) – Rebuilt during the Genki era. * Inari Shrine (Kanayachō) – Built during the Bunsei era. * Ichihime Shrine (Shinkanayachō) – Relics moved to Uozu Shrine in 1979. * Kizuki Shrine (Kōnoura-chō) – Established during the Bunsei era by the Uozu magistrate. == Annual Events == * January 26 – {{ill|Atago Fire Festival|ja|愛宕社の火祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Fire Festival|qq=}} * June 4–6 – Uozu Shrine Festival ** Locally known as "Shinmeihan's Festival," this event attracts numerous stalls along the central streets of Uozu. ** Historical children’s kabuki floats were once paraded, using traditional techniques and lacquerware craftsmanship. Some relics are preserved in the {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu History and Folklore Museum|qq=}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Matsuri float|Matsuri float]] * {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu History and Folklore Museum|qq=}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Atago Faith}} {{translated page|ja|魚津神社|version=103419258|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[sn:Uozu Shrine]]}} [[sn:Uozu Shrine]] [[ja:魚津神社]] [[sn:ozu Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Uozu, Toyama]] [[Category:History of Uozu, Toyama]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

following the integration of five shrines located in areas affected by the {{ill|Uozu Great Fire|ja|魚津大火|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu Great Fire|qq=|1=Great Fire of Uozu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674015}}. The new shrine building was completed on {{ill|June 1, 1965|ja|昭和40th year 6月1日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 1, 1965|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.『北日本新聞』1965th year 5月21日付朝刊12面（富山新川版）『来月1日から遷座祭 魚津神社が完成』より。『富山県神社誌』（1983rd year 11月10日、富山県神社庁発行）346頁。 In [[:en:1965|1965]], the Atago Shrine, formerly located on the grounds of Uozu Municipal Ōmachi Elementary School, was relocated to the north of the main shrine. In [[:en:1968|1968]], a large torii gate (8.5 meters tall) was erected through a donation.『富山新聞』1968th year 9月18日付朝刊10面『魚津神社に大鳥居』より。 In [[:en:1970|1970]], a sumo ring was added to the grounds.『北日本新聞』1970th year 8月1日付朝刊（富山新川版）12面『魚津相撲場が完成』より。 == Shrine and Grounds == * Construction began: September 30, 1963 (groundbreaking ceremony held on September 24)『北日本新聞』1963rd year 9月20日付朝刊7面『二千三百万円で新築 7th year ぶりに解決の魚津神社』より。 * Completed: May 30, 1965 * Architect: Matsumoto Taisaku * Builder: Asano Nobuharu * Shrine grounds: 1,238 tsubo == Integrated Shrines == [[File:Atago-sha (Uozu)009.jpg|thumb|Atago Shrine]] * Shinmei Shrine (Shinmei-chō) – Established on March 17, 702 (Daibō 2). * Hi-no-Miya Shrine (Shimomuragi-chō) – Rebuilt during the Genki era. * Inari Shrine (Kanayachō) – Built during the Bunsei era. * Ichihime Shrine (Shinkanayachō) – Relics moved to Uozu Shrine in 1979. * Kizuki Shrine (Kōnoura-chō) – Established during the Bunsei era by the Uozu magistrate. == Annual Events == * January 26 – {{ill|Atago Fire Festival|ja|愛宕社の火祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Fire Festival|qq=}} * June 4–6 – Uozu Shrine Festival ** Locally known as "Shinmeihan's Festival," this event attracts numerous stalls along the central streets of Uozu. ** Historical children’s kabuki floats were once paraded, using traditional techniques and lacquerware craftsmanship. Some relics are preserved in the {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu History and Folklore Museum|qq=|1=Uozu Museum of History and Folklore|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674043}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Matsuri float|Matsuri float]] * {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu History and Folklore Museum|qq=|1=Uozu Museum of History and Folklore|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674043}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Atago Faith}} {{translated page|ja|魚津神社|version=103419258|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[sn:Uozu Shrine]]}} [[sn:Uozu Shrine]] [[ja:魚津神社]] [[sn:ozu Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Uozu, Toyama]] [[Category:History of Uozu, Toyama]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

Atago Fire Festival (January 26) |prefecture= {{ill|Uozu|qid=Q819613}} }} '''Uozu Shrine''' (魚津神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Uozu, Toyama|Uozu]], [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]]. == Deities == * Main deity: [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], originally enshrined in the Shinmei Shrine. * Enshrined deities: ** {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ|1=Takeminakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9150391}}, originally enshrined in the Shinmei Shrine. ** [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukano Mitama no Mikoto]], originally enshrined in the Inari Shrine. ** {{ill|Hoori|lt=Hoori|lt=Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|ja|彦火火出見命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}}, originally enshrined in the Hi-no-Miya Shrine. ** {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, originally enshrined in the Kizuki Shrine. ** [[:en:Kamuo Ichihime|Ōichihime no Mikoto]], originally enshrined in the Ichihime Shrine. ** {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速須佐之男命|Susanoo-no-Mikoto=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Takehayasusanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, originally a guardian deity of Uozu Castle. ** {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売命|lt=Kushinadahime no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ}}, originally a guardian deity of Uozu Castle. ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, enshrined by Maeda Noritsuna of the Kaga Domain. == History == Originally named Shinmei Shrine, the site became Uozu Shrine in [[:en:1956|1956]] following the integration of five shrines located in areas affected by the {{ill|Uozu Great Fire|ja|魚津大火|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu Great Fire|qq=|1=Great Fire of Uozu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674015}}. The new shrine building was completed on {{ill|June 1, 1965|ja|昭和40th year 6月1日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 1, 1965|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.『北日本新聞』1965th year 5月21日付朝刊12面（富山新川版）『来月1日から遷座祭 魚津神社が完成』より。『富山県神社誌』（1983rd year 11月10日、富山県神社庁発行）346頁。 In [[:en:1965|1965]], the Atago Shrine, formerly located on the grounds of Uozu Municipal Ōmachi Elementary School, was relocated to the north of the main shrine. In [[:en:1968|1968]], a large torii gate (8.5 meters tall) was erected through a donation.『富山新聞』1968th year 9月18日付朝刊10面『魚津神社に大鳥居』より。 In [[:en:1970|1970]], a sumo ring was added to the grounds.『北日本新聞』1970th year 8月1日付朝刊（富山新川版）12面『魚津相撲場が完成』より。 == Shrine and Grounds == * Construction began: September 30, 1963 (groundbreaking ceremony held on September 24)『北日本新聞』1963rd year 9月20日付朝刊7面『二千三百万円で新築 7th year ぶりに解決の魚津神社』より。 * Completed: May 30, 1965 * Architect: Matsumoto Taisaku * Builder: Asano Nobuharu * Shrine grounds: 1,238 tsubo == Integrated Shrines == [[File:Atago-sha (Uozu)009.jpg|thumb|Atago Shrine]] * Shinmei Shrine (Shinmei-chō) – Established on March 17, 702 (Daibō 2). * Hi-no-Miya Shrine (Shimomuragi-chō) – Rebuilt during the Genki era. * Inari Shrine (Kanayachō) – Built during the Bunsei era. * Ichihime Shrine (Shinkanayachō) – Relics moved to Uozu Shrine in 1979. * Kizuki Shrine (Kōnoura-chō) – Established during the Bunsei era by the Uozu magistrate. == Annual Events == * January 26 – {{ill|Atago Fire Festival|ja|愛宕社の火祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Fire Festival|qq=}} * June 4–6 – Uozu Shrine Festival ** Locally known as "Shinmeihan's Festival," this event attracts numerous stalls along the central streets of Uozu. ** Historical children’s kabuki floats were once paraded, using traditional techniques and lacquerware craftsmanship. Some relics are preserved in the {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu History and Folklore Museum|qq=|1=Uozu Museum of History and Folklore|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674043}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Matsuri float|Matsuri float]] * {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu History and Folklore Museum|qq=|1=Uozu Museum of History and Folklore|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674043}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Atago Faith}} {{translated page|ja|魚津神社|version=103419258|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[sn:Uozu Shrine]]}} [[sn:Uozu Shrine]] [[ja:魚津神社]] [[sn:ozu Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Uozu, Toyama]] [[Category:History of Uozu, Toyama]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

Fire|ja|魚津大火|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu Great Fire|qq=|1=Great Fire of Uozu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674015}}. The new shrine building was completed on {{ill|June 1, 1965|ja|昭和40th year 6月1日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 1, 1965|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.『北日本新聞』1965th year 5月21日付朝刊12面（富山新川版）『来月1日から遷座祭 魚津神社が完成』より。『富山県神社誌』（1983rd year 11月10日、富山県神社庁発行）346頁。 In [[:en:1965|1965]], the Atago Shrine, formerly located on the grounds of Uozu Municipal Ōmachi Elementary School, was relocated to the north of the main shrine. In [[:en:1968|1968]], a large torii gate (8.5 meters tall) was erected through a donation.『富山新聞』1968th year 9月18日付朝刊10面『魚津神社に大鳥居』より。 In [[:en:1970|1970]], a sumo ring was added to the grounds.『北日本新聞』1970th year 8月1日付朝刊（富山新川版）12面『魚津相撲場が完成』より。 == Shrine and Grounds == * Construction began: September 30, 1963 (groundbreaking ceremony held on September 24)『北日本新聞』1963rd year 9月20日付朝刊7面『二千三百万円で新築 7th year ぶりに解決の魚津神社』より。 * Completed: May 30, 1965 * Architect: Matsumoto Taisaku * Builder: Asano Nobuharu * Shrine grounds: 1,238 tsubo == Integrated Shrines == [[File:Atago-sha (Uozu)009.jpg|thumb|Atago Shrine]] * Shinmei Shrine (Shinmei-chō) – Established on March 17, 702 (Daibō 2). * Hi-no-Miya Shrine (Shimomuragi-chō) – Rebuilt during the Genki era. * Inari Shrine (Kanayachō) – Built during the Bunsei era. * Ichihime Shrine (Shinkanayachō) – Relics moved to Uozu Shrine in 1979. * Kizuki Shrine (Kōnoura-chō) – Established during the Bunsei era by the Uozu magistrate. == Annual Events == * January 26 – {{ill|Atago Fire Festival|ja|愛宕社の火祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Fire Festival|qq=}} * June 4–6 – Uozu Shrine Festival ** Locally known as "Shinmeihan's Festival," this event attracts numerous stalls along the central streets of Uozu. ** Historical children’s kabuki floats were once paraded, using traditional techniques and lacquerware craftsmanship. Some relics are preserved in the {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu History and Folklore Museum|qq=|1=Uozu Museum of History and Folklore|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674043}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Matsuri float|Matsuri float]] * {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu History and Folklore Museum|qq=|1=Uozu Museum of History and Folklore|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674043}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Atago Faith}} {{translated page|ja|魚津神社|version=103419258|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[sn:Uozu Shrine]]}} [[sn:Uozu Shrine]] [[ja:魚津神社]] [[sn:ozu Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Uozu, Toyama]] [[Category:History of Uozu, Toyama]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

Atago Fire Festival (January 26) |prefecture= {{ill|Uozu|qid=Q819613}} }} '''Uozu Shrine''' (魚津神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Uozu, Toyama|Uozu]], [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]]. == Deities == * Main deity: [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], originally enshrined in the Shinmei Shrine. * Enshrined deities: ** {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ|1=Takeminakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9150391}}, originally enshrined in the Shinmei Shrine. ** [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukano Mitama no Mikoto]], originally enshrined in the Inari Shrine. ** {{ill|Hoori|lt=Hoori|lt=Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|ja|彦火火出見命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}}, originally enshrined in the Hi-no-Miya Shrine. ** {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, originally enshrined in the Kizuki Shrine. ** [[:en:Kamuo Ichihime|Ōichihime no Mikoto]], originally enshrined in the Ichihime Shrine. ** {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速須佐之男命|Susanoo-no-Mikoto=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Takehayasusanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, originally a guardian deity of Uozu Castle. ** {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売命|lt=Kushinadahime no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ}}, originally a guardian deity of Uozu Castle. ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, enshrined by Maeda Noritsuna of the Kaga Domain. == History == Originally named Shinmei Shrine, the site became Uozu Shrine in [[:en:1956|1956]] following the integration of five shrines located in areas affected by the {{ill|Uozu Great Fire|ja|魚津大火|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu Great Fire|qq=|1=Great Fire of Uozu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674015}}. The new shrine building was completed on {{ill|June 1, 1965|ja|昭和40th year 6月1日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 1, 1965|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.『北日本新聞』1965th year 5月21日付朝刊12面（富山新川版）『来月1日から遷座祭 魚津神社が完成』より。『富山県神社誌』（1983rd year 11月10日、富山県神社庁発行）346頁。 In [[:en:1965|1965]], the Atago Shrine, formerly located on the grounds of Uozu Municipal Ōmachi Elementary School, was relocated to the north of the main shrine. In [[:en:1968|1968]], a large torii gate (8.5 meters tall) was erected through a donation.『富山新聞』1968th year 9月18日付朝刊10面『魚津神社に大鳥居』より。 In [[:en:1970|1970]], a sumo ring was added to the grounds.『北日本新聞』1970th year 8月1日付朝刊（富山新川版）12面『魚津相撲場が完成』より。 == Shrine and Grounds == * Construction began: September 30, 1963 (groundbreaking ceremony held on September 24)『北日本新聞』1963rd year 9月20日付朝刊7面『二千三百万円で新築 7th year ぶりに解決の魚津神社』より。 * Completed: May 30, 1965 * Architect: Matsumoto Taisaku * Builder: Asano Nobuharu * Shrine grounds: 1,238 tsubo == Integrated Shrines == [[File:Atago-sha (Uozu)009.jpg|thumb|Atago Shrine]] * Shinmei Shrine (Shinmei-chō) – Established on March 17, 702 (Daibō 2). * Hi-no-Miya Shrine (Shimomuragi-chō) – Rebuilt during the Genki era. * Inari Shrine (Kanayachō) – Built during the Bunsei era. * Ichihime Shrine (Shinkanayachō) – Relics moved to Uozu Shrine in 1979. * Kizuki Shrine (Kōnoura-chō) – Established during the Bunsei era by the Uozu magistrate. == Annual Events == * January 26 – {{ill|Atago Fire Festival|ja|愛宕社の火祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Fire Festival|qq=}} * June 4–6 – Uozu Shrine Festival ** Locally known as "Shinmeihan's Festival," this event attracts numerous stalls along the central streets of Uozu. ** Historical children’s kabuki floats were once paraded, using traditional techniques and lacquerware craftsmanship. {{ill|Uozu Great Fire|ja|魚津大火|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu Great Fire|qq=|1=Great Fire of Uozu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674015}}. The new shrine building was completed on {{ill|June 1, 1965|ja|昭和40th year 6月1日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 1, 1965|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.『北日本新聞』1965th year 5月21日付朝刊12面（富山新川版）『来月1日から遷座祭 魚津神社が完成』より。『富山県神社誌』（1983rd year 11月10日、富山県神社庁発行）346頁。 In [[:en:1965|1965]], the Atago Shrine, formerly located on the grounds of Uozu Municipal Ōmachi Elementary School, was relocated to the north of the main shrine. In [[:en:1968|1968]], a large torii gate (8.5 meters tall) was erected through a donation.『富山新聞』1968th year 9月18日付朝刊10面『魚津神社に大鳥居』より。 In [[:en:1970|1970]], a sumo ring was added to the grounds.『北日本新聞』1970th year 8月1日付朝刊（富山新川版）12面『魚津相撲場が完成』より。 == Shrine and Grounds == * Construction began: September 30, 1963 (groundbreaking ceremony held on September 24)『北日本新聞』1963rd year 9月20日付朝刊7面『二千三百万円で新築 7th year ぶりに解決の魚津神社』より。 * Completed: May 30, 1965 * Architect: Matsumoto Taisaku * Builder: Asano Nobuharu * Shrine grounds: 1,238 tsubo == Integrated Shrines == [[File:Atago-sha (Uozu)009.jpg|thumb|Atago Shrine]] * Shinmei Shrine (Shinmei-chō) – Established on March 17, 702 (Daibō 2). * Hi-no-Miya Shrine (Shimomuragi-chō) – Rebuilt during the Genki era. * Inari Shrine (Kanayachō) – Built during the Bunsei era. * Ichihime Shrine (Shinkanayachō) – Relics moved to Uozu Shrine in 1979. * Kizuki Shrine (Kōnoura-chō) – Established during the Bunsei era by the Uozu magistrate. == Annual Events == * January 26 – {{ill|Atago Fire Festival|ja|愛宕社の火祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Fire Festival|qq=}} * June 4–6 – Uozu Shrine Festival ** Locally known as "Shinmeihan's Festival," this event attracts numerous stalls along the central streets of Uozu. ** Historical children’s kabuki floats were once paraded, using traditional techniques and lacquerware craftsmanship. Some relics are preserved in the {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu History and Folklore Museum|qq=|1=Uozu Museum of History and Folklore|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674043}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Matsuri float|Matsuri float]] * {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu History and Folklore Museum|qq=|1=Uozu Museum of History and Folklore|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674043}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Atago Faith}} {{translated page|ja|魚津神社|version=103419258|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] }} [[ja:魚津神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Uozu, Toyama]] [[Category:History of Uozu, Toyama]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] {{wikidata link|Q11674049}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11674049]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Uozu Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= File:Uozu-Jinja006.jpg |location= [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]] [[:en:Uozu, Toyama|Uozu]] Chūō-dōri 1-3-28『富山県神社誌』（1983rd year 11月10日、富山県神社庁発行）346ページ。 |coordinates= {{Coord|36.818222|N|137.397639|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Main deity= |Shrine Rank= |Established= |Main hall= |Other names= |festival= June 4 |Rituals= Uozu Jinja Festival (June 4–6)
Atago Fire Festival (January 26) |prefecture= {{ill|Uozu|qid=Q819613}} }} '''Uozu Shrine''' (魚津神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Uozu, Toyama|Uozu]], [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]]. == Deities == * Main deity: [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], originally enshrined in the Shinmei Shrine. * Enshrined deities: ** {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ|1=Takeminakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9150391}}, originally enshrined in the Shinmei Shrine. ** [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukano Mitama no Mikoto]], originally enshrined in the Inari Shrine. ** {{ill|Hoori|lt=Hoori|lt=Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|ja|彦火火出見命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}}, originally enshrined in the Hi-no-Miya Shrine. ** {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, originally enshrined in the Kizuki Shrine. ** [[:en:Kamuo Ichihime|Ōichihime no Mikoto]], originally enshrined in the Ichihime Shrine. ** {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速須佐之男命|Susanoo-no-Mikoto=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Takehayasusanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, originally a guardian deity of Uozu Castle. ** {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売命|lt=Kushinadahime no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ}}, originally a guardian deity of Uozu Castle. ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, enshrined by Maeda Noritsuna of the Kaga Domain. == History == Originally named Shinmei Shrine, the site became Uozu Shrine in [[:en:1956|1956]] following the integration of five shrines located in areas affected by the {{ill|Uozu Great Fire|ja|魚津大火|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu Great Fire|qq=|1=Great Fire of Uozu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674015}}. The new shrine building was completed on {{ill|June 1, 1965|ja|昭和40th year 6月1日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 1, 1965|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.『北日本新聞』1965th year 5月21日付朝刊12面（富山新川版）『来月1日から遷座祭 魚津神社が完成』より。『富山県神社誌』（1983rd year 11月10日、富山県神社庁発行）346頁。 In [[:en:1965|1965]], the Atago Shrine, formerly located on the grounds of Uozu Municipal Ōmachi Elementary School, was relocated to the north of the main shrine. In [[:en:1968|1968]], a large torii gate (8.5 meters tall) was erected through a donation.『富山新聞』1968th year 9月18日付朝刊10面『魚津神社に大鳥居』より。 In [[:en:1970|1970]], a sumo ring was added to the grounds.『北日本新聞』1970th year 8月1日付朝刊（富山新川版）12面『魚津相撲場が完成』より。 == Shrine and Grounds == * Construction began: September 30, 1963 (groundbreaking ceremony held on September 24)『北日本新聞』1963rd year 9月20日付朝刊7面『二千三百万円で新築 7th year ぶりに解決の魚津神社』より。 * Completed: May 30, 1965 * Architect: Matsumoto Taisaku * Builder: Asano Nobuharu * Shrine grounds: 1,238 tsubo == Integrated Shrines == [[File:Atago-sha (Uozu)009.jpg|thumb|Atago Shrine]] * Shinmei Shrine (Shinmei-chō) – Established on March 17, 702 (Daibō 2). * Hi-no-Miya Shrine (Shimomuragi-chō) – Rebuilt during the Genki era. * Inari Shrine (Kanayachō) – Built during the Bunsei era. * Ichihime Shrine (Shinkanayachō) – Relics moved to Uozu Shrine in 1979. * Kizuki Shrine (Kōnoura-chō) – Established during the Bunsei era by the Uozu magistrate. == Annual Events == * January 26 – {{ill|Atago Fire Festival|ja|愛宕社の火祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Fire Festival|qq=}} * June 4–6 – Uozu Shrine Festival ** Locally known as "Shinmeihan's Festival," this event attracts numerous stalls along the central streets of Uozu. ** Historical children’s kabuki floats were once paraded, using traditional techniques and lacquerware craftsmanship. Some relics are preserved in the {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu History and Folklore Museum|qq=|1=Uozu Museum of History and Folklore|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674043}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Matsuri float|Matsuri float]] * {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu History and Folklore Museum|qq=|1=Uozu Museum of History and Folklore|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674043}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Atago Faith}} {{translated page|ja|魚津神社|version=103419258|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] }} [[ja:魚津神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Uozu, Toyama]] [[Category:History of Uozu, Toyama]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:Qq 4]] {{wikidata link|Q11674049}}
