In [[:en:1968|1968]], a large torii gate (8.5 meters tall) was erected through a donation.『富山新聞』1968th year 9月18日付朝刊10面『魚津神社に大鳥居』より。 In [[:en:1970|1970]], a sumo ring was added to the grounds.『北日本新聞』1970th year 8月1日付朝刊（富山新川版）12面『魚津相撲場が完成』より。 == Shrine and Grounds == * Construction began: September 30, 1963 (groundbreaking ceremony held on September 24)『北日本新聞』1963rd year 9月20日付朝刊7面『二千三百万円で新築 7th year ぶりに解決の魚津神社』より。 * Completed: May 30, 1965 * Architect: Matsumoto Taisaku * Builder: Asano Nobuharu * Shrine grounds: 1,238 tsubo == Integrated Shrines == [[File:Atago-sha (Uozu)009.jpg|thumb|Atago Shrine]] * Shinmei Shrine (Shinmei-chō) – Established on March 17, 702 (Daibō 2). * Hi-no-Miya Shrine (Shimomuragi-chō) – Rebuilt during the Genki era. * Inari Shrine (Kanayachō) – Built during the Bunsei era. * Ichihime Shrine (Shinkanayachō) – Relics moved to Uozu Shrine in 1979. * Kizuki Shrine (Kōnoura-chō) – Established during the Bunsei era by the Uozu magistrate. == Annual Events == * January 26 – {{ill|Atago Fire Festival|ja|愛宕社の火祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Fire Festival|qq=}} * June 4–6 – Uozu Shrine Festival ** Locally known as "Shinmeihan's Festival," this event attracts numerous stalls along the central streets of Uozu. ** Historical children’s kabuki floats were once paraded, using traditional techniques and lacquerware craftsmanship. Some relics are preserved in the {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu History and Folklore Museum|qq=|1=Uozu Museum of History and Folklore|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674043}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Matsuri float|Matsuri float]] * {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu History and Folklore Museum|qq=|1=Uozu Museum of History and Folklore|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674043}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Atago Faith}} {{translated page|ja|魚津神社|version=103419258|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] }} [[ja:魚津神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Uozu, Toyama]] [[Category:History of Uozu, Toyama]] {{wikidata link|Q11674049}}

Some relics are preserved in the {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu History and Folklore Museum|1=Uozu Museum of History and Folklore|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674043}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Matsuri float|Matsuri float]] * {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu History and Folklore Museum|1=Uozu Museum of History and Folklore|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674043}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Atago Faith}} {{translated page|ja|魚津神社|version=103419258|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] }} [[ja:魚津神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Uozu, Toyama]] [[Category:History of Uozu, Toyama]] {{wikidata link|Q11674049}}

Atago Fire Festival (January 26) |prefecture= {{ill|Uozu|WD=Q819613}} }} '''Uozu Shrine''' (魚津神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Uozu, Toyama|Uozu]], [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]]. == Deities == * Main deity: [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], originally enshrined in the Shinmei Shrine. * Enshrined deities: ** {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ|1=Takeminakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9150391}}, originally enshrined in the Shinmei Shrine. ** [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukano Mitama no Mikoto]], originally enshrined in the Inari Shrine. ** {{ill|Hoori|lt=Hoori|lt=Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|ja|彦火火出見命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}}, originally enshrined in the Hi-no-Miya Shrine. ** {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, originally enshrined in the Kizuki Shrine. ** [[:en:Kamuo Ichihime|Ōichihime no Mikoto]], originally enshrined in the Ichihime Shrine. ** {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速須佐之男命|Susanoo-no-Mikoto=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Takehayasusanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, originally a guardian deity of Uozu Castle. ** {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売命|lt=Kushinadahime no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ}}, originally a guardian deity of Uozu Castle. ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, enshrined by Maeda Noritsuna of the Kaga Domain. == History == Originally named Shinmei Shrine, the site became Uozu Shrine in [[:en:1956|1956]] following the integration of five shrines located in areas affected by the {{ill|Uozu Great Fire|ja|魚津大火|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu Great Fire|1=Great Fire of Uozu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674015}}. The new shrine building was completed on {{ill|June 1, 1965|ja|昭和40th year 6月1日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 1, 1965|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.『北日本新聞』1965th year 5月21日付朝刊12面（富山新川版）『来月1日から遷座祭 魚津神社が完成』より。『富山県神社誌』（1983rd year 11月10日、富山県神社庁発行）346頁。 In [[:en:1965|1965]], the Atago Shrine, formerly located on the grounds of Uozu Municipal Ōmachi Elementary School, was relocated to the north of the main shrine. In [[:en:1968|1968]], a large torii gate (8.5 meters tall) was erected through a donation.『富山新聞』1968th year 9月18日付朝刊10面『魚津神社に大鳥居』より。 In [[:en:1970|1970]], a sumo ring was added to the grounds.『北日本新聞』1970th year 8月1日付朝刊（富山新川版）12面『魚津相撲場が完成』より。 == Shrine and Grounds == * Construction began: September 30, 1963 (groundbreaking ceremony held on September 24)『北日本新聞』1963rd year 9月20日付朝刊7面『二千三百万円で新築 7th year ぶりに解決の魚津神社』より。 * Completed: May 30, 1965 * Architect: Matsumoto Taisaku * Builder: Asano Nobuharu * Shrine grounds: 1,238 tsubo == Integrated Shrines == [[File:Atago-sha (Uozu)009.jpg|thumb|Atago Shrine]] * Shinmei Shrine (Shinmei-chō) – Established on March 17, 702 (Daibō 2). * Hi-no-Miya Shrine (Shimomuragi-chō) – Rebuilt during the Genki era. * Inari Shrine (Kanayachō) – Built during the Bunsei era. * Ichihime Shrine (Shinkanayachō) – Relics moved to Uozu Shrine in 1979. * Kizuki Shrine (Kōnoura-chō) – Established during the Bunsei era by the Uozu magistrate. == Annual Events == * January 26 – {{ill|Atago Fire Festival|ja|愛宕社の火祭り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Fire Festival|qq=}} * June 4–6 – Uozu Shrine Festival ** Locally known as "Shinmeihan's Festival," this event attracts numerous stalls along the central streets of Uozu. ** Historical children’s kabuki floats were once paraded, using traditional techniques and lacquerware craftsmanship. Some relics are preserved in the {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu History and Folklore Museum|1=Uozu Museum of History and Folklore|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674043}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Matsuri float|Matsuri float]] * {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu History and Folklore Museum|1=Uozu Museum of History and Folklore|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674043}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Atago Faith}} {{translated page|ja|魚津神社|version=103419258|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] }} [[ja:魚津神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Uozu, Toyama]] [[Category:History of Uozu, Toyama]] {{wikidata link|Q11674049}}

Some relics are preserved in the {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu History and Folklore Museum|1=Uozu Museum of History and Folklore|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674043}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Matsuri float|Matsuri float]] * {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu History and Folklore Museum|1=Uozu Museum of History and Folklore|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674043}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Atago Faith}} {{translated page|ja|魚津神社|version=103419258|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] }} [[ja:魚津神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Uozu, Toyama]] [[Category:History of Uozu, Toyama]] {{wikidata link|Q11674049}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[ja:魚津神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11674049]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Uozu Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= File:Uozu-Jinja006.jpg |location= [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]] [[:en:Uozu, Toyama|Uozu]] Chūō-dōri 1-3-28『富山県神社誌』（1983rd year 11月10日、富山県神社庁発行）346ページ。 |coordinates= {{Coord|36.818222|N|137.397639|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Main deity= |Shrine Rank= |Established= |Main hall= |Other names= |festival= June 4 |Rituals= Uozu Jinja Festival (June 4–6)
Atago Fire Festival (January 26) |prefecture= {{ill|Uozu|WD=Q819613}} }} '''Uozu Shrine''' (魚津神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Uozu, Toyama|Uozu]], [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]]. == Deities == * Main deity: [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], originally enshrined in the Shinmei Shrine. * Enshrined deities: ** {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ|1=Takeminakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9150391}}, originally enshrined in the Shinmei Shrine. ** [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukano Mitama no Mikoto]], originally enshrined in the Inari Shrine. ** {{ill|Hoori|lt=Hoori|lt=Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|ja|彦火火出見命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}}, originally enshrined in the Hi-no-Miya Shrine. ** {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, originally enshrined in the Kizuki Shrine. ** [[:en:Kamuo Ichihime|Ōichihime no Mikoto]], originally enshrined in the Ichihime Shrine. ** {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速須佐之男命|Susanoo-no-Mikoto=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Takehayasusanoo-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, originally a guardian deity of Uozu Castle. ** {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売命|lt=Kushinadahime no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ}}, originally a guardian deity of Uozu Castle. ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, enshrined by Maeda Noritsuna of the Kaga Domain. == History == Originally named Shinmei Shrine, the site became Uozu Shrine in [[:en:1956|1956]] following the integration of five shrines located in areas affected by the {{ill|Uozu Great Fire|ja|魚津大火|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu Great Fire|1=Great Fire of Uozu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674015}}. The new shrine building was completed on {{ill|June 1, 1965|ja|昭和40th year 6月1日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 1, 1965|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.『北日本新聞』1965th year 5月21日付朝刊12面（富山新川版）『来月1日から遷座祭 魚津神社が完成』より。『富山県神社誌』（1983rd year 11月10日、富山県神社庁発行）346頁。 In [[:en:1965|1965]], the Atago Shrine, formerly located on the grounds of Uozu Municipal Ōmachi Elementary School, was relocated to the north of the main shrine. In [[:en:1968|1968]], a large torii gate (8.5 meters tall) was erected through a donation.『富山新聞』1968th year 9月18日付朝刊10面『魚津神社に大鳥居』より。 In [[:en:1970|1970]], a sumo ring was added to the grounds.『北日本新聞』1970th year 8月1日付朝刊（富山新川版）12面『魚津相撲場が完成』より。 == Shrine and Grounds == * Construction began: September 30, 1963 (groundbreaking ceremony held on September 24)『北日本新聞』1963rd year 9月20日付朝刊7面『二千三百万円で新築 7th year ぶりに解決の魚津神社』より。 * Completed: May 30, 1965 * Architect: Matsumoto Taisaku * Builder: Asano Nobuharu * Shrine grounds: 1,238 tsubo == Integrated Shrines == [[File:Atago-sha (Uozu)009.jpg|thumb|Atago Shrine]] * Shinmei Shrine (Shinmei-chō) – Established on March 17, 702 (Daibō 2). * Hi-no-Miya Shrine (Shimomuragi-chō) – Rebuilt during the Genki era. * Inari Shrine (Kanayachō) – Built during the Bunsei era. * Ichihime Shrine (Shinkanayachō) – Relics moved to Uozu Shrine in 1979. * Kizuki Shrine (Kōnoura-chō) – Established during the Bunsei era by the Uozu magistrate. == Annual Events == * January 26 – {{ill|Atago Fire Festival|ja|愛宕社の火祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Fire Festival|qq=}} * June 4–6 – Uozu Shrine Festival ** Locally known as "Shinmeihan's Festival," this event attracts numerous stalls along the central streets of Uozu. ** Historical children’s kabuki floats were once paraded, using traditional techniques and lacquerware craftsmanship. Some relics are preserved in the {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu History and Folklore Museum|1=Uozu Museum of History and Folklore|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674043}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Matsuri float|Matsuri float]] * {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu History and Folklore Museum|1=Uozu Museum of History and Folklore|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674043}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Atago Faith}} {{translated page|ja|魚津神社|version=103419258|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] }} [[ja:魚津神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Uozu, Toyama]] [[Category:History of Uozu, Toyama]] {{wikidata link|Q11674049}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

Some relics are preserved in the {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu History and Folklore Museum|1=Uozu Museum of History and Folklore|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674043}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Matsuri float|Matsuri float]] * {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu History and Folklore Museum|1=Uozu Museum of History and Folklore|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674043}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Atago Faith}} {{translated page|ja|魚津神社|version=103419258|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] }} [[ja:魚津神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Uozu, Toyama]] [[Category:History of Uozu, Toyama]] {{wikidata link|Q11674049}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

Some relics are preserved in the {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu History and Folklore Museum|1=Uozu Museum of History and Folklore|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674043}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Matsuri float|Matsuri float]] * {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu History and Folklore Museum|1=Uozu Museum of History and Folklore|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674043}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Atago Faith}} {{translated page|ja|魚津神社|version=103419258|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] }} [[ja:魚津神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Uozu, Toyama]] [[Category:History of Uozu, Toyama]] {{wikidata link|Q11674049}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] {{wikidata link|Q11674049}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

Atago Fire Festival (January 26) |prefecture= {{ill|Uozu|WD=Q819613}} }} '''Uozu Shrine''' (魚津神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Uozu, Toyama|Uozu]], [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]]. == Deities == * Main deity: [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], originally enshrined in the Shinmei Shrine. * Enshrined deities: ** {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ|1=Takeminakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9150391}}, originally enshrined in the Shinmei Shrine. ** [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukano Mitama no Mikoto]], originally enshrined in the Inari Shrine. ** {{ill|Hoori|lt=Hoori|lt=Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|ja|彦火火出見命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}}, originally enshrined in the Hi-no-Miya Shrine. ** {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, originally enshrined in the Kizuki Shrine. ** [[:en:Kamuo Ichihime|Ōichihime no Mikoto]], originally enshrined in the Ichihime Shrine. ** {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速須佐之男命|Susanoo-no-Mikoto=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Takehayasusanoo-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, originally a guardian deity of Uozu Castle. ** {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売命|lt=Kushinadahime no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ}}, originally a guardian deity of Uozu Castle. ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, enshrined by Maeda Noritsuna of the Kaga Domain. == History == Originally named Shinmei Shrine, the site became Uozu Shrine in [[:en:1956|1956]] following the integration of five shrines located in areas affected by the {{ill|Uozu Great Fire|ja|魚津大火|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu Great Fire|1=Great Fire of Uozu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674015}}. The new shrine building was completed on {{ill|June 1, 1965|ja|昭和40th year 6月1日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 1, 1965|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.『北日本新聞』1965th year 5月21日付朝刊12面（富山新川版）『来月1日から遷座祭 魚津神社が完成』より。『富山県神社誌』（1983rd year 11月10日、富山県神社庁発行）346頁。 In [[:en:1965|1965]], the Atago Shrine, formerly located on the grounds of Uozu Municipal Ōmachi Elementary School, was relocated to the north of the main shrine. In [[:en:1968|1968]], a large torii gate (8.5 meters tall) was erected through a donation.『富山新聞』1968th year 9月18日付朝刊10面『魚津神社に大鳥居』より。 In [[:en:1970|1970]], a sumo ring was added to the grounds.『北日本新聞』1970th year 8月1日付朝刊（富山新川版）12面『魚津相撲場が完成』より。 == Shrine and Grounds == * Construction began: September 30, 1963 (groundbreaking ceremony held on September 24)『北日本新聞』1963rd year 9月20日付朝刊7面『二千三百万円で新築 7th year ぶりに解決の魚津神社』より。 * Completed: May 30, 1965 * Architect: Matsumoto Taisaku * Builder: Asano Nobuharu * Shrine grounds: 1,238 tsubo == Integrated Shrines == [[File:Atago-sha (Uozu)009.jpg|thumb|Atago Shrine]] * Shinmei Shrine (Shinmei-chō) – Established on March 17, 702 (Daibō 2). * Hi-no-Miya Shrine (Shimomuragi-chō) – Rebuilt during the Genki era. * Inari Shrine (Kanayachō) – Built during the Bunsei era. * Ichihime Shrine (Shinkanayachō) – Relics moved to Uozu Shrine in 1979. * Kizuki Shrine (Kōnoura-chō) – Established during the Bunsei era by the Uozu magistrate. == Annual Events == * January 26 – {{ill|Atago Fire Festival|ja|愛宕社の火祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Fire Festival|qq=}} * June 4–6 – Uozu Shrine Festival ** Locally known as "Shinmeihan's Festival," this event attracts numerous stalls along the central streets of Uozu. ** Historical children’s kabuki floats were once paraded, using traditional techniques and lacquerware craftsmanship. Some relics are preserved in the {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu History and Folklore Museum|1=Uozu Museum of History and Folklore|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674043}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Matsuri float|Matsuri float]] * {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu History and Folklore Museum|1=Uozu Museum of History and Folklore|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674043}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Atago Faith}} {{translated page|ja|魚津神社|version=103419258|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] }} [[ja:魚津神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Uozu, Toyama]] [[Category:History of Uozu, Toyama]] {{wikidata link|Q11674049}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] {{wikidata link|Q11674049}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]]

In [[:en:1968|1968]], a large torii gate (8.5 meters tall) was erected through a donation.『富山新聞』1968th year 9月18日付朝刊10面『魚津神社に大鳥居』より。 In [[:en:1970|1970]], a sumo ring was added to the grounds.『北日本新聞』1970th year 8月1日付朝刊（富山新川版）12面『魚津相撲場が完成』より。 == Shrine and Grounds == * Construction began: September 30, 1963 (groundbreaking ceremony held on September 24)『北日本新聞』1963rd year 9月20日付朝刊7面『二千三百万円で新築 7th year ぶりに解決の魚津神社』より。 * Completed: May 30, 1965 * Architect: Matsumoto Taisaku * Builder: Asano Nobuharu * Shrine grounds: 1,238 tsubo == Integrated Shrines == [[File:Atago-sha (Uozu)009.jpg|thumb|Atago Shrine]] * Shinmei Shrine (Shinmei-chō) – Established on March 17, 702 (Daibō 2). * Hi-no-Miya Shrine (Shimomuragi-chō) – Rebuilt during the Genki era. * Inari Shrine (Kanayachō) – Built during the Bunsei era. * Ichihime Shrine (Shinkanayachō) – Relics moved to Uozu Shrine in 1979. * Kizuki Shrine (Kōnoura-chō) – Established during the Bunsei era by the Uozu magistrate. == Annual Events == * January 26 – {{ill|Atago Fire Festival|ja|愛宕社の火祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Fire Festival|qq=}} * June 4–6 – Uozu Shrine Festival ** Locally known as "Shinmeihan's Festival," this event attracts numerous stalls along the central streets of Uozu. ** Historical children’s kabuki floats were once paraded, using traditional techniques and lacquerware craftsmanship. Some relics are preserved in the {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu History and Folklore Museum|1=Uozu Museum of History and Folklore|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674043}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Matsuri float|Matsuri float]] * {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu History and Folklore Museum|1=Uozu Museum of History and Folklore|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674043}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Atago Faith}} {{translated page|ja|魚津神社|version=103419258|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] }} [[ja:魚津神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Uozu, Toyama]] [[Category:History of Uozu, Toyama]] {{wikidata link|Q11674049}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] {{wikidata link|Q11674049}} [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]]

[[ja:魚津神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11674049]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Uozu Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= File:Uozu-Jinja006.jpg |location= [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]] [[:en:Uozu, Toyama|Uozu]] Chūō-dōri 1-3-28『富山県神社誌』（1983rd year 11月10日、富山県神社庁発行）346ページ。 |coordinates= {{Coord|36.818222|N|137.397639|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Main deity= |Shrine Rank= |Established= |Main hall= |Other names= |festival= June 4 |Rituals= Uozu Jinja Festival (June 4–6)
Atago Fire Festival (January 26) |prefecture= {{ill|Uozu|WD=Q819613}} }} '''Uozu Shrine''' (魚津神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Uozu, Toyama|Uozu]], [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]]. == Deities == * Main deity: [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], originally enshrined in the Shinmei Shrine. * Enshrined deities: ** {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ|1=Takeminakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9150391}}, originally enshrined in the Shinmei Shrine. ** [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukano Mitama no Mikoto]], originally enshrined in the Inari Shrine. ** {{ill|Hoori|lt=Hoori|lt=Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|ja|彦火火出見命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}}, originally enshrined in the Hi-no-Miya Shrine. ** {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, originally enshrined in the Kizuki Shrine. ** [[:en:Kamuo Ichihime|Ōichihime no Mikoto]], originally enshrined in the Ichihime Shrine. ** {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速須佐之男命|Susanoo-no-Mikoto=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Takehayasusanoo-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, originally a guardian deity of Uozu Castle. ** {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売命|lt=Kushinadahime no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ}}, originally a guardian deity of Uozu Castle. ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, enshrined by Maeda Noritsuna of the Kaga Domain. == History == Originally named Shinmei Shrine, the site became Uozu Shrine in [[:en:1956|1956]] following the integration of five shrines located in areas affected by the {{ill|Uozu Great Fire|ja|魚津大火|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu Great Fire|1=Great Fire of Uozu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674015}}. The new shrine building was completed on {{ill|June 1, 1965|ja|昭和40th year 6月1日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 1, 1965|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.『北日本新聞』1965th year 5月21日付朝刊12面（富山新川版）『来月1日から遷座祭 魚津神社が完成』より。『富山県神社誌』（1983rd year 11月10日、富山県神社庁発行）346頁。 In [[:en:1965|1965]], the Atago Shrine, formerly located on the grounds of Uozu Municipal Ōmachi Elementary School, was relocated to the north of the main shrine. In [[:en:1968|1968]], a large torii gate (8.5 meters tall) was erected through a donation.『富山新聞』1968th year 9月18日付朝刊10面『魚津神社に大鳥居』より。 In [[:en:1970|1970]], a sumo ring was added to the grounds.『北日本新聞』1970th year 8月1日付朝刊（富山新川版）12面『魚津相撲場が完成』より。 == Shrine and Grounds == * Construction began: September 30, 1963 (groundbreaking ceremony held on September 24)『北日本新聞』1963rd year 9月20日付朝刊7面『二千三百万円で新築 7th year ぶりに解決の魚津神社』より。 * Completed: May 30, 1965 * Architect: Matsumoto Taisaku * Builder: Asano Nobuharu * Shrine grounds: 1,238 tsubo == Integrated Shrines == [[File:Atago-sha (Uozu)009.jpg|thumb|Atago Shrine]] * Shinmei Shrine (Shinmei-chō) – Established on March 17, 702 (Daibō 2). * Hi-no-Miya Shrine (Shimomuragi-chō) – Rebuilt during the Genki era. * Inari Shrine (Kanayachō) – Built during the Bunsei era. * Ichihime Shrine (Shinkanayachō) – Relics moved to Uozu Shrine in 1979. * Kizuki Shrine (Kōnoura-chō) – Established during the Bunsei era by the Uozu magistrate. == Annual Events == * January 26 – {{ill|Atago Fire Festival|ja|愛宕社の火祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Fire Festival|qq=}} * June 4–6 – Uozu Shrine Festival ** Locally known as "Shinmeihan's Festival," this event attracts numerous stalls along the central streets of Uozu. ** Historical children’s kabuki floats were once paraded, using traditional techniques and lacquerware craftsmanship. Some relics are preserved in the {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu History and Folklore Museum|1=Uozu Museum of History and Folklore|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674043}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Matsuri float|Matsuri float]] * {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu History and Folklore Museum|1=Uozu Museum of History and Folklore|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674043}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Atago Faith}} {{translated page|ja|魚津神社|version=103419258|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] }} [[ja:魚津神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Uozu, Toyama]] [[Category:History of Uozu, Toyama]] {{wikidata link|Q11674049}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11674049}}

Some relics are preserved in the {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu History and Folklore Museum|1=Uozu Museum of History and Folklore|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674043}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Matsuri float|Matsuri float]] * {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu History and Folklore Museum|1=Uozu Museum of History and Folklore|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674043}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Atago Faith}} {{translated page|ja|魚津神社|version=103419258|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] }} [[ja:魚津神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Uozu, Toyama]] [[Category:History of Uozu, Toyama]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11674049}}

[[ja:魚津神社]] [[ja:魚津神社]] [[ja:魚津神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11674049]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Uozu Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= File:Uozu-Jinja006.jpg |location= [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]] [[:en:Uozu, Toyama|Uozu]] Chūō-dōri 1-3-28『富山県神社誌』（1983rd year 11月10日、富山県神社庁発行）346ページ。 |coordinates= {{Coord|36.818222|N|137.397639|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Main deity= |Shrine Rank= |Established= |Main hall= |Other names= |festival= June 4 |Rituals= Uozu Jinja Festival (June 4–6)
Atago Fire Festival (January 26) |prefecture= {{ill|Uozu|WD=Q819613}} }} '''Uozu Shrine''' (魚津神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Uozu, Toyama|Uozu]], [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]]. == Deities == * Main deity: [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], originally enshrined in the Shinmei Shrine. * Enshrined deities: ** {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ|1=Takeminakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9150391}}, originally enshrined in the Shinmei Shrine. ** [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukano Mitama no Mikoto]], originally enshrined in the Inari Shrine. ** {{ill|Hoori|lt=Hoori|lt=Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|ja|彦火火出見命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}}, originally enshrined in the Hi-no-Miya Shrine. ** {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, originally enshrined in the Kizuki Shrine. ** [[:en:Kamuo Ichihime|Ōichihime no Mikoto]], originally enshrined in the Ichihime Shrine. ** {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速須佐之男命|Susanoo-no-Mikoto=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Takehayasusanoo-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, originally a guardian deity of Uozu Castle. ** {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売命|lt=Kushinadahime no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ}}, originally a guardian deity of Uozu Castle. ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, enshrined by Maeda Noritsuna of the Kaga Domain. == History == Originally named Shinmei Shrine, the site became Uozu Shrine in [[:en:1956|1956]] following the integration of five shrines located in areas affected by the {{ill|Uozu Great Fire|ja|魚津大火|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu Great Fire|1=Great Fire of Uozu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674015}}. The new shrine building was completed on {{ill|June 1, 1965|ja|昭和40th year 6月1日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 1, 1965|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.『北日本新聞』1965th year 5月21日付朝刊12面（富山新川版）『来月1日から遷座祭 魚津神社が完成』より。『富山県神社誌』（1983rd year 11月10日、富山県神社庁発行）346頁。 In [[:en:1965|1965]], the Atago Shrine, formerly located on the grounds of Uozu Municipal Ōmachi Elementary School, was relocated to the north of the main shrine. In [[:en:1968|1968]], a large torii gate (8.5 meters tall) was erected through a donation.『富山新聞』1968th year 9月18日付朝刊10面『魚津神社に大鳥居』より。 In [[:en:1970|1970]], a sumo ring was added to the grounds.『北日本新聞』1970th year 8月1日付朝刊（富山新川版）12面『魚津相撲場が完成』より。 == Shrine and Grounds == * Construction began: September 30, 1963 (groundbreaking ceremony held on September 24)『北日本新聞』1963rd year 9月20日付朝刊7面『二千三百万円で新築 7th year ぶりに解決の魚津神社』より。 * Completed: May 30, 1965 * Architect: Matsumoto Taisaku * Builder: Asano Nobuharu * Shrine grounds: 1,238 tsubo == Integrated Shrines == [[File:Atago-sha (Uozu)009.jpg|thumb|Atago Shrine]] * Shinmei Shrine (Shinmei-chō) – Established on March 17, 702 (Daibō 2). * Hi-no-Miya Shrine (Shimomuragi-chō) – Rebuilt during the Genki era. * Inari Shrine (Kanayachō) – Built during the Bunsei era. * Ichihime Shrine (Shinkanayachō) – Relics moved to Uozu Shrine in 1979. * Kizuki Shrine (Kōnoura-chō) – Established during the Bunsei era by the Uozu magistrate. == Annual Events == * January 26 – {{ill|Atago Fire Festival|ja|愛宕社の火祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Fire Festival|qq=}} * June 4–6 – Uozu Shrine Festival ** Locally known as "Shinmeihan's Festival," this event attracts numerous stalls along the central streets of Uozu. ** Historical children’s kabuki floats were once paraded, using traditional techniques and lacquerware craftsmanship. Some relics are preserved in the {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu History and Folklore Museum|1=Uozu Museum of History and Folklore|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674043}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Matsuri float|Matsuri float]] * {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu History and Folklore Museum|1=Uozu Museum of History and Folklore|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674043}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Atago Faith}} {{translated page|ja|魚津神社|version=103419258|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:魚津神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11674049}} [[Category:Buildings in Uozu, Toyama]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:History of Uozu, Toyama]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Some relics are preserved in the {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu History and Folklore Museum|1=Uozu Museum of History and Folklore|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674043}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Matsuri float|Matsuri float]] * {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu History and Folklore Museum|1=Uozu Museum of History and Folklore|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674043}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Atago Faith}} {{translated page|ja|魚津神社|version=103419258|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:魚津神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11674049}} [[Category:Buildings in Uozu, Toyama]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:History of Uozu, Toyama]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[ja:魚津神社]] [[ja:魚津神社]] [[ja:魚津神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11674049]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Uozu Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= File:Uozu-Jinja006.jpg |location= [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]] [[:en:Uozu, Toyama|Uozu]] Chūō-dōri 1-3-28『富山県神社誌』（1983rd year 11月10日、富山県神社庁発行）346ページ。 |coordinates= {{Coord|36.818222|N|137.397639|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Main deity= |Shrine Rank= |Established= |Main hall= |Other names= |festival= June 4 |Rituals= Uozu Jinja Festival (June 4–6)
Atago Fire Festival (January 26) |prefecture= {{ill|Uozu|WD=Q819613}} }} '''Uozu Shrine''' (魚津神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Uozu, Toyama|Uozu]], [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]]. == Deities == * Main deity: [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], originally enshrined in the Shinmei Shrine. * Enshrined deities: ** {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ|1=Takeminakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9150391}}, originally enshrined in the Shinmei Shrine. ** [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukano Mitama no Mikoto]], originally enshrined in the Inari Shrine. ** {{ill|Hoori|lt=Hoori|lt=Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|ja|彦火火出見命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}}, originally enshrined in the Hi-no-Miya Shrine. ** {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, originally enshrined in the Kizuki Shrine. ** [[:en:Kamuo Ichihime|Ōichihime no Mikoto]], originally enshrined in the Ichihime Shrine. ** {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速須佐之男命|Susanoo-no-Mikoto=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Takehayasusanoo-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, originally a guardian deity of Uozu Castle. ** {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売命|lt=Kushinadahime no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ}}, originally a guardian deity of Uozu Castle. ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, enshrined by Maeda Noritsuna of the Kaga Domain. == History == Originally named Shinmei Shrine, the site became Uozu Shrine in [[:en:1956|1956]] following the integration of five shrines located in areas affected by the {{ill|Uozu Great Fire|ja|魚津大火|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu Great Fire|1=Great Fire of Uozu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674015}}. The new shrine building was completed on {{ill|June 1, 1965|ja|昭和40th year 6月1日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 1, 1965|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.『北日本新聞』1965th year 5月21日付朝刊12面（富山新川版）『来月1日から遷座祭 魚津神社が完成』より。『富山県神社誌』（1983rd year 11月10日、富山県神社庁発行）346頁。 In [[:en:1965|1965]], the Atago Shrine, formerly located on the grounds of Uozu Municipal Ōmachi Elementary School, was relocated to the north of the main shrine. In [[:en:1968|1968]], a large torii gate (8.5 meters tall) was erected through a donation.『富山新聞』1968th year 9月18日付朝刊10面『魚津神社に大鳥居』より。 In [[:en:1970|1970]], a sumo ring was added to the grounds.『北日本新聞』1970th year 8月1日付朝刊（富山新川版）12面『魚津相撲場が完成』より。 == Shrine and Grounds == * Construction began: September 30, 1963 (groundbreaking ceremony held on September 24)『北日本新聞』1963rd year 9月20日付朝刊7面『二千三百万円で新築 7th year ぶりに解決の魚津神社』より。 * Completed: May 30, 1965 * Architect: Matsumoto Taisaku * Builder: Asano Nobuharu * Shrine grounds: 1,238 tsubo == Integrated Shrines == [[File:Atago-sha (Uozu)009.jpg|thumb|Atago Shrine]] * Shinmei Shrine (Shinmei-chō) – Established on March 17, 702 (Daibō 2). * Hi-no-Miya Shrine (Shimomuragi-chō) – Rebuilt during the Genki era. * Inari Shrine (Kanayachō) – Built during the Bunsei era. * Ichihime Shrine (Shinkanayachō) – Relics moved to Uozu Shrine in 1979. * Kizuki Shrine (Kōnoura-chō) – Established during the Bunsei era by the Uozu magistrate. == Annual Events == * January 26 – {{ill|Atago Fire Festival|ja|愛宕社の火祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Fire Festival|qq=}} * June 4–6 – Uozu Shrine Festival ** Locally known as "Shinmeihan's Festival," this event attracts numerous stalls along the central streets of Uozu. ** Historical children’s kabuki floats were once paraded, using traditional techniques and lacquerware craftsmanship. Some relics are preserved in the {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu History and Folklore Museum|1=Uozu Museum of History and Folklore|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674043}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Matsuri float|Matsuri float]] * {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu History and Folklore Museum|1=Uozu Museum of History and Folklore|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674043}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Atago Faith}} {{translated page|ja|魚津神社|version=103419258|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:魚津神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11674049}} [[Category:Buildings in Uozu, Toyama]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:History of Uozu, Toyama]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Some relics are preserved in the {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu History and Folklore Museum|1=Uozu Museum of History and Folklore|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674043}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Matsuri float|Matsuri float]] * {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu History and Folklore Museum|1=Uozu Museum of History and Folklore|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674043}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Atago Faith}} {{translated page|ja|魚津神社|version=103419258|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:魚津神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11674049}} [[Category:Buildings in Uozu, Toyama]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:History of Uozu, Toyama]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[ja:魚津神社]] [[ja:魚津神社]] [[ja:魚津神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11674049]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Uozu Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= File:Uozu-Jinja006.jpg |location= {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|en|Toyama Prefecture|WD=Q132929|lt=Toyama Prefecture}} {{ill|Uozu, Toyama|en|Uozu, Toyama|WD=Q819613|lt=Uozu}} Chūō-dōri 1-3-28『富山県神社誌』（1983rd year 11月10日、富山県神社庁発行）346ページ。 |coordinates= {{Coord|36.818222|N|137.397639|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Main deity= |Shrine Rank= |Established= |Main hall= |Other names= |festival= June 4 |Rituals= Uozu Jinja Festival (June 4–6)
Atago Fire Festival (January 26) |prefecture= {{ill|Uozu|WD=Q819613}} }} '''Uozu Shrine''' (魚津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Uozu, Toyama|en|Uozu, Toyama|WD=Q819613|lt=Uozu}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|en|Toyama Prefecture|WD=Q132929|lt=Toyama Prefecture}}. == Deities == * Main deity: {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, originally enshrined in the Shinmei Shrine. * Enshrined deities: ** {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ|1=Takeminakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9150391}}, originally enshrined in the Shinmei Shrine. ** {{ill|Ukanomitama|en|Ukanomitama|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukano Mitama no Mikoto}}, originally enshrined in the Inari Shrine. ** {{ill|Hoori|lt=Hoori|lt=Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|ja|彦火火出見命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}}, originally enshrined in the Hi-no-Miya Shrine. ** {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, originally enshrined in the Kizuki Shrine. ** {{ill|Kamuo Ichihime|en|Kamuo Ichihime|WD=Q11588867|lt=Ōichihime no Mikoto}}, originally enshrined in the Ichihime Shrine. ** {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速須佐之男命|Susanoo-no-Mikoto=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Takehayasusanoo-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, originally a guardian deity of Uozu Castle. ** {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売命|lt=Kushinadahime no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ}}, originally a guardian deity of Uozu Castle. ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, enshrined by Maeda Noritsuna of the Kaga Domain. == History == Originally named Shinmei Shrine, the site became Uozu Shrine in {{ill|1956|en|1956|WD=Q5221|lt=1956}} following the integration of five shrines located in areas affected by the {{ill|Uozu Great Fire|ja|魚津大火|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu Great Fire|1=Great Fire of Uozu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674015}}. The new shrine building was completed on {{ill|June 1, 1965|ja|昭和40th year 6月1日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 1, 1965|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.『北日本新聞』1965th year 5月21日付朝刊12面（富山新川版）『来月1日から遷座祭 魚津神社が完成』より。『富山県神社誌』（1983rd year 11月10日、富山県神社庁発行）346頁。 In {{ill|1965|en|1965|WD=Q2650|lt=1965}}, the Atago Shrine, formerly located on the grounds of Uozu Municipal Ōmachi Elementary School, was relocated to the north of the main shrine. In {{ill|1968|en|1968|WD=Q2644|lt=1968}}, a large torii gate (8.5 meters tall) was erected through a donation.『富山新聞』1968th year 9月18日付朝刊10面『魚津神社に大鳥居』より。 In {{ill|1970|en|1970|WD=Q2474|lt=1970}}, a sumo ring was added to the grounds.『北日本新聞』1970th year 8月1日付朝刊（富山新川版）12面『魚津相撲場が完成』より。 == Shrine and Grounds == * Construction began: September 30, 1963 (groundbreaking ceremony held on September 24)『北日本新聞』1963rd year 9月20日付朝刊7面『二千三百万円で新築 7th year ぶりに解決の魚津神社』より。 * Completed: May 30, 1965 * Architect: Matsumoto Taisaku * Builder: Asano Nobuharu * Shrine grounds: 1,238 tsubo == Integrated Shrines == [[File:Atago-sha (Uozu)009.jpg|thumb|Atago Shrine]] * Shinmei Shrine (Shinmei-chō) – Established on March 17, 702 (Daibō 2). * Hi-no-Miya Shrine (Shimomuragi-chō) – Rebuilt during the Genki era. * Inari Shrine (Kanayachō) – Built during the Bunsei era. * Ichihime Shrine (Shinkanayachō) – Relics moved to Uozu Shrine in 1979. * Kizuki Shrine (Kōnoura-chō) – Established during the Bunsei era by the Uozu magistrate. == Annual Events == * January 26 – {{ill|Atago Fire Festival|ja|愛宕社の火祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Fire Festival|qq=}} * June 4–6 – Uozu Shrine Festival ** Locally known as "Shinmeihan's Festival," this event attracts numerous stalls along the central streets of Uozu. ** Historical children’s kabuki floats were once paraded, using traditional techniques and lacquerware craftsmanship. In {{ill|1968|en|1968|WD=Q2644|lt=1968}}, a large torii gate (8.5 meters tall) was erected through a donation.『富山新聞』1968th year 9月18日付朝刊10面『魚津神社に大鳥居』より。 In {{ill|1970|en|1970|WD=Q2474|lt=1970}}, a sumo ring was added to the grounds.『北日本新聞』1970th year 8月1日付朝刊（富山新川版）12面『魚津相撲場が完成』より。 == Shrine and Grounds == * Construction began: September 30, 1963 (groundbreaking ceremony held on September 24)『北日本新聞』1963rd year 9月20日付朝刊7面『二千三百万円で新築 7th year ぶりに解決の魚津神社』より。 * Completed: May 30, 1965 * Architect: Matsumoto Taisaku * Builder: Asano Nobuharu * Shrine grounds: 1,238 tsubo == Integrated Shrines == [[File:Atago-sha (Uozu)009.jpg|thumb|Atago Shrine]] * Shinmei Shrine (Shinmei-chō) – Established on March 17, 702 (Daibō 2). * Hi-no-Miya Shrine (Shimomuragi-chō) – Rebuilt during the Genki era. * Inari Shrine (Kanayachō) – Built during the Bunsei era. * Ichihime Shrine (Shinkanayachō) – Relics moved to Uozu Shrine in 1979. * Kizuki Shrine (Kōnoura-chō) – Established during the Bunsei era by the Uozu magistrate. == Annual Events == * January 26 – {{ill|Atago Fire Festival|ja|愛宕社の火祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Fire Festival|qq=}} * June 4–6 – Uozu Shrine Festival ** Locally known as "Shinmeihan's Festival," this event attracts numerous stalls along the central streets of Uozu. ** Historical children’s kabuki floats were once paraded, using traditional techniques and lacquerware craftsmanship. Some relics are preserved in the {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu History and Folklore Museum|1=Uozu Museum of History and Folklore|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674043}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Matsuri float|en|Matsuri float|WD=Q11470974|lt=Matsuri float}} * {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu History and Folklore Museum|1=Uozu Museum of History and Folklore|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674043}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Atago Faith}} {{translated page|ja|魚津神社|version=103419258|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:魚津神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11674049}} [[Category:Buildings in Uozu, Toyama]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:History of Uozu, Toyama]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Atago Fire Festival (January 26) |prefecture= {{ill|Uozu|WD=Q819613}} }} '''Uozu Shrine''' (魚津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Uozu, Toyama|en|Uozu, Toyama|WD=Q819613|lt=Uozu}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|en|Toyama Prefecture|WD=Q132929|lt=Toyama Prefecture}}. == Deities == * Main deity: {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, originally enshrined in the Shinmei Shrine. * Enshrined deities: ** {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ|1=Takeminakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9150391}}, originally enshrined in the Shinmei Shrine. ** {{ill|Ukanomitama|en|Ukanomitama|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukano Mitama no Mikoto}}, originally enshrined in the Inari Shrine. ** {{ill|Hoori|lt=Hoori|lt=Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|ja|彦火火出見命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}}, originally enshrined in the Hi-no-Miya Shrine. ** {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, originally enshrined in the Kizuki Shrine. ** {{ill|Kamuo Ichihime|en|Kamuo Ichihime|WD=Q11588867|lt=Ōichihime no Mikoto}}, originally enshrined in the Ichihime Shrine. ** {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速須佐之男命|Susanoo-no-Mikoto=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Takehayasusanoo-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, originally a guardian deity of Uozu Castle. ** {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売命|lt=Kushinadahime no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ}}, originally a guardian deity of Uozu Castle. ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, enshrined by Maeda Noritsuna of the Kaga Domain. == History == Originally named Shinmei Shrine, the site became Uozu Shrine in {{ill|1956|en|1956|WD=Q5221|lt=1956}} following the integration of five shrines located in areas affected by the {{ill|Uozu Great Fire|ja|魚津大火|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu Great Fire|1=Great Fire of Uozu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674015}}. The new shrine building was completed on {{ill|June 1, 1965|ja|昭和40th year 6月1日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 1, 1965|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.『北日本新聞』1965th year 5月21日付朝刊12面（富山新川版）『来月1日から遷座祭 魚津神社が完成』より。『富山県神社誌』（1983rd year 11月10日、富山県神社庁発行）346頁。 In {{ill|1965|en|1965|WD=Q2650|lt=1965}}, the Atago Shrine, formerly located on the grounds of Uozu Municipal Ōmachi Elementary School, was relocated to the north of the main shrine. In {{ill|1968|en|1968|WD=Q2644|lt=1968}}, a large torii gate (8.5 meters tall) was erected through a donation.『富山新聞』1968th year 9月18日付朝刊10面『魚津神社に大鳥居』より。 In {{ill|1970|en|1970|WD=Q2474|lt=1970}}, a sumo ring was added to the grounds.『北日本新聞』1970th year 8月1日付朝刊（富山新川版）12面『魚津相撲場が完成』より。 == Shrine and Grounds == * Construction began: September 30, 1963 (groundbreaking ceremony held on September 24)『北日本新聞』1963rd year 9月20日付朝刊7面『二千三百万円で新築 7th year ぶりに解決の魚津神社』より。 * Completed: May 30, 1965 * Architect: Matsumoto Taisaku * Builder: Asano Nobuharu * Shrine grounds: 1,238 tsubo == Integrated Shrines == [[File:Atago-sha (Uozu)009.jpg|thumb|Atago Shrine]] * Shinmei Shrine (Shinmei-chō) – Established on March 17, 702 (Daibō 2). * Hi-no-Miya Shrine (Shimomuragi-chō) – Rebuilt during the Genki era. * Inari Shrine (Kanayachō) – Built during the Bunsei era. * Ichihime Shrine (Shinkanayachō) – Relics moved to Uozu Shrine in 1979. * Kizuki Shrine (Kōnoura-chō) – Established during the Bunsei era by the Uozu magistrate. == Annual Events == * January 26 – {{ill|Atago Fire Festival|ja|愛宕社の火祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Fire Festival|qq=}} * June 4–6 – Uozu Shrine Festival ** Locally known as "Shinmeihan's Festival," this event attracts numerous stalls along the central streets of Uozu. ** Historical children’s kabuki floats were once paraded, using traditional techniques and lacquerware craftsmanship. In {{ill|1968|en|1968|WD=Q2644|lt=1968}}, a large torii gate (8.5 meters tall) was erected through a donation.『富山新聞』1968th year 9月18日付朝刊10面『魚津神社に大鳥居』より。 In {{ill|1970|en|1970|WD=Q2474|lt=1970}}, a sumo ring was added to the grounds.『北日本新聞』1970th year 8月1日付朝刊（富山新川版）12面『魚津相撲場が完成』より。 == Shrine and Grounds == * Construction began: September 30, 1963 (groundbreaking ceremony held on September 24)『北日本新聞』1963rd year 9月20日付朝刊7面『二千三百万円で新築 7th year ぶりに解決の魚津神社』より。 * Completed: May 30, 1965 * Architect: Matsumoto Taisaku * Builder: Asano Nobuharu * Shrine grounds: 1,238 tsubo == Integrated Shrines == [[File:Atago-sha (Uozu)009.jpg|thumb|Atago Shrine]] * Shinmei Shrine (Shinmei-chō) – Established on March 17, 702 (Daibō 2). * Hi-no-Miya Shrine (Shimomuragi-chō) – Rebuilt during the Genki era. * Inari Shrine (Kanayachō) – Built during the Bunsei era. * Ichihime Shrine (Shinkanayachō) – Relics moved to Uozu Shrine in 1979. * Kizuki Shrine (Kōnoura-chō) – Established during the Bunsei era by the Uozu magistrate. == Annual Events == * January 26 – {{ill|Atago Fire Festival|ja|愛宕社の火祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Fire Festival|qq=}} * June 4–6 – Uozu Shrine Festival ** Locally known as "Shinmeihan's Festival," this event attracts numerous stalls along the central streets of Uozu. ** Historical children’s kabuki floats were once paraded, using traditional techniques and lacquerware craftsmanship. Some relics are preserved in the {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu History and Folklore Museum|1=Uozu Museum of History and Folklore|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674043}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Matsuri float|en|Matsuri float|WD=Q11470974|lt=Matsuri float}} * {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu History and Folklore Museum|1=Uozu Museum of History and Folklore|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674043}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Atago Faith}} {{translated page|ja|魚津神社|version=103419258|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:魚津神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11674049}} [[Category:Buildings in Uozu, Toyama]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:History of Uozu, Toyama]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

[[ja:魚津神社]] [[ja:魚津神社]] [[ja:魚津神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Uozu Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= File:Uozu-Jinja006.jpg |location= {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|en|Toyama Prefecture|WD=Q132929|lt=Toyama Prefecture|1=Toyama Prefecture}} {{ill|Uozu, Toyama|en|Uozu, Toyama|WD=Q819613|lt=Uozu|1=Uozu, Toyama}} Chūō-dōri 1-3-28『富山県神社誌』（1983rd year 11月10日、富山県神社庁発行）346ページ。 |coordinates= {{Coord|36.818222|N|137.397639|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Main deity= |Shrine Rank= |Established= |Main hall= |Other names= |festival= June 4 |Rituals= Uozu Jinja Festival (June 4–6)
Atago Fire Festival (January 26) |prefecture= {{ill|Uozu|WD=Q819613|1=Uozu, Toyama}} }} '''Uozu Shrine''' (魚津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Uozu, Toyama|en|Uozu, Toyama|WD=Q819613|lt=Uozu|1=Uozu, Toyama}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|en|Toyama Prefecture|WD=Q132929|lt=Toyama Prefecture|1=Toyama Prefecture}}. == Deities == * Main deity: {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}, originally enshrined in the Shinmei Shrine. * Enshrined deities: ** {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ|1=Takeminakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9150391}}, originally enshrined in the Shinmei Shrine. ** {{ill|Ukanomitama|en|Ukanomitama|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukano Mitama no Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}}, originally enshrined in the Inari Shrine. ** {{ill|Hoori|lt=Hoori|lt=Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|ja|彦火火出見命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}}, originally enshrined in the Hi-no-Miya Shrine. ** {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, originally enshrined in the Kizuki Shrine. ** {{ill|Kamuo Ichihime|en|Kamuo Ichihime|WD=Q11588867|lt=Ōichihime no Mikoto|1=Kamuo Ichihime}}, originally enshrined in the Ichihime Shrine. ** {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速須佐之男命|Susanoo-no-Mikoto=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Takehayasusanoo-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, originally a guardian deity of Uozu Castle. ** {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売命|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ|1=Kushinadahime}}, originally a guardian deity of Uozu Castle. ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, enshrined by Maeda Noritsuna of the Kaga Domain. == History == Originally named Shinmei Shrine, the site became Uozu Shrine in {{ill|1956|en|1956|WD=Q5221|lt=1956|1=1956}} following the integration of five shrines located in areas affected by the {{ill|Uozu Great Fire|ja|魚津大火|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu Great Fire|1=Great Fire of Uozu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674015}}. The new shrine building was completed on {{ill|June 1, 1965|ja|昭和40th year 6月1日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 1, 1965|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.『北日本新聞』1965th year 5月21日付朝刊12面（富山新川版）『来月1日から遷座祭 魚津神社が完成』より。『富山県神社誌』（1983rd year 11月10日、富山県神社庁発行）346頁。 In {{ill|1965|en|1965|WD=Q2650|lt=1965|1=1965}}, the Atago Shrine, formerly located on the grounds of Uozu Municipal Ōmachi Elementary School, was relocated to the north of the main shrine. In {{ill|1968|en|1968|WD=Q2644|lt=1968|1=1968}}, a large torii gate (8.5 meters tall) was erected through a donation.『富山新聞』1968th year 9月18日付朝刊10面『魚津神社に大鳥居』より。 In {{ill|1970|en|1970|WD=Q2474|lt=1970|1=1970}}, a sumo ring was added to the grounds.『北日本新聞』1970th year 8月1日付朝刊（富山新川版）12面『魚津相撲場が完成』より。 == Shrine and Grounds == * Construction began: September 30, 1963 (groundbreaking ceremony held on September 24)『北日本新聞』1963rd year 9月20日付朝刊7面『二千三百万円で新築 7th year ぶりに解決の魚津神社』より。 * Completed: May 30, 1965 * Architect: Matsumoto Taisaku * Builder: Asano Nobuharu * Shrine grounds: 1,238 tsubo == Integrated Shrines == [[File:Atago-sha (Uozu)009.jpg|thumb|Atago Shrine]] * Shinmei Shrine (Shinmei-chō) – Established on March 17, 702 (Daibō 2). * Hi-no-Miya Shrine (Shimomuragi-chō) – Rebuilt during the Genki era. * Inari Shrine (Kanayachō) – Built during the Bunsei era. * Ichihime Shrine (Shinkanayachō) – Relics moved to Uozu Shrine in 1979. * Kizuki Shrine (Kōnoura-chō) – Established during the Bunsei era by the Uozu magistrate. == Annual Events == * January 26 – {{ill|Atago Fire Festival|ja|愛宕社の火祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Fire Festival|qq=}} * June 4–6 – Uozu Shrine Festival ** Locally known as "Shinmeihan's Festival," this event attracts numerous stalls along the central streets of Uozu. ** Historical children’s kabuki floats were once paraded, using traditional techniques and lacquerware craftsmanship. Some relics are preserved in the {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu History and Folklore Museum|1=Uozu Museum of History and Folklore|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674043}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Matsuri float|en|Matsuri float|WD=Q11470974|lt=Matsuri float|1=Matsuri float}} * {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu History and Folklore Museum|1=Uozu Museum of History and Folklore|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674043}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Atago Faith}} {{translated page|ja|魚津神社|version=103419258|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:魚津神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11674049}} [[Category:Buildings in Uozu, Toyama]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:History of Uozu, Toyama]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

by the {{ill|Uozu Great Fire|ja|魚津大火|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu Great Fire|1=Great Fire of Uozu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674015}}. The new shrine building was completed on {{ill|June 1, 1965|ja|昭和40th year 6月1日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 1, 1965|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.『北日本新聞』1965th year 5月21日付朝刊12面（富山新川版）『来月1日から遷座祭 魚津神社が完成』より。『富山県神社誌』（1983rd year 11月10日、富山県神社庁発行）346頁。 In {{ill|1965|en|1965|WD=Q2650|lt=1965|1=1965}}, the Atago Shrine, formerly located on the grounds of Uozu Municipal Ōmachi Elementary School, was relocated to the north of the main shrine. In {{ill|1968|en|1968|WD=Q2644|lt=1968|1=1968}}, a large torii gate (8.5 meters tall) was erected through a donation.『富山新聞』1968th year 9月18日付朝刊10面『魚津神社に大鳥居』より。 In {{ill|1970|en|1970|WD=Q2474|lt=1970|1=1970}}, a sumo ring was added to the grounds.『北日本新聞』1970th year 8月1日付朝刊（富山新川版）12面『魚津相撲場が完成』より。 == Shrine and Grounds == * Construction began: September 30, 1963 (groundbreaking ceremony held on September 24)『北日本新聞』1963rd year 9月20日付朝刊7面『二千三百万円で新築 7th year ぶりに解決の魚津神社』より。 * Completed: May 30, 1965 * Architect: Matsumoto Taisaku * Builder: Asano Nobuharu * Shrine grounds: 1,238 tsubo == Integrated Shrines == [[File:Atago-sha (Uozu)009.jpg|thumb|Atago Shrine]] * Shinmei Shrine (Shinmei-chō) – Established on March 17, 702 (Daibō 2). * Hi-no-Miya Shrine (Shimomuragi-chō) – Rebuilt during the Genki era. * Inari Shrine (Kanayachō) – Built during the Bunsei era. * Ichihime Shrine (Shinkanayachō) – Relics moved to Uozu Shrine in 1979. * Kizuki Shrine (Kōnoura-chō) – Established during the Bunsei era by the Uozu magistrate. == Annual Events == * January 26 – {{ill|Atago Fire Festival|ja|愛宕社の火祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Fire Festival|qq=}} * June 4–6 – Uozu Shrine Festival ** Locally known as "Shinmeihan's Festival," this event attracts numerous stalls along the central streets of Uozu. ** Historical children’s kabuki floats were once paraded, using traditional techniques and lacquerware craftsmanship. Some relics are preserved in the {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu History and Folklore Museum|1=Uozu Museum of History and Folklore|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674043}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Matsuri float|en|Matsuri float|WD=Q11470974|lt=Matsuri float|1=Matsuri float}} * {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu History and Folklore Museum|1=Uozu Museum of History and Folklore|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674043}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Atago Faith}} {{translated page|ja|魚津神社|version=103419258|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:魚津神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11674049}} [[Category:Buildings in Uozu, Toyama]] [[Category:History of Uozu, Toyama]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[ja:魚津神社]] [[ja:魚津神社]] [[ja:魚津神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Uozu Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= File:Uozu-Jinja006.jpg |location= {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|en|Toyama Prefecture|WD=Q132929|lt=Toyama Prefecture|1=Toyama Prefecture}} {{ill|Uozu, Toyama|en|Uozu, Toyama|WD=Q819613|lt=Uozu|1=Uozu, Toyama}} Chūō-dōri 1-3-28『富山県神社誌』（1983rd year 11月10日、富山県神社庁発行）346ページ。 |coordinates= {{Coord|36.818222|N|137.397639|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Main deity= |Shrine Rank= |Established= |Main hall= |Other names= |festival= June 4 |Rituals= Uozu Jinja Festival (June 4–6)
Atago Fire Festival (January 26) |prefecture= {{ill|Uozu|WD=Q819613|1=Uozu, Toyama}} }} '''Uozu Shrine''' (魚津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Uozu, Toyama|en|Uozu, Toyama|WD=Q819613|lt=Uozu|1=Uozu, Toyama}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|en|Toyama Prefecture|WD=Q132929|lt=Toyama Prefecture|1=Toyama Prefecture}}. == Deities == * Main deity: {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}, originally enshrined in the Shinmei Shrine. * Enshrined deities: ** {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ|1=Takeminakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9150391}}, originally enshrined in the Shinmei Shrine. ** {{ill|Ukanomitama|en|Ukanomitama|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukano Mitama no Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}}, originally enshrined in the Inari Shrine. ** {{ill|Hoori|lt=Hoori|lt=Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|ja|彦火火出見命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}}, originally enshrined in the Hi-no-Miya Shrine. ** {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, originally enshrined in the Kizuki Shrine. ** {{ill|Kamuo Ichihime|en|Kamuo Ichihime|WD=Q11588867|lt=Ōichihime no Mikoto|1=Kamuo Ichihime}}, originally enshrined in the Ichihime Shrine. ** {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速須佐之男命|Susanoo-no-Mikoto=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Takehayasusanoo-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, originally a guardian deity of Uozu Castle. ** {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売命|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ|1=Kushinadahime}}, originally a guardian deity of Uozu Castle. ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, enshrined by Maeda Noritsuna of the Kaga Domain. == History == Originally named Shinmei Shrine, the site became Uozu Shrine in {{ill|1956|en|1956|WD=Q5221|lt=1956|1=1956}} following the integration of five shrines located in areas affected by the {{ill|Uozu Great Fire|ja|魚津大火|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu Great Fire|1=Great Fire of Uozu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674015}}. The new shrine building was completed on {{ill|June 1, 1965|ja|昭和40th year 6月1日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 1, 1965|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.『北日本新聞』1965th year 5月21日付朝刊12面（富山新川版）『来月1日から遷座祭 魚津神社が完成』より。『富山県神社誌』（1983rd year 11月10日、富山県神社庁発行）346頁。 In {{ill|1965|en|1965|WD=Q2650|lt=1965|1=1965}}, the Atago Shrine, formerly located on the grounds of Uozu Municipal Ōmachi Elementary School, was relocated to the north of the main shrine. In {{ill|1968|en|1968|WD=Q2644|lt=1968|1=1968}}, a large torii gate (8.5 meters tall) was erected through a donation.『富山新聞』1968th year 9月18日付朝刊10面『魚津神社に大鳥居』より。 In {{ill|1970|en|1970|WD=Q2474|lt=1970|1=1970}}, a sumo ring was added to the grounds.『北日本新聞』1970th year 8月1日付朝刊（富山新川版）12面『魚津相撲場が完成』より。 == Shrine and Grounds == * Construction began: September 30, 1963 (groundbreaking ceremony held on September 24)『北日本新聞』1963rd year 9月20日付朝刊7面『二千三百万円で新築 7th year ぶりに解決の魚津神社』より。 * Completed: May 30, 1965 * Architect: Matsumoto Taisaku * Builder: Asano Nobuharu * Shrine grounds: 1,238 tsubo == Integrated Shrines == [[File:Atago-sha (Uozu)009.jpg|thumb|Atago Shrine]] * Shinmei Shrine (Shinmei-chō) – Established on March 17, 702 (Daibō 2). * Hi-no-Miya Shrine (Shimomuragi-chō) – Rebuilt during the Genki era. * Inari Shrine (Kanayachō) – Built during the Bunsei era. * Ichihime Shrine (Shinkanayachō) – Relics moved to Uozu Shrine in 1979. * Kizuki Shrine (Kōnoura-chō) – Established during the Bunsei era by the Uozu magistrate. == Annual Events == * January 26 – {{ill|Atago Fire Festival|ja|愛宕社の火祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Fire Festival|qq=}} * June 4–6 – Uozu Shrine Festival ** Locally known as "Shinmeihan's Festival," this event attracts numerous stalls along the central streets of Uozu. ** Historical children’s kabuki floats were once paraded, using traditional techniques and lacquerware craftsmanship. Some relics are preserved in the {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu History and Folklore Museum|1=Uozu Museum of History and Folklore|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674043}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Matsuri float|en|Matsuri float|WD=Q11470974|lt=Matsuri float|1=Matsuri float}} * {{ill|Uozu History and Folklore Museum|ja|魚津歴史民俗博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu History and Folklore Museum|1=Uozu Museum of History and Folklore|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674043}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Atago Faith}} {{translated page|ja|魚津神社|version=103419258|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:魚津神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11674049}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Uozu, Toyama]] [[Category:History of Uozu, Toyama]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
